## TOPIC 02001: DAQmx Setup 9638 AO Calibration VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-9638-ao-calibration-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-9638-ao-calibration-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Writes the specified binary value to the DAC on the specified physical channel. This module requires a sweep of DAC values from the DAQmx Get 9638:AO Calibration Adjustment Points VI. icon Inputs/Outputs cu32.png calhandle in calhandle in is a reference to the calibration session that you created us

### DAQmx Setup 9638 AO Calibration VI

Writes the specified binary value to the DAC on the specified physical channel. This module requires a sweep of DAC values from the DAQmx Get 9638:AO Calibration Adjustment Points VI.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-setup-9638-ao-calibration-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| calhandle in — calhandle in is a reference to the calibration session that you created using the DAQmx Initialize External Calibration VI. physical channels — physical channels specifies the name of the physical channel to use to create the virtual channel. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a physical channel to this input. You cannot specify more than one physical channel. DAC value — DAC value is the binary number to write to the DAC. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. calhandle out — calhandle out is a reference to the calibration session. Wire this output to other external calibration VIs. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

## TOPIC 02002: DAQmx Setup C Series Calibration VI

Refer to the calibration procedure for your device for detailed calibration instructions. After you commit the calibration, you cannot undo it. If any calibrati

### DAQmx Setup C Series Calibration VI

Sets up calibration for C Series devices.
 The instances of this [polymorphic VI](/csh?context=lvcore_lvhelp_using_polymorphic_vis) correspond to the model of the device you want to calibrate.
 Refer to the [calibration procedure for your device](http://www.ni.com/cgi-bin/redirect.cgi?dest=infcoprod&src=help&openagent&code=rdcalp) for detailed calibration instructions.

Note

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-setup-c-series-calibration-vi.png']

- [DAQmx Setup 9218 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-9218-calibration-vi.html) Sets up external calibration for an NI 9218 device.
- [DAQmx Setup 9219 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-9219-calibration-vi.html) Sets up external calibration for an NI 9219 device.
- [DAQmx Setup 9242 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-9242-calibration-vi.html) Reads the specified value from the ADC on the specified physical channel. This module requires a sweep of adjustment points obtained from the DAQmx Get 9242 Calibration Adjustment Points VI.
- [DAQmx Setup 9244 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-9244-calibration-vi.html) Reads the specified value from the ADC on the specified physical channel. This module requires a sweep of adjustment points obtained from the DAQmx Get 9244 Calibration Adjustment Points VI.
- [DAQmx Setup 9260 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-9260-calibration-vi.html) Writes the specified binary value to the DAC on the specified physical channel. This module requires a sweep of DAC values obtained from the DAQmx Get 9260 Calibration Adjustment Points VI.
- [DAQmx Setup 9262 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-9262-calibration-vi.html) Writes the specified binary value to the DAC on the specified physical channel. This module requires a sweep of DAC values obtained from the DAQmx Get 9262 Calibration Adjustment Points VI.
- [DAQmx Setup 9263 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-9263-calibration-vi.html) Writes the specified binary value to the DAC on the specified physical channel. This module requires a sweep of DAC values obtained from the DAQmx Get 9263 Calibration Adjustment Points VI.
- [DAQmx Setup 9264 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-9264-calibration-vi.html) Writes the specified binary value to the DAC on the specified physical channel. This module requires a sweep of DAC values obtained from the DAQmx Get 9264 Calibration Adjustment Points VI.
- [DAQmx Setup 9265 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-9265-calibration-vi.html) Writes the specified binary value to the DAC on the specified physical channel. This module requires a sweep of DAC values obtained from the DAQmx Get 9265 Calibration Adjustment Points VI.
- [DAQmx Setup 9266 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-9266-calibration-vi.html) Writes the specified binary value to the DAC on the specified physical channel. This module requires a sweep of adjustment points obtained from the DAQmx Get 9266 Calibration Adjustment Points VI.
- [DAQmx Setup 9269 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-9269-calibration-vi.html) Writes the specified binary value to the DAC on the specified physical channel. This module requires a sweep of DAC values obtained from the DAQmx Get 9269 Calibration Adjustment Points VI.
- [DAQmx Setup 9628 AO Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-9628-ao-calibration-vi.html) Writes the specified binary value to the DAC on the specified physical channel. This module requires a sweep of DAC values from the DAQmx Get 9628:AO Calibration Adjustment Points VI.
- [DAQmx Setup 9629 AO Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-9629-ao-calibration-vi.html) Writes the specified binary value to the DAC on the specified physical channel. This module requires a sweep of DAC values from the DAQmx Get 9629 AO Calibration Adjustment Points VI.
- [DAQmx Setup 9638 AO Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-9638-ao-calibration-vi.html) Writes the specified binary value to the DAC on the specified physical channel. This module requires a sweep of DAC values from the DAQmx Get 9638:AO Calibration Adjustment Points VI.

## TOPIC 02003: DAQmx Setup DSA AO Timebase Calibration VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-dsa-ao-timebase-calibration-vi.html`

- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Generates a sine wave of a fixed frequency from the DSA device. You must measure the frequency of the generated sine wave and pass that value into the DAQmx Adjust DSA AO Timebase Calibration VI. icon Inputs/Outputs cu32.png calhandle in calhandle in is a reference to the calibration session that yo

### DAQmx Setup DSA AO Timebase Calibration VI

Generates a sine wave of a fixed frequency from the DSA device. You must measure the frequency of the generated sine wave and pass that value into the DAQmx Adjust DSA AO Timebase Calibration VI.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-setup-dsa-ao-timebase-calibration-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| calhandle in — calhandle in is a reference to the calibration session that you created using the DAQmx Initialize External Calibration VI. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. calhandle out — calhandle out is a reference to the calibration session. Wire this output to other external calibration VIs. expectedFrequency — expectedFrequency indicates the expected frequency of the sine wave of a calibrated device. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

DAQmx Setup DSA Calibration Polymorphic VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-dsa-calibration-polymorphic-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02004: DAQmx Setup DSA Calibration Polymorphic VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-dsa-calibration-polymorphic-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-dsa-calibration-polymorphic-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Sets up calibration for DSA devices. The instances of this polymorphic VI specify whether to use a specified gain and coupling configuration. icon

### DAQmx Setup DSA Calibration Polymorphic VI

Sets up calibration for DSA devices.

The instances of this [polymorphic VI](/csh?context=lvcore_lvhelp_using_polymorphic_vis) specify whether to use a specified gain and coupling configuration.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-setup-dsa-calibration-polymorphic-vi.png']

- [DAQmx Setup 4480 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-4480-calibration-vi.html) Initializes a PXIe-4480 device for calibrating the specified channel type. Refer to the calibration procedure for your device for specific calibration instructions.
- [DAQmx Setup DSA AO Timebase Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-dsa-ao-timebase-calibration-vi.html) Generates a sine wave of a fixed frequency from the DSA device. You must measure the frequency of the generated sine wave and pass that value into the DAQmx Adjust DSA AO Timebase Calibration VI.
- [DAQmx Setup 4463 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-4463-calibration-vi.html) Generates a specified voltage from the device. You must measure the voltage and pass that value into the DAQmx Adjust 4463 Calibration VI.

Parent topic:

DAQmx DSA Calibration

Refer to the calibration procedure for your device for detailed calibration instructions. After you commit the calibration, you cannot undo it. If any calibrati

### DAQmx Setup FieldDAQ Calibration VI

Sets up calibration for FieldDAQ devices.
 The instances of this [polymorphic VI](/csh?context=lvcore_lvhelp_using_polymorphic_vis) correspond to the model of the device you want to calibrate.
 Refer to the [calibration procedure for your device](http://www.ni.com/cgi-bin/redirect.cgi?dest=infcoprod&src=help&openagent&code=rdcalp) for detailed calibration instructions.

Note

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-setup-fielddaq-calibration-vi.png']

- [DAQmx Setup 11605 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-11605-calibration-vi.html) Initializes the NI FD-11605 device with the specified range. Refer to the calibration procedure for your device for specific calibration instructions.
- [DAQmx Setup 11634 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-11634-calibration-vi.html) Initializes the NI FD-11634 device with the specified range. Refer to the calibration procedure for your device for specific calibration instructions.
- [DAQmx Setup 11637 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-11637-calibration-vi.html) Sets up external calibration of a single channel for a FD-11637 device.

- source_description: Sets up calibration for SC Express devices. The instances of this polymorphic VI correspond to the model of the device you want to calibrate. Refer to the calibration procedure for your device for detailed calibration instructions. After you commit the calibration, you cannot undo it. If any calibra

### DAQmx Setup SC Express Calibration VI

Sets up calibration for SC Express devices.
 The instances of this [polymorphic VI](/csh?context=lvcore_lvhelp_using_polymorphic_vis) correspond to the model of the device you want to calibrate.
 Refer to the [calibration procedure for your device](http://www.ni.com/cgi-bin/redirect.cgi?dest=infcoprod&src=help&openagent&code=rdcalp) for detailed calibration instructions.

Note

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-setup-sc-express-calibration-vi.png']

- [DAQmx Setup 4302 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-4302-calibration-vi.html) Initializes an NI PXIe-4302 device with the specified mode, range, and excitation for calibration. Refer to the calibration procedure for your device for specific calibration instructions.
- [DAQmx Setup 4303 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-4303-calibration-vi.html) Initializes an NI PXIe-4303 device with the specified mode, range, and excitation for calibration. Refer to the calibration procedure for your device for specific calibration instructions.
- [DAQmx Setup 4304 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-4304-calibration-vi.html) Initializes an NI PXIe-4304 device with the specified mode, range, and excitation for calibration. Refer to the calibration procedure for your device for specific calibration instructions.
- [DAQmx Setup 4305 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-4305-calibration-vi.html) Initializes an NI PXIe-4305 device with the specified mode, range, and excitation for calibration. Refer to the calibration procedure for your device for specific calibration instructions.
- [DAQmx Setup 4322 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-4322-calibration-vi.html) Writes the specified value to the DAC on the specified physical channel(s) for an NI PXIe-4322. This device requires a sweep of values obtained from the calibration procedure.
- [DAQmx Setup 433x Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-433x-calibration-vi.html) Initializes an NI PXIe-4330 or NI PXIe-4331 device with the specified excitation voltage value for calibration.
- [DAQmx Setup 4339 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-4339-calibration-vi.html) Initializes an NI PXIe-4339 device with the specified mode, range, and excitation for calibration. Refer to the calibration procedure for your device for specific calibration instructions.

## TOPIC 02007: DAQmx Setup SCXI 1102 Calibration VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-scxi-1102-calibration-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-scxi-1102-calibration-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Sets an SCXI-1102 module to the specified gain value for calibration. Measure calibration input and output points by supplying reference signals to the specified physical channel and measuring the outputs. Use the DAQmx Adjust SCXI Calibration VI to specify each input and output point and adjust cal

### DAQmx Setup SCXI 1102 Calibration VI

Sets an SCXI-1102 module to the specified gain value for calibration. 
 Measure calibration input and output points by supplying reference signals to the specified physical channel and measuring the outputs. 
 Use the [DAQmx Adjust SCXI Calibration](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_mxadjustscxical) VI to specify each input and output point and adjust calibration constants.

Note

SCXI-1102/B/C User Manual

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-setup-scxi-1102-calibration-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| calhandle in — calhandle in is a reference to the calibration session that you created using the DAQmx Initialize External Calibration VI. physical channel — physical channel specifies the physical channel to calibrate. gain — gain specifies the gain setting to calibrate. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. calhandle out — calhandle out is a reference to the calibration session. Wire this output to other external calibration VIs. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Setup SCXI Calibration VI

### DAQmx Setup SCXI 1104 Calibration VI

Configures the specified physical channel on an SCXI-1104 module for calibration. 
 Measure calibration input and output points by supplying reference signals to the specified physical channel and measuring the outputs. 
 Use the [DAQmx Adjust SCXI Calibration](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_mxadjustscxical) VI to specify each input and output point and adjust calibration constants.

Note

SCXI-1104/C User Manual

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-setup-scxi-1104-calibration-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| calhandle in — calhandle in is a reference to the calibration session that you created using the DAQmx Initialize External Calibration VI. physical channel — physical channel specifies the physical channel to calibrate. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. calhandle out — calhandle out is a reference to the calibration session. Wire this output to other external calibration VIs. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |

### DAQmx Setup SCXI 1112 Calibration VI

Configures the specified physical channel on an SCXI-1112 module for calibration. 
 Measure calibration input and output points by supplying reference signals to the specified physical channel and measuring the outputs. 
 Use the [DAQmx Adjust SCXI Calibration](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_mxadjustscxical) VI to specify each input and output point and adjust calibration constants.

Note

SCXI-1112 User Manual

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-setup-scxi-1112-calibration-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| calhandle in — calhandle in is a reference to the calibration session that you created using the DAQmx Initialize External Calibration VI. physical channel — physical channel specifies the physical channel to calibrate. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. calhandle out — calhandle out is a reference to the calibration session. Wire this output to other external calibration VIs. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Setup SCXI Calibration VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-scxi-1122-calibration-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02010: DAQmx Setup SCXI 1122 Calibration VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-scxi-1122-calibration-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-scxi-1122-calibration-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Sets an SCXI-1122 module to the specified gain value for calibration. Measure calibration input and output points by supplying reference signals to the specified physical channel and measuring the outputs. Use the DAQmx Adjust SCXI Calibration VI to specify each input and output point and adjust cal

### DAQmx Setup SCXI 1122 Calibration VI

Sets an SCXI-1122 module to the specified gain value for calibration. 
 Measure calibration input and output points by supplying reference signals to the specified physical channel and measuring the outputs. 
 Use the [DAQmx Adjust SCXI Calibration](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_mxadjustscxical) VI to specify each input and output point and adjust calibration constants.

Note

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-setup-scxi-1122-calibration-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| calhandle in — calhandle in is a reference to the calibration session that you created using the DAQmx Initialize External Calibration VI. physical channel — physical channel specifies the physical channel to calibrate. gain — gain specifies the gain setting to calibrate. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. calhandle out — calhandle out is a reference to the calibration session. Wire this output to other external calibration VIs. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Setup SCXI Calibration VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-scxi-1124-calibration-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02011: DAQmx Setup SCXI 1124 Calibration VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-scxi-1124-calibration-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-scxi-1124-calibration-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Writes the specified binary value to the DAC on the specified physical channel at the specified range. Measure the voltage or current generated, then use the DAQmx Adjust SCXI Calibration VI to specify the measured value and adjust calibration constants. Specify at least two calibration points for e

### DAQmx Setup SCXI 1124 Calibration VI

Writes the specified binary value to the DAC on the specified physical channel at the specified range. 
 Measure the voltage or current generated, then use the [DAQmx Adjust SCXI Calibration](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_mxadjustscxical) VI to specify the measured value and adjust calibration constants.

Note

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-setup-scxi-1124-calibration-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| calhandle in — calhandle in is a reference to the calibration session that you created using the DAQmx Initialize External Calibration VI. physical channel — physical channel specifies the physical channel to calibrate. range — range specifies the range to calibrate. 0Volts to 1Volts (14629) 0 V to 1 V. 0Volts to 5Volts (14630) 0 V to 5 V. 0Volts to 10Volts (14631) 0 V to 10 V. -1Volts to 1Volts (14632) -1 V to 1 V. -5Volts to 5Volts (14633) -5 V to 5 V. -10Volts to 10Volts (14634) -10 V to 10 V. 0Amps to 20mAmps (14635) 0 A to 20 mA. DAC value — DAC value is the binary number to write to the DAC. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. calhandle out — calhandle out is a reference to the calibration session. Wire this output to other external calibration VIs. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| 0Volts to 1Volts (14629) | 0 V to 1 V. |
| 0Volts to 5Volts (14630) | 0 V to 5 V. |
| 0Volts to 10Volts (14631) | 0 V to 10 V. |
| -1Volts to 1Volts (14632) | -1 V to 1 V. |
| -5Volts to 5Volts (14633) | -5 V to 5 V. |
| -10Volts to 10Volts (14634) | -10 V to 10 V. |
| 0Amps to 20mAmps (14635) | 0 A to 20 mA. |

### DAQmx Setup SCXI 1126 Calibration VI

Specifies the channel and range on an SCXI-1126 module for calibration. 
 Measure calibration input and output points by supplying reference signals to the specified physical channel and measuring the outputs. 
 Use the [DAQmx Adjust SCXI Calibration](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_mxadjustscxical) VI to specify each input and output point and adjust calibration constants.

Note

SCXI-1126 User Manual

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-setup-scxi-1126-calibration-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| calhandle in — calhandle in is a reference to the calibration session that you created using the DAQmx Initialize External Calibration VI. physical channel — physical channel specifies the physical channel to calibrate. upper frequency limit — upper frequency limit is the high frequency limit in hertz which, with 0 Hz as the low frequency limit, most closely encapsulates the ranges to calibrate. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. calhandle out — calhandle out is a reference to the calibration session. Wire this output to other external calibration VIs. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |

## TOPIC 02019: DAQmx Setup SCXI 1520 Calibration VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-scxi-1520-calibration-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-scxi-1520-calibration-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Sets an SCXI-1520 module to the specified gain value for calibration. This VI disables the sample-and-hold circuitry on the module so external devices that cannot supply the appropriate sample-and-hold timing signals can measure the output. Measure calibration input and output points by supplying re

### DAQmx Setup SCXI 1520 Calibration VI

Sets an SCXI-1520 module to the specified gain value for calibration. 
 This VI disables the sample-and-hold circuitry on the module so external devices that cannot supply the appropriate sample-and-hold timing signals can measure the output.
 Measure calibration input and output points by supplying reference signals to the specified physical channel and measuring the outputs. 
 Use the [DAQmx Adjust SCXI Calibration](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_mxadjustscxical) VI to specify each input and output point and adjust calibration constants.

Note

SCXI-1520 User Manual

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-setup-scxi-1520-calibration-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| calhandle in — calhandle in is a reference to the calibration session that you created using the DAQmx Initialize External Calibration VI. physical channel — physical channel specifies the physical channel to calibrate. gain — gain specifies the gain setting to calibrate. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. calhandle out — calhandle out is a reference to the calibration session. Wire this output to other external calibration VIs. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Setup SCXI Calibration VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-scxi-1521-calibration-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02020: DAQmx Setup SCXI 1521 Calibration VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-scxi-1521-calibration-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-scxi-1521-calibration-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Configures the specified physical channel on an SCXI-1521 module for calibration. Measure calibration input and output points by supplying reference signals to the specified physical channel and measuring the outputs. Use the DAQmx Adjust SCXI Calibration VI to specify each input and output point an

### DAQmx Setup SCXI 1521 Calibration VI

Configures the specified physical channel on an SCXI-1521 module for calibration. 
 Measure calibration input and output points by supplying reference signals to the specified physical channel and measuring the outputs. 
 Use the [DAQmx Adjust SCXI Calibration](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_mxadjustscxical) VI to specify each input and output point and adjust calibration constants.

Note

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-setup-scxi-1521-calibration-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| calhandle in — calhandle in is a reference to the calibration session that you created using the DAQmx Initialize External Calibration VI. physical channel — physical channel specifies the physical channel to calibrate. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. calhandle out — calhandle out is a reference to the calibration session. Wire this output to other external calibration VIs. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Setup SCXI Calibration VI

### DAQmx Setup SCXI 153x Calibration VI

Sets an SCXI-153*x* module to the specified gain value for calibration. 
 Measure calibration input and output points by supplying reference signals to the specified physical channel and measuring the outputs. 
 Use the [DAQmx Adjust SCXI Calibration](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_mxadjustscxical) VI to specify each input and output point and adjust calibration constants.

Note

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-setup-scxi-153x-calibration-vi.png']

| calhandle in — calhandle in is a reference to the calibration session that you created using the DAQmx Initialize External Calibration VI. physical channel — physical channel specifies the physical channel to calibrate. gain — gain specifies the gain setting to calibrate. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. calhandle out — calhandle out is a reference to the calibration session. Wire this output to other external calibration VIs. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. calhandle out — calhandle out is a reference to the calibration session. Wire this output to other external calibration VIs. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |

## TOPIC 02023: DAQmx Setup SCXI Calibration VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-scxi-calibration-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-scxi-calibration-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Sets up calibration for SCXI modules. The instances of this polymorphic VI correspond to the SCXI module you want to calibrate. Refer to the calibration procedure for your module for detailed calibration instructions. After you commit the calibration, you cannot undo it. If any calibration settings

### DAQmx Setup SCXI Calibration VI

Sets up calibration for SCXI modules.
 The instances of this [polymorphic VI](/csh?context=lvcore_lvhelp_using_polymorphic_vis) correspond to the SCXI module you want to calibrate.
 Refer to the [calibration procedure for your module](http://www.ni.com/cgi-bin/redirect.cgi?dest=infcoprod&src=help&openagent&code=rdcalp) for detailed calibration instructions.

Note

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-setup-scxi-calibration-vi.png']

- [DAQmx Setup SCXI 1102 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-scxi-1102-calibration-vi.html) Sets an SCXI-1102 module to the specified gain value for calibration. Measure calibration input and output points by supplying reference signals to the specified physical channel and measuring the outputs. Use the DAQmx Adjust SCXI Calibration VI to specify each input and output point and adjust calibration constants.
- [DAQmx Setup SCXI 1104 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-scxi-1104-calibration-vi.html) Configures the specified physical channel on an SCXI-1104 module for calibration. Measure calibration input and output points by supplying reference signals to the specified physical channel and measuring the outputs. Use the DAQmx Adjust SCXI Calibration VI to specify each input and output point and adjust calibration constants.
- [DAQmx Setup SCXI 1112 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-scxi-1112-calibration-vi.html) Configures the specified physical channel on an SCXI-1112 module for calibration. Measure calibration input and output points by supplying reference signals to the specified physical channel and measuring the outputs. Use the DAQmx Adjust SCXI Calibration VI to specify each input and output point and adjust calibration constants.
- [DAQmx Setup SCXI 1122 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-scxi-1122-calibration-vi.html) Sets an SCXI-1122 module to the specified gain value for calibration. Measure calibration input and output points by supplying reference signals to the specified physical channel and measuring the outputs. Use the DAQmx Adjust SCXI Calibration VI to specify each input and output point and adjust calibration constants.
- [DAQmx Setup SCXI 1124 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-scxi-1124-calibration-vi.html) Writes the specified binary value to the DAC on the specified physical channel at the specified range. Measure the voltage or current generated, then use the DAQmx Adjust SCXI Calibration VI to specify the measured value and adjust calibration constants.
- [DAQmx Setup SCXI 1125 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-scxi-1125-calibration-vi.html) Sets an SCXI-1125 module to the specified gain value for calibration. Measure calibration input and output points by supplying reference signals to the specified physical channel and measuring the outputs. Use the DAQmx Adjust SCXI Calibration VI to specify each input and output point and adjust calibration constants.
- [DAQmx Setup SCXI 1126 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-scxi-1126-calibration-vi.html) Specifies the channel and range on an SCXI-1126 module for calibration. Measure calibration input and output points by supplying reference signals to the specified physical channel and measuring the outputs. Use the DAQmx Adjust SCXI Calibration VI to specify each input and output point and adjust calibration constants.
- [DAQmx Setup SCXI 1141 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-scxi-1141-calibration-vi.html) Sets an SCXI-1141 module to the specified gain value for calibration. Measure calibration input and output points by supplying reference signals to the specified physical channel and measuring the outputs. Use the DAQmx Adjust SCXI Calibration VI to specify each input and output point and adjust calibration constants.
- [DAQmx Setup SCXI 1142 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-scxi-1142-calibration-vi.html) Sets an SCXI-1142 module to the specified gain value for calibration. Measure calibration input and output points by supplying reference signals to the specified physical channel and measuring the outputs. Use the DAQmx Adjust SCXI Calibration VI to specify each input and output point and adjust calibration constants.
- [DAQmx Setup SCXI 1143 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-scxi-1143-calibration-vi.html) Sets an SCXI-1143 module to the specified gain value for calibration. Measure calibration input and output points by supplying reference signals to the specified physical channel and measuring the outputs. Use the DAQmx Adjust SCXI Calibration VI to specify each input and output point and adjust calibration constants.
- [DAQmx Setup SCXI 1502 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-scxi-1502-calibration-vi.html) Sets the SCXI-1502 module to the specified gain value for calibration. Measure calibration input and output points by supplying reference signals to the specified physical channel and measuring the outputs. Use the DAQmx Adjust SCXI Calibration VI to specify each input and output point and adjust calibration constants.
- [DAQmx Setup SCXI 1503 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-scxi-1503-calibration-vi.html) Sets the SCXI-1503 module to the specified gain value for calibration. Measure calibration input and output points by supplying reference signals to the specified physical channel and measuring the outputs. Use the DAQmx Adjust SCXI Calibration VI to specify each input and output point and adjust calibration constants.
- [DAQmx Setup SCXI 1520 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-scxi-1520-calibration-vi.html) Sets an SCXI-1520 module to the specified gain value for calibration. This VI disables the sample-and-hold circuitry on the module so external devices that cannot supply the appropriate sample-and-hold timing signals can measure the output. Measure calibration input and output points by supplying reference signals to the specified physical channel and measuring the outputs. Use the DAQmx Adjust SCXI Calibration VI to specify each input and output point and adjust calibration constants.
- [DAQmx Setup SCXI 1521 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-scxi-1521-calibration-vi.html) Configures the specified physical channel on an SCXI-1521 module for calibration. Measure calibration input and output points by supplying reference signals to the specified physical channel and measuring the outputs. Use the DAQmx Adjust SCXI Calibration VI to specify each input and output point and adjust calibration constants.
- [DAQmx Setup SCXI 153x Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-scxi-153x-calibration-vi.html) Sets an SCXI-153 x module to the specified gain value for calibration. Measure calibration input and output points by supplying reference signals to the specified physical channel and measuring the outputs. Use the DAQmx Adjust SCXI Calibration VI to specify each input and output point and adjust calibration constants.
- [DAQmx Setup SCXI 1540 Calibration VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/calibration/calibrationinfo-llb/daqmx-setup-scxi-1540-calibration-vi.html) Sets an SCXI-1540 module to the specified gain value for calibration. Measure calibration input and output points by supplying reference signals to the specified physical channel and measuring the outputs. Use the DAQmx Adjust SCXI Calibration VI to specify each input and output point and adjust calibration constants.

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/buffer-llb/daqmx-buffer-property-node-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02025: DAQmx Buffer Property Node

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/buffer-llb/daqmx-buffer-property-node-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/buffer-llb/daqmx-buffer-property-node-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: A Property Node with the DAQmx Buffer class preselected. Right-click the Property Node and choose Select Filter from the shortcut menu to make the Property Node show only the properties supported by a particular device installed in the system or supported by all the devices installed in the system.

### DAQmx Buffer Property Node

A Property Node with the [DAQmx Buffer](/csh?context=nidaqmx_nidaq_daqmx_buffer_p) class preselected. Right-click the Property Node and choose **Select Filter** from the shortcut menu to make the Property Node show only the properties supported by a particular device installed in the system or supported by all the devices installed in the system.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-buffer-property-node-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. error in (no error) — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. Input:Buffer Size — Input:Buffer Size is an example of a property you want to get (read) or set (write). task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

## TOPIC 02026: DAQmx Configure Input Buffer VI

- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Overrides the automatic input buffer allocation that NI-DAQmx performs. icon Inputs/Outputs cgenclassrntag.png task/channels in task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task au

### DAQmx Configure Input Buffer VI

Overrides the [automatic input buffer allocation](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_buffersize) that NI-DAQmx performs.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-configure-input-buffer-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. buffer size (in samples per channel) — buffer size (in samples per channel) is the number of samples the buffer can hold for each channel in the task. Zero indicates to allocate no buffer. Use a buffer size of 0 to perform a hardware-timed operation without using a buffer. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Advanced Task Options

## TOPIC 02028: DAQmx Channel Property Node

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/channels-llb/daqmx-channel-property-node-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/channels-llb/daqmx-channel-property-node-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: A Property Node with the DAQmx Channel class preselected. Right-click the Property Node and choose Select Filter from the shortcut menu to make the Property Node show only the properties supported by a particular device installed in the system or supported by all devices installed in the system. ico

### DAQmx Channel Property Node

A Property Node with the [DAQmx Channel](/csh?context=nidaqmx_nidaq_daqmx_channel_p) class preselected. Right-click the Property Node and choose **Select Filter** from the shortcut menu to make the Property Node show only the properties supported by a particular device installed in the system or supported by all devices installed in the system.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-channel-property-node-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. error in (no error) — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. Property — property 1..n is an example of a property you want to get (read) or set (write). |
| --- |

DAQmx - Data Acquisition

## TOPIC 02029: DAQmx Clear TEDS VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/channels-llb/daqmx-clear-teds-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/channels-llb/daqmx-clear-teds-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Removes TEDS information from the physical channel you specify. This VI temporarily overrides any TEDS configuration for the physical channel that you performed in MAX. icon Inputs/Outputs cdaqmxscale.png physical channel physical channel is the name of the physical channel you want to configure. A

### DAQmx Clear TEDS VI

Removes TEDS information from the physical channel you specify. This VI temporarily overrides any TEDS configuration for the physical channel that you performed in MAX.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-clear-teds-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| physical channel — physical channel is the name of the physical channel you want to configure. A DAQmx physical channel constant or control lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a physical channel name to this input. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

DAQmx TEDS

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/channels-llb/daqmx-configure-teds-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02030: DAQmx Configure TEDS VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/channels-llb/daqmx-configure-teds-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/channels-llb/daqmx-configure-teds-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Associates TEDS information with the physical channel you specify. If you do not specify the filename of a data sheet in the virtual TEDS file input, this VI attempts to find a TEDS sensor connected to the physical channel. This VI temporarily overrides any TEDS configuration for the physical channe

### DAQmx Configure TEDS VI

Associates TEDS information with the physical channel you specify. If you do not specify the filename of a data sheet in the **virtual TEDS file** input, this VI attempts to find a TEDS sensor connected to the physical channel. This VI temporarily overrides any TEDS configuration for the physical channel that you performed in MAX.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-configure-teds-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| physical channel — physical channel is the name of the physical channel you want to configure. A DAQmx physical channel constant or control lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a physical channel name to this input. virtual TEDS file — virtual TEDS file is the path to a Virtual TEDS data sheet that you want to associate with the physical channel. If you do not wire anything to this input, this VI attempts to find a TEDS sensor connected to the physical channel. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

Parent topic:

DAQmx TEDS

### DAQmx Physical Channel Property Node

A Property Node with the [DAQmx Physical Channel](/csh?context=nidaqmx_nidaq_daqmx_physical_channel_p) class preselected. Right-click the Property Node and choose **Select Filter** from the shortcut menu to make the Property Node show only the properties supported by a particular device installed in the system or supported by all the devices installed in the system.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-physical-channel-property-node-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| error in (no error) — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. Active Physical Channels — Active Physical Channels is an example of a property you want to get (read) or set (write). error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. ## TOPIC 02032: DAQmx Write TEDS Data (TEDS Data File) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/channels-llb/daqmx-write-teds-data-teds-data-file-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/channels-llb/daqmx-write-teds-data-teds-data-file-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Writes data from a virtual TEDS file to the TEDS sensor. icon Inputs/Outputs ci32.png basic TEDS data options basic TEDS data options specifies how to handle basic TEDS data in the bitstream. Do Not Write (12540) Ignore basic TEDS data. Write To EEPROM (12538) Write basic TEDS data to the EEPROM, ev

### DAQmx Write TEDS Data (TEDS Data File) VI

Writes data from a virtual TEDS file to the TEDS sensor.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-write-teds-data-teds-data-file-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| basic TEDS data options — basic TEDS data options specifies how to handle basic TEDS data in the bitstream. Do Not Write (12540) Ignore basic TEDS data. Write To EEPROM (12538) Write basic TEDS data to the EEPROM, even if the sensor includes a PROM. You cannot write basic TEDS data if the PROM contains data. Write To PROM Once (12539) Write basic TEDS data to the PROM. Any subsequent attempts to write basic TEDS data result in an error. physical channel — physical channel is the name of the physical channel you want to configure. A DAQmx physical channel constant or control lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a physical channel name to this input. virtual TEDS file — virtual TEDS file specifies the filename of a virtual TEDS file that contains the bitstream to write. National Instruments provides a LabVIEW library for viewing and editing TEDS bitstreams and virtual TEDS files. You can download the TEDS Library for LabVIEW at ni.com/pnp. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Do Not Write (12540) | Ignore basic TEDS data. |
| Write To EEPROM (12538) | Write basic TEDS data to the EEPROM, even if the sensor includes a PROM. You cannot write basic TEDS data if the PROM contains data. |
| Write To PROM Once (12539) | Write basic TEDS data to the PROM. Any subsequent attempts to write basic TEDS data result in an error. |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Write TEDS Data VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/channels-llb/daqmx-write-teds-data-u8-array-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02033: DAQmx Write TEDS Data (U8 Array) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/channels-llb/daqmx-write-teds-data-u8-array-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/channels-llb/daqmx-write-teds-data-u8-array-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Writes data from a 1D array of 8-bit unsigned integers to the TEDS sensor. icon Inputs/Outputs ci32.png basic TEDS data options basic TEDS data options specifies how to handle basic TEDS data in the bitstream. Do Not Write (12540) Ignore basic TEDS data. Write To EEPROM (12538) Write basic TEDS data

### DAQmx Write TEDS Data (U8 Array) VI

Writes data from a 1D array of 8-bit unsigned integers to the TEDS sensor.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-write-teds-data-u8-array-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| basic TEDS data options — basic TEDS data options specifies how to handle basic TEDS data in the bitstream. Do Not Write (12540) Ignore basic TEDS data. Write To EEPROM (12538) Write basic TEDS data to the EEPROM, even if the sensor includes a PROM. You cannot write basic TEDS data if the PROM contains data. Write To PROM Once (12539) Write basic TEDS data to the PROM. Any subsequent attempts to write basic TEDS data result in an error. physical channel — physical channel is the name of the physical channel you want to configure. A DAQmx physical channel constant or control lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a physical channel name to this input. TEDS bitstream — TEDS bitstream is the TEDS bitstream to write to the sensor. This bitstream must be constructed according to the IEEE 1451.4 specification. National Instruments provides a LabVIEW library for viewing and editing TEDS bitstreams and virtual TEDS files. You can download the TEDS Library for LabVIEW at ni.com/pnp. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Do Not Write (12540) | Ignore basic TEDS data. |
| Write To EEPROM (12538) | Write basic TEDS data to the EEPROM, even if the sensor includes a PROM. You cannot write basic TEDS data if the PROM contains data. |
| Write To PROM Once (12539) | Write basic TEDS data to the PROM. Any subsequent attempts to write basic TEDS data result in an error. |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Write TEDS Data VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/channels-llb/daqmx-write-teds-data-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02034: DAQmx Write TEDS Data VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/channels-llb/daqmx-write-teds-data-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/channels-llb/daqmx-write-teds-data-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Writes TEDS data to the TEDS sensor connected to the physical channel you specify. This VI can write TEDS data from a 1D array of 8-bit unsigned integers or from a virtual TEDS file. icon

### DAQmx Write TEDS Data VI

Writes [TEDS](/csh?context=nidaqmx_measfunds_teds) data to the TEDS sensor connected to the **physical channel** you specify. This VI can write TEDS data from a 1D array of 8-bit unsigned integers or from a virtual TEDS file.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-write-teds-data-vi.png']

- [DAQmx Write TEDS Data (U8 Array) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/configure/channels-llb/daqmx-write-teds-data-u8-array-vi.html) Writes data from a 1D array of 8-bit unsigned integers to the TEDS sensor.
- [DAQmx Write TEDS Data (TEDS Data File) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/configure/channels-llb/daqmx-write-teds-data-teds-data-file-vi.html) Writes data from a virtual TEDS file to the TEDS sensor.

Parent topic:

DAQmx TEDS

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/events/hardwaresignals-llb/daqmx-export-signal-most-signals-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02035: DAQmx Export Signal (Most Signals) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/events/hardwaresignals-llb/daqmx-export-signal-most-signals-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/events/hardwaresignals-llb/daqmx-export-signal-most-signals-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Routes a control signal to the terminal you specify. The output terminal can reside on the device that generates the control signal or on a different device. You can use this VI to share clocks and triggers among multiple tasks and devices. The routes this VI creates are task-based routes. icon Inpu

### DAQmx Export Signal (Most Signals) VI

Routes a [control signal](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_timetrig) to the [terminal](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_termnames) you specify. The output terminal can reside on the device that generates the control signal or on a different device. You can use this VI to share clocks and triggers among multiple tasks and devices. The routes this VI creates are [task-based](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_taskbasedrouting) routes.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-export-signal-most-signals-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. signal — signal is the name of the trigger, clock, or event to export. 10 MHz Reference Clock (12536) Output of an oscillator that you can use to synchronize multiple devices. 20Mhz Timebase Clock (12486) Output of an oscillator that is the onboard source of the Master Timebase. Other timebases are derived from this clock. Advance Complete Event (12492) Signal a switch product generates after it both executes the command(s) in a scan list entry and waits for the settling time to elapse. Advance Trigger (12488) Trigger that moves a switch to the next entry in a scan list. AI Convert Clock (12484) Clock that causes an analog-to-digital conversion on a multiplexed device. One conversion corresponds to a single sample from one channel. AI Hold Complete Event (12493) Signal a multiplexed device generates when the device latches analog input data (the ADC enters "hold" mode) and it is safe for any external switching hardware to remove the signal and replace it with the next signal. This event does not indicate the completion of the actual analog-to-digital conversion. Change Detection Event (12511) Signal a static DIO device generates when the device detects a rising or falling edge on any of the lines or ports you selected when you configured change detection timing. Counter Output Event (12494) Signal a counter generates. Each time the counter reaches terminal count, this signal toggles or pulses. This also applies to counter output tasks. Reference Trigger (12490) Trigger that establishes the reference point between pretrigger and posttrigger samples. Sample Clock (12487) Clock the device uses to time each sample. Start Trigger (12491) Trigger that begins a measurement or generation. Watchdog Timer Expired Event (12512) Signal a static DIO device generates when the watchdog timer expires. output terminal — output terminal is the destination of the exported signal. A DAQmx terminal constant lists all terminals on installed devices. You can also specify a string containing a comma-delimited list of terminal names. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| 10 MHz Reference Clock (12536) | Output of an oscillator that you can use to synchronize multiple devices. |
| 20Mhz Timebase Clock (12486) | Output of an oscillator that is the onboard source of the Master Timebase. Other timebases are derived from this clock. |
| Advance Complete Event (12492) | Signal a switch product generates after it both executes the command(s) in a scan list entry and waits for the settling time to elapse. |
| Advance Trigger (12488) | Trigger that moves a switch to the next entry in a scan list. |
| AI Convert Clock (12484) | Clock that causes an analog-to-digital conversion on a multiplexed device. One conversion corresponds to a single sample from one channel. |
| AI Hold Complete Event (12493) | Signal a multiplexed device generates when the device latches analog input data (the ADC enters "hold" mode) and it is safe for any external switching hardware to remove the signal and replace it with the next signal. This event does not indicate the completion of the actual analog-to-digital conversion. |
| Change Detection Event (12511) | Signal a static DIO device generates when the device detects a rising or falling edge on any of the lines or ports you selected when you configured change detection timing. |
| Counter Output Event (12494) | Signal a counter generates. Each time the counter reaches terminal count, this signal toggles or pulses. This also applies to counter output tasks. |
| Reference Trigger (12490) | Trigger that establishes the reference point between pretrigger and posttrigger samples. |
| Sample Clock (12487) | Clock the device uses to time each sample. |
| Start Trigger (12491) | Trigger that begins a measurement or generation. |
| Watchdog Timer Expired Event (12512) | Signal a static DIO device generates when the watchdog timer expires. |

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-export-signal-property-node-vi.png']
