| task — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. error in (no error) — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. Clocks:AI Convert Clock:Output Terminal — Clocks:AI Convert Clock:Output Terminal is an example of a property you want to get (read) or set (write). task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |

DAQmx Constants & Property Nodes

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/events/hardwaresignals-llb/daqmx-export-signal-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02037: DAQmx Export Signal VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/events/hardwaresignals-llb/daqmx-export-signal-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/events/hardwaresignals-llb/daqmx-export-signal-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Routes a control signal to the terminal you specify. The output terminal can reside on the device that generates the control signal or on a different device. You can use this VI to share clocks and triggers among multiple tasks and devices. The routes this VI creates are task-based routes. The DAQmx

### DAQmx Export Signal VI

Routes a [control signal](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_timetrig) to the [terminal](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_termnames) you specify. The output terminal can reside on the device that generates the control signal or on a different device. You can use this VI to share clocks and triggers among multiple tasks and devices. The routes this VI creates are [task-based](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_taskbasedrouting) routes.

The DAQmx Export Signal properties include additional configuration options for export signal operations.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-export-signal-vi.png']

- [DAQmx Export Signal (Most Signals) VI](../../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/configure/events/hardwaresignals-llb/daqmx-export-signal-most-signals-vi.html) Routes a control signal to the terminal you specify. The output terminal can reside on the device that generates the control signal or on a different device. You can use this VI to share clocks and triggers among multiple tasks and devices. The routes this VI creates are task-based routes.

Parent topic:

DAQmx Export Signal

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/routing-llb/daqmx-connect-terminals-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02038: DAQmx Connect Terminals VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/routing-llb/daqmx-connect-terminals-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/routing-llb/daqmx-connect-terminals-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates a route between a source and destination terminal. The route can carry a variety of digital signals, such as triggers, clocks, and hardware events. The source and destination terminals can be on different devices as long as a connecting public bus, such as RTSI or the PXI backplane, connects

### DAQmx Connect Terminals VI

Creates a route between a source and destination [terminal](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_termnames). The route can carry a variety of digital signals, such as triggers, clocks, and hardware events.

The source and destination terminals can be on different devices as long as a connecting public bus, such as RTSI or the PXI backplane, connects the devices. The DAQmx Connect Terminals VI does not modify a task. When the VI runs, the route is immediately reserved and committed to hardware. This type of routing is called [immediate routing](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_immediaterouting).

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-connect-terminals-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| source terminal — source terminal specifies the originating terminal of the route. A DAQmx terminal constant lists all terminals available on devices installed in the system. You also can specify a source terminal by wiring a string that contains a terminal name. destination terminal — destination terminal specifies the receiving terminal of the route. A DAQmx terminal constant provides a list of all terminals available on devices installed in the system. You also can specify a destination terminal by wiring a string that contains a terminal name. invert polarity — invert polarity specifies whether to invert the signal this VI routes from the source terminal to the destination terminal. If the device is not capable of signal inversion or if a previous route reserved the inversion circuitry in an incompatible configuration, attempting to invert the signal causes an error. no (0) Do not invert the signal. yes (1) Invert the signal. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| no (0) | Do not invert the signal. |
| yes (1) | Invert the signal. |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Signal Routing

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/routing-llb/daqmx-disconnect-terminals-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02039: DAQmx Disconnect Terminals VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/routing-llb/daqmx-disconnect-terminals-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/routing-llb/daqmx-disconnect-terminals-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Removes signal routes you created by using the DAQmx Connect Terminals VI. The DAQmx Disconnect Terminals VI cannot remove task-based routes, such as those you create through timing and triggering configuration. When this VI runs, it immediately removes the route. This type of routing is called imme

### DAQmx Disconnect Terminals VI

Removes signal routes you created by using the [DAQmx Connect Terminals](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_mxconnectterminals) VI. The DAQmx Disconnect Terminals VI cannot remove task-based routes, such as those you create through timing and triggering configuration.

When this VI runs, it immediately removes the route. This type of routing is called [immediate routing](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_immediaterouting).

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-disconnect-terminals-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| source terminal — source terminal specifies the originating terminal of the route. A DAQmx terminal constant lists all terminals available on devices installed in the system. You also can specify a source terminal by wiring a string that contains a terminal name. destination terminal — destination terminal specifies the receiving terminal of the route. A DAQmx terminal constant provides a list of all terminals available on devices installed in the system. You also can specify a destination terminal by wiring a string that contains a terminal name. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. error out — error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
| --- |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Signal Routing

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/routing-llb/daqmx-tristate-output-terminal-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02040: DAQmx Tristate Output Terminal VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/routing-llb/daqmx-tristate-output-terminal-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/routing-llb/daqmx-tristate-output-terminal-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Sets a terminal to high-impedance state. If you connect an external signal to a terminal on the I/O connector, the terminal must be in high-impedance state. Otherwise, the device could double-drive the terminal and damage the hardware. If you use this VI on a terminal in an active route, the VI fail

### DAQmx Tristate Output Terminal VI

Sets a terminal to [high-impedance state](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_tristating). If you connect an external signal to a terminal on the I/O connector, the terminal must be in high-impedance state. Otherwise, the device could double-drive the terminal and damage the hardware. If you use this VI on a terminal in an active route, the VI fails and returns an error.

The [DAQmx Reset Device](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_mxresetdevice) VI sets all terminals on the I/O connector to high-impedance state but aborts any running tasks associated with the device.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-tristate-output-terminal-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| output terminal — output terminal specifies the terminal on the I/O connector to set to high-impedance state. A DAQmx terminal constant lists all available terminals on installed devices. You also can specify an output terminal by using a string that contains a terminal name. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. error out — error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
| --- |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Signal Routing

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/scales-llb/daqmx-scale-property-node-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02041: DAQmx Scale Property Node

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/scales-llb/daqmx-scale-property-node-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/scales-llb/daqmx-scale-property-node-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: A Property Node with the DAQmx Scale class preselected. Right-click the Property Node and choose Select Filter from the shortcut menu to make the Property Node show only the properties supported by a particular device installed in the system or supported by all the devices installed in the system. i

### DAQmx Scale Property Node

A Property Node with the [DAQmx Scale](/csh?context=nidaqmx_nidaq_daqmx_scale_p) class preselected. Right-click the Property Node and choose **Select Filter** from the shortcut menu to make the Property Node show only the properties supported by a particular device installed in the system or supported by all the devices installed in the system.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-scale-property-node-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| error in (no error) — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. Active Scale — Active Scale is an example of a property you want to get (read) or set (write). error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

### DAQmx Delete Saved Global Channel VI

Deletes the specified [global channel](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_chans) from MAX. This VI does not remove the global channel from [tasks](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_tasksnidaqmx) that use it.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-delete-saved-global-channel-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| channel name — channel name is the global channel you want to delete. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |

If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. <!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/storage-llb/daqmx-delete-saved-task-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02044: DAQmx Delete Saved Task VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/storage-llb/daqmx-delete-saved-task-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/storage-llb/daqmx-delete-saved-task-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Deletes the specified task from MAX. This VI does not clear the copy of the task stored in memory. Use the DAQmx Clear Task VI to clear that copy of the task. icon Inputs/Outputs cgenclassrntag.png task in task in is the name of the task to which this operation applies. cerrcodeclst.png error in err

### DAQmx Delete Saved Task VI

Deletes the specified [task](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_tasksnidaqmx) from MAX. This VI does not clear the copy of the task stored in memory. Use the [DAQmx Clear Task](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_mxcleartask) VI to clear that copy of the task.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-delete-saved-task-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task in — task in is the name of the task to which this operation applies. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function completes execution. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Storage

## TOPIC 02048: DAQmx Save Global Channel VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/storage-llb/daqmx-save-global-channel-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/storage-llb/daqmx-save-global-channel-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Saves the specified local or global channel to MAX as a global channel. You must specify both the local or global channel to save and a task that contains that channel. Programmatically saved global channels cannot be viewed in NI-DAQ 7.3 or earlier. To view a programmatically saved global channel i

### DAQmx Save Global Channel VI

Saves the specified [local or global channel](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_chans) to MAX as a global channel. You must specify both the local or global channel to save and a task that contains that channel.

Programmatically saved global channels cannot be viewed in NI-DAQ 7.3 or earlier. To view a programmatically saved global channel in an earlier version of NI-DAQ, first use MAX to open the global channel in NI-DAQ 7.4 or later and resave it.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-save-global-channel-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| allow interactive deletion? — allow interactive deletion? specifies whether to allow the task, global channel, or custom scale to be deleted through MAX. allow interactive editing? — allow interactive editing? specifies whether to allow the task, global channel, or custom scale to be edited in the DAQ Assistant. If allow interactive editing? is TRUE, the DAQ Assistant must support all task or global channel settings. task/channels in — task/channels in is the task that contains the local or global channel you want to save. If you use a DAQmx global channel constant or control to select a global channel to modify and save, wire that constant or control to both this input and the channel name input. channel name — channel name is the local or global channel you want to save. The channel must be inside the task specified with the task/channels in input. If you use a DAQmx global channel constant or control to select a global channel to modify and save, wire that constant or control to both this input and the task/channels in input. save as — save as is the name to save the task, global channel, or custom scale as. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the name currently assigned to the task, global channel, or custom scale. author — author is a name to store with the task, global channel, or custom scale. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. overwrite existing channel? — overwrite existing channel? specifies whether to overwrite a global channel of the same name if one is already saved in MAX. If this input is FALSE and a global channel of the same name is already saved in MAX, this VI returns an error. task out — task out is the same task or global channels you wired to the task/channels in input. This output is not the saved task or global channels. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Storage

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/storage-llb/daqmx-save-task-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02050: DAQmx Save Task VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/storage-llb/daqmx-save-task-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/storage-llb/daqmx-save-task-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Saves the specified task and any local channels it contains to MAX. This VI does not save global channels. Use the DAQmx Save Global Channel VI to save global channels. Programmatically saved tasks cannot be viewed in NI-DAQ 7.3 or earlier. To view a programmatically saved task in an earlier version

### DAQmx Save Task VI

Saves the specified [task](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_tasksnidaqmx) and any [local channels](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_chans) it contains to MAX. This VI does not save global channels. Use the [DAQmx Save Global Channel](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_mxsaveglobalchan) VI to save global channels.

Programmatically saved tasks cannot be viewed in NI-DAQ 7.3 or earlier. To view a programmatically saved task in an earlier version of NI-DAQ, first use MAX to open the task in NI-DAQ 7.4 or later and resave it.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-save-task-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| allow interactive deletion? — allow interactive deletion? specifies whether to allow the task, global channel, or custom scale to be deleted through MAX. allow interactive editing? — allow interactive editing? specifies whether to allow the task, global channel, or custom scale to be edited in the DAQ Assistant. If allow interactive editing? is TRUE, the DAQ Assistant must support all task or global channel settings. task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. save as — save as is the name to save the task, global channel, or custom scale as. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the name currently assigned to the task, global channel, or custom scale. author — author is a name to store with the task, global channel, or custom scale. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. overwrite existing task? — overwrite existing task? specifies whether to overwrite a task of the same name if one is already saved in MAX. If this input is FALSE and a task of the same name is already saved in MAX, this VI returns an error. task out — task out is the same task or global channels you wired to the task/channels in input. This output is not the saved task or global channels. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Storage

The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. <!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-add-network-device-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02052: DAQmx Add Network Device VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-add-network-device-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-add-network-device-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Adds a Network cDAQ device to the system and, if specified, attempts to reserve it. icon Inputs/Outputs cstr.png device IP address/hostname device IP address/hostname specifies the string containing the IP address (in dotted decimal notation) or hostname of the device to add to the system. cbool.png

### DAQmx Add Network Device VI

Adds a Network cDAQ device to the system and, if specified, attempts to reserve it.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-add-network-device-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| device IP address/hostname — device IP address/hostname specifies the string containing the IP address (in dotted decimal notation) or hostname of the device to add to the system. attempt device reservation? — attempt device reservation? indicates if a reservation should be attempted after the device is successfully added. By default, this parameter is set to false. Note MAX automatically attempts to reserve a device when the device is added, but this VI does not. device name — device name indicates the name to assign to the device. If unspecified, NI-DAQmx chooses the device name. timeout — timeout specifies the time in seconds to wait for the device to respond before timing out. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. device out — device out is the name of the device the operation applied to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

Parent topic:

DAQmx System Setup

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-are-configured-cdaq-sync-ports-disconnected-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02053: DAQmx Are Configured cDAQ Sync Ports Disconnected VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-are-configured-cdaq-sync-ports-disconnected-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-are-configured-cdaq-sync-ports-disconnected-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Verifies configured cDAQ Sync connections between devices. Failures generally indicate a wiring issue or that a device has been powered off or removed. Stop all NI-DAQmx tasks running on the devices prior to running this VI because any running tasks cause the verification process to fail. Verificati

### DAQmx Are Configured cDAQ Sync Ports Disconnected VI

Verifies configured cDAQ Sync connections between devices. Failures generally indicate a wiring issue or that a device has been powered off or removed. Stop all NI-DAQmx tasks running on the devices prior to running this VI because any running tasks cause the verification process to fail.

Note

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-are-configured-cdaq-sync-ports-disconnected-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| chassis/devices/ports — chassis/devices/ports specifies the names of the CompactDAQ chassis, C Series modules, or cDAQ Sync ports in comma separated form to search. If no names are specified, all cDAQ Sync ports on non-simulated devices are scanned. timeout — timeout specifies the time in seconds to wait for the device to respond before timing out. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. disconnected ports exist — disconnected ports exist indicates if any port connections are unverified. disconnected ports — disconnected ports returns the port-to-port connections that failed verification. output port — input port — error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |
| output port — input port — |

Parent topic:

DAQmx cDAQ Sync

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-auto-configure-cdaq-sync-connections-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02054: DAQmx Auto-Configure cDAQ Sync Connections VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-auto-configure-cdaq-sync-connections-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-auto-configure-cdaq-sync-connections-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Detects and configures cDAQ Sync connections between devices. Stop all NI-DAQmx tasks running on the devices prior to running this VI because any running tasks cause auto-configuration to fail. Auto-configure toggles lines on ports during configuration. Upon completion, all previous configurations a

### DAQmx Auto-Configure cDAQ Sync Connections VI

Detects and configures cDAQ Sync connections between devices. Stop all NI-DAQmx tasks running on the devices prior to running this VI because any running tasks cause auto-configuration to fail.

Note

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-auto-configure-cdaq-sync-connections-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| chassis/devices/ports — chassis/devices/ports specifies the names of the CompactDAQ chassis, C Series modules, or cDAQ Sync ports in comma separated form to search. Connections to ports not specified for this parameter are not configured. If no names are specified, all cDAQ Sync ports on connected, non-simulated devices are scanned. timeout — timeout specifies the time in seconds to wait for the device to respond before timing out. If a timeout occurs, no configuration is changed. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. connections configured — connections configured returns the configured port-to-port connections. output port — input port — error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |
| output port — input port — |

Parent topic:

DAQmx cDAQ Sync

A DAQmx device name constant lists all

### DAQmx Delete Network Device VI

Deletes a Network DAQ device previously added to the host. If the device is reserved, it is unreserved before it is removed.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-delete-network-device-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| device in — device in is the name as configured in MAX of the device to which this operation applies. A DAQmx device name constant lists all devices installed in the system. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |

## TOPIC 02056: DAQmx Device Property Node

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-device-property-node-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-device-property-node-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: A Property Node with the DAQmx Device class preselected. Right-click the Property Node and choose Select Filter from the shortcut menu to make the Property Node show only the properties supported by a particular device installed in the system or supported by all the devices installed in the system.

### DAQmx Device Property Node

A Property Node with the [DAQmx Device](/csh?context=nidaqmx_nidaq_daqmx_device_p) class preselected. Right-click the Property Node and choose **Select Filter** from the shortcut menu to make the Property Node show only the properties supported by a particular device installed in the system or supported by all the devices installed in the system.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-device-property-node-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| error in (no error) — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. Active Device — Active Device is an example of a property you want to get (read) or set (write). error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`

A DAQmx device name constant lists all devices installed in the system. cerrcodeclst.png error i

### DAQmx Get Power Up States (Analog) VI

(Deprecated) Gets the power up states for analog physical channels.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-get-power-up-states-analog-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| device in — device in is the name as configured in MAX of the device to which this operation applies. A DAQmx device name constant lists all devices installed in the system. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. device out — device out is the name of the device the operation applied to. voltage power up states — voltage power up states contains the physical channels and power up states set. Each element of the array contains a physical channel and the power up state set for that physical channel. Voltage power up states are set only for physical channels that support voltage output. physical channel — physical channel is the physical channel that was modified. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels for devices installed in the system. value — value is the power up state set for the physical channel specified with the physical channel output. current power up states — current power up states contains the physical channels and power up states set. Each element of the array contains a physical channel and the power up state set for that physical channel. Power up states are set only for physical channels that support current output. physical channel — physical channel is the physical channel that was modified. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels for devices installed in the system. value — value is the power up state set for the physical channel specified with the physical channel output. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |
| physical channel — physical channel is the physical channel that was modified. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels for devices installed in the system. value — value is the power up state set for the physical channel specified with the physical channel output. |
| physical channel — physical channel is the physical channel that was modified. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels for devices installed in the system. value — value is the power up state set for the physical channel specified with the physical channel output. |

DAQmx Get Power Up States VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-get-power-up-states-analog-with-output-type-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02058: DAQmx Get Power Up States (Analog With Output Type) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-get-power-up-states-analog-with-output-type-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-get-power-up-states-analog-with-output-type-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Gets the power up states for analog physical channels. icon Inputs/Outputs cdaqmxscale.png physical channels physical channels is the digital line or port to modify. You cannot modify dedicated digital input lines. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all lines and ports for devices installed i

### DAQmx Get Power Up States (Analog With Output Type) VI

Gets the power up states for analog physical channels.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-get-power-up-states-analog-with-output-type-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| physical channels — physical channels is the digital line or port to modify. You cannot modify dedicated digital input lines. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all lines and ports for devices installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of digital lines or ports to this input. If you have an array of lines or ports, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. analog power up states — analog power up states contains the physical channels and power up states. Each element of the array contains a physical channel and the power up state for that physical channel. physical channel — physical channel is the physical channel that was modified. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels for devices installed in the system. output type — output type indicates the output type of the physical channel. Voltage (10322) Voltage output. Current (10134) Current output. High-Impedance (12527) High-impedance state. value — value is the power up state set for the physical channel specified with the physical channel output. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| physical channel — physical channel is the physical channel that was modified. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels for devices installed in the system. output type — output type indicates the output type of the physical channel. Voltage (10322) Voltage output. Current (10134) Current output. High-Impedance (12527) High-impedance state. value — value is the power up state set for the physical channel specified with the physical channel output. |  |
| Voltage (10322) | Voltage output. |
| Current (10134) | Current output. |
| High-Impedance (12527) | High-impedance state. |

DAQmx Get Power Up States VI

## TOPIC 02059: DAQmx Get Power Up States (Digital Logic Family) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-get-power-up-states-digital-logic-family-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-get-power-up-states-digital-logic-family-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Gets the digital logic family for a device. icon Inputs/Outputs cdaqmxscale.png device in device in is the name as configured in MAX of the device to which this operation applies. A DAQmx device name constant lists all devices installed in the system. cerrcodeclst.png error in error in describes err

### DAQmx Get Power Up States (Digital Logic Family) VI

Gets the digital logic family for a device.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-get-power-up-states-digital-logic-family-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| device in — device in is the name as configured in MAX of the device to which this operation applies. A DAQmx device name constant lists all devices installed in the system. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. device out — device out is the name of the device the operation applied to. logic family — logic family specifies the logic family set to the device to when it powers up. A logic family corresponds to voltage thresholds that are compatible with a group of voltage standards. Refer to device documentation for information on the logic high and logic low voltages for these logic families. 2.5 V (14620) Compatible with CMOS signals. 3.3 V (14621) Compatible with LVTTL and LVCMOS signals. 5.0 V (14619) Compatible with TTL and CMOS signals. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| 2.5 V (14620) | Compatible with CMOS signals. |
| 3.3 V (14621) | Compatible with LVTTL and LVCMOS signals. |
| 5.0 V (14619) | Compatible with TTL and CMOS signals. |

- source_description: Gets the resistor level for lines when they are in tristate logic. icon Inputs/Outputs cdaqmxscale.png device in device in is the name as configured in MAX of the device to which this operation applies. A DAQmx device name constant lists all devices installed in the system. cerrcodeclst.png error in

### DAQmx Get Power Up States (Digital Pull Up Pull Down) VI

Gets the resistor level for lines when they are in tristate logic.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-get-power-up-states-digital-pull-up-pull-down-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| device in — device in is the name as configured in MAX of the device to which this operation applies. A DAQmx device name constant lists all devices installed in the system. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. device out — device out is the name of the device the operation applied to. pull up pull down levels — pull up pull down levels is the tristate logic level (either pull up or pull down) for each line. physical channel — physical channel is the physical channel that was modified. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels for devices installed in the system. power up state — power up state is the pull up pull down level set for the physical channel specified with the physical channel output. Pull-up (15950) High logic. Pull-down (15951) Low logic. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| physical channel — physical channel is the physical channel that was modified. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels for devices installed in the system. power up state — power up state is the pull up pull down level set for the physical channel specified with the physical channel output. Pull-up (15950) High logic. Pull-down (15951) Low logic. |  |
| Pull-up (15950) | High logic. |
| Pull-down (15951) | Low logic. |

## TOPIC 02061: DAQmx Get Power Up States (Digital) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-get-power-up-states-digital-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-get-power-up-states-digital-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Gets the power up states for digital physical lines. icon Inputs/Outputs cdaqmxscale.png device in device in is the name as configured in MAX of the device to which this operation applies. A DAQmx device name constant lists all devices installed in the system. cerrcodeclst.png error in error in desc

### DAQmx Get Power Up States (Digital) VI

Gets the power up states for digital physical lines.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-get-power-up-states-digital-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| device in — device in is the name as configured in MAX of the device to which this operation applies. A DAQmx device name constant lists all devices installed in the system. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. device out — device out is the name of the device the operation applied to. power up states — power up states contains the physical channels and power up states set. Each element of the array contains a physical channel and the power up state set for that physical channel. physical channel — physical channel is the physical channel that was modified. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels for devices installed in the system. power up state — power up state is the power up state set for the physical channel specified with the physical channel output. High (10192) High logic. Low (10214) Low logic. Tristate (10310) High-impedance state. This power up state is set only on devices with bidirectional ports, and only on entire ports. This power up state is not set on dedicated digital output lines. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| physical channel — physical channel is the physical channel that was modified. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels for devices installed in the system. power up state — power up state is the power up state set for the physical channel specified with the physical channel output. High (10192) High logic. Low (10214) Low logic. Tristate (10310) High-impedance state. This power up state is set only on devices with bidirectional ports, and only on entire ports. This power up state is not set on dedicated digital output lines. |  |
| High (10192) | High logic. |
| Low (10214) | Low logic. |
| Tristate (10310) | High-impedance state. This power up state is set only on devices with bidirectional ports, and only on entire ports. This power up state is not set on dedicated digital output lines. |

DAQmx Get Power Up States VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-get-power-up-states-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02062: DAQmx Get Power Up States VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-get-power-up-states-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-get-power-up-states-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Returns the power up states that physical channels on a device are set to when the device powers up or when you reset the device. This VI returns data for only one line. The instances of this polymorphic VI specify whether to get power up states for analog or digital physical channels. icon

### DAQmx Get Power Up States VI

Returns the power up states that physical channels on a device are set to when the device powers up or when you reset the device. 
 This VI returns data for only one line.

The instances of this [polymorphic VI](/csh?context=lvcore_lvhelp_using_polymorphic_vis) specify whether to get power up states for analog or digital physical channels.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-get-power-up-states-vi.png']

- [DAQmx Get Power Up States (Digital) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-get-power-up-states-digital-vi.html) Gets the power up states for digital physical lines.
- [DAQmx Get Power Up States (Digital Logic Family) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-get-power-up-states-digital-logic-family-vi.html) Gets the digital logic family for a device.
- [DAQmx Get Power Up States (Digital Pull Up Pull Down) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-get-power-up-states-digital-pull-up-pull-down-vi.html) Gets the resistor level for lines when they are in tristate logic.
- [DAQmx Get Power Up States (Analog) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-get-power-up-states-analog-vi.html) (Deprecated) Gets the power up states for analog physical channels.
- [DAQmx Get Power Up States (Analog With Output Type) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-get-power-up-states-analog-with-output-type-vi.html) Gets the power up states for analog physical channels.

## TOPIC 02063: DAQmx Read ID Pin Memory VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-read-id-pin-memory-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-read-id-pin-memory-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Reads and returns the data and format code stored in the memory attached to the specified ID Pin. icon Inputs/Outputs cdaqmxscale.png device in device in is the name as configured in MAX of the device to which this operation applies. A DAQmx device name constant lists all devices installed in the sy

### DAQmx Read ID Pin Memory VI

Reads and returns the data and format code stored in the memory attached to the specified ID Pin.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-read-id-pin-memory-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| device in — device in is the name as configured in MAX of the device to which this operation applies. A DAQmx device name constant lists all devices installed in the system. id pin name — id pin name is the name of the ID Pin to access (ex: id0). error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. device out — device out is the name of the device the operation applied to. data — data returns the 1D array of 8-bit unsigned integers read from the ID Pin memory format code — format code returns the format code read from the memory. Use this when interpreting the data read. Detail of format codes are as follow: 0x01 UTF-8 String 0x10..0x7F Available for users other reserved error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| 0x01 | UTF-8 String |
| 0x10..0x7F | Available for users |
| other | reserved |

## TOPIC 02065: DAQmx Reserve Network Device VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-reserve-network-device-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-reserve-network-device-vi.html

- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Reserves the Network DAQ device for the current host. Reservation is required to run NI-DAQmx tasks, and the device must be added in MAX before it can be reserved. icon Inputs/Outputs cdaqmxscale.png device in device in is the name as configured in MAX of the device to which this operation applies.

### DAQmx Reserve Network Device VI

Reserves the Network DAQ device for the current host. Reservation is required to run NI-DAQmx tasks, and the device must be added in MAX before it can be reserved.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-reserve-network-device-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| device in — device in is the name as configured in MAX of the device to which this operation applies. A DAQmx device name constant lists all devices installed in the system. override reservation? — override reservation? indicates if an existing reservation on the device should be overridden by this reservation. By default, this parameter is set to false. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. device out — device out is the name of the device the operation applied to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-reset-device-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02066: DAQmx Reset Device VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-reset-device-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-reset-device-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Immediately aborts all active tasks associated with a device, disconnects any routes, and returns the device to an initialized state. Aborting a task immediately terminates the currently active operation, such as a read or a write. Aborting a task puts the task into an unstable but recoverable state

### DAQmx Reset Device VI

Immediately aborts all active tasks associated with a device, disconnects any routes, and returns the device to an [initialized](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxdevconsid_initstates) state. Aborting a task immediately terminates the currently active operation, such as a read or a write. Aborting a task puts the task into an unstable but recoverable state. To recover the task, use DAQmx Start to restart the task or use DAQmx Stop to reset the task without starting it.

This VI does not wait for the device to reset before continuing execution. When resetting a chassis, attached modules are unavailable. If you attempt to reset an attached module during this time, you will receive an error.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-reset-device-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| device in — device in is the name as configured in MAX of the device to which this operation applies. A DAQmx device name constant lists all devices installed in the system. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. error out — error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
| --- |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Device Configuration

A DAQmx device name constant lists all devi

### DAQmx Self-Test Device VI

Performs a brief test of device resources. If a failure occurs, refer to your device documentation for more information.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-self-test-device-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| device in — device in is the name as configured in MAX of the device to which this operation applies. A DAQmx device name constant lists all devices installed in the system. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

A DAQmx device name constant lists all devices installed in the system. c1dcclst.png voltage powe

### DAQmx Set Power Up States (Analog) VI

(Deprecated) Updates power up states for analog physical channels.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-set-power-up-states-analog-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| device in — device in is the name as configured in MAX of the device to which this operation applies. A DAQmx device name constant lists all devices installed in the system. voltage power up states — voltage power up states contains the physical channels and power up states to set. Each element of the array contains a physical channel and the power up state to set for that physical channel. You can set voltage power up states only for physical channels that support voltage output. physical channel — physical channel is the physical channel to modify. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels for devices installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. value — value is the power up state to set for the physical channel specified with the physical channel input. current power up states — current power up states contains the physical channels and power up states to set. Each element of the array contains a physical channel and the power up state to set for that physical channel. You can set current power up states only for physical channels that support current output. physical channel — physical channel is the physical channel to modify. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels for devices installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. value — value is the power up state to set for the physical channel specified with the physical channel input. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. device out — device out is the name of the device the operation applied to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |

| physical channel — physical channel is the physical channel to modify. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels for devices installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. value — value is the power up state to set for the physical channel specified with the physical channel input. |

## TOPIC 02070: DAQmx Set Power Up States (Digital Logic Family) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-set-power-up-states-digital-logic-family-vi.html`

- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Sets the digital logic family to use when the device powers up. icon Inputs/Outputs cdaqmxscale.png device in device in is the name as configured in MAX of the device to which this operation applies. A DAQmx device name constant lists all devices installed in the system. ci32.png logic family logic

### DAQmx Set Power Up States (Digital Logic Family) VI

Sets the digital logic family to use when the device powers up.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-set-power-up-states-digital-logic-family-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| device in — device in is the name as configured in MAX of the device to which this operation applies. A DAQmx device name constant lists all devices installed in the system. logic family — logic family specifies the logic family to set the device to when it powers up. A logic family corresponds to voltage thresholds that are compatible with a group of voltage standards. Refer to device documentation for information on the logic high and logic low voltages for these logic families. 2.5 V (14620) Compatible with CMOS signals. 3.3 V (14621) Compatible with LVTTL and LVCMOS signals. 5.0 V (14619) Compatible with TTL and CMOS signals. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. device out — device out is the name of the device the operation applied to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| 2.5 V (14620) | Compatible with CMOS signals. |
| 3.3 V (14621) | Compatible with LVTTL and LVCMOS signals. |
| 5.0 V (14619) | Compatible with TTL and CMOS signals. |

DAQmx Set Power Up States VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`

A DAQmx device name constant lists all devices installed in the system. c

### DAQmx Set Power Up States (Digital Pull Up Pull Down) VI

Sets the resistor level to pull up or pull down for lines when they are in tristate logic.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-set-power-up-states-digital-pull-up-pull-down-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| device in — device in is the name as configured in MAX of the device to which this operation applies. A DAQmx device name constant lists all devices installed in the system. pull up pull down levels — pull up pull down levels specifies the tristate logic level (either pull up or pull down) for each line. physical channel — physical channel is the digital line or port to modify. You cannot modify dedicated digital input lines. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all lines and ports for devices installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of digital lines or ports to this input. If you have an array of lines or ports, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. power up state — power up state is the pull up pull down level to set for the physical channel specified with the physical channel input. Pull-up (15950) High logic. Pull-down (15951) Low logic. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. device out — device out is the name of the device the operation applied to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| physical channel — physical channel is the digital line or port to modify. You cannot modify dedicated digital input lines. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all lines and ports for devices installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of digital lines or ports to this input. If you have an array of lines or ports, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. power up state — power up state is the pull up pull down level to set for the physical channel specified with the physical channel input. Pull-up (15950) High logic. Pull-down (15951) Low logic. |  |
| Pull-up (15950) | High logic. |
| Pull-down (15951) | Low logic. |
