### DAQmx Set Power Up States (Digital) VI

Updates power up states for digital physical channels.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-set-power-up-states-digital-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| device in — device in is the name as configured in MAX of the device to which this operation applies. A DAQmx device name constant lists all devices installed in the system. power up states — power up states contains the physical channels and power up states to set. Each element of the array contains a physical channel and the power up state to set for that physical channel. physical channel — physical channel is the digital line or port to modify. You cannot modify dedicated digital input lines. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all lines and ports for devices installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of digital lines or ports to this input. If you have an array of lines or ports, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. power up state — power up state is the power up state to set for the physical channel specified with the physical channel input. High (10192) High logic. Low (10214) Low logic. Tristate (10310) High-impedance state. You can set this power up state only on devices with bidirectional ports, and you can set it only on entire ports. You cannot set this power up state on dedicated digital output lines. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. device out — device out is the name of the device the operation applied to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| physical channel — physical channel is the digital line or port to modify. You cannot modify dedicated digital input lines. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all lines and ports for devices installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of digital lines or ports to this input. If you have an array of lines or ports, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. power up state — power up state is the power up state to set for the physical channel specified with the physical channel input. High (10192) High logic. Low (10214) Low logic. Tristate (10310) High-impedance state. You can set this power up state only on devices with bidirectional ports, and you can set it only on entire ports. You cannot set this power up state on dedicated digital output lines. |  |
| High (10192) | High logic. |
| Low (10214) | Low logic. |
| Tristate (10310) | High-impedance state. You can set this power up state only on devices with bidirectional ports, and you can set it only on entire ports. You cannot set this power up state on dedicated digital output lines. |

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-set-power-up-states-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02073: DAQmx Set Power Up States VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-set-power-up-states-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-set-power-up-states-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Updates the states to which to set physical channels on a device when the device powers up or when you reset the device. A device stores power up states in non-volatile memory that you can write to only a limited number of times. Therefore, you should use this VI as infrequently as possible. This VI

### DAQmx Set Power Up States VI

Updates the states to which to set physical channels on a device when the device powers up or when you reset the device. 
 A device stores power up states in non-volatile memory that you can write to only a limited number of times. 
 Therefore, you should use this VI as infrequently as possible. 
 This VI writes to the non-volatile memory only if a setting you request is different from the one currently stored. 
 This VI writes power up states in the sequential order of the power up states array. 
 Therefore, any physical channels with multiple entries in that array use the highest array index.

The instances of this [polymorphic VI](/csh?context=lvcore_lvhelp_using_polymorphic_vis) specify whether to set power up states for analog or digital physical channels.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-set-power-up-states-vi.png']

- [DAQmx Set Power Up States (Digital) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-set-power-up-states-digital-vi.html) Updates power up states for digital physical channels.
- [DAQmx Set Power Up States (Digital Logic Family) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-set-power-up-states-digital-logic-family-vi.html) Sets the digital logic family to use when the device powers up.
- [DAQmx Set Power Up States (Digital Pull Up Pull Down) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-set-power-up-states-digital-pull-up-pull-down-vi.html) Sets the resistor level to pull up or pull down for lines when they are in tristate logic.
- [DAQmx Set Power Up States (Analog) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-set-power-up-states-analog-vi.html) (Deprecated) Updates power up states for analog physical channels.
- [DAQmx Set Power Up States (Analog With Output Type) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-set-power-up-states-analog-with-output-type-vi.html) Updates power up states for analog physical channels.

## TOPIC 02074: DAQmx System Property Node

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-system-property-node-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/system-llb/daqmx-system-property-node-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: A Property Node with the DAQmx System class preselected. Right-click the Property Node and choose Select Filter from the shortcut menu to make the Property Node show only the properties supported by a particular device installed in the system or supported by all the devices installed in the system.

### DAQmx System Property Node

A Property Node with the [DAQmx System](/csh?context=nidaqmx_nidaq_daqmx_system_p) class preselected. Right-click the Property Node and choose **Select Filter** from the shortcut menu to make the Property Node show only the properties supported by a particular device installed in the system or supported by all the devices installed in the system.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-system-property-node-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| error in (no error) — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. Global Channels — Global Channels is an example of a property you want to get (read) or set (write). |
| --- |

- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Writes the supplied data and format code to the EEPROM connected to the specified ID pin. icon Inputs/Outputs cu8.png format code format code specifies the type and structure of the data being written. User to define the meaning of the format code. Detail of format codes are as follow: 0x01 UTF-8 St

### DAQmx Write ID Pin Memory VI

Writes the supplied data and format code to the EEPROM connected to the specified ID pin.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-write-id-pin-memory-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| format code — format code specifies the type and structure of the data being written. User to define the meaning of the format code. Detail of format codes are as follow: 0x01 UTF-8 String 0x10..0x7F Available for users other reserved device in — device in is the name as configured in MAX of the device to which this operation applies. A DAQmx device name constant lists all devices installed in the system. id pin name — id pin name is the name of the ID Pin to access (ex: id0). data — data is the 1D array of 8-bit unsigned integers to write to the memory error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. device out — device out is the name of the device the operation applied to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| 0x01 | UTF-8 String |
| 0x10..0x7F | Available for users |
| other | reserved |

Parent topic:

DAQmx System Setup

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/task-llb/daqmx-clear-task-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02077: DAQmx Clear Task VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/task-llb/daqmx-clear-task-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/task-llb/daqmx-clear-task-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Clears the task. Before clearing, this VI aborts the task, if necessary, and releases any resources the task reserved. You cannot use a task after you clear it unless you recreate the task. If you use the DAQmx Create Task VI or the DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI within a loop, use this VI within t

### DAQmx Clear Task VI

Clears the [task](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_tasksnidaqmx). Before clearing, this VI aborts the task, if necessary, and releases any resources the task reserved. You cannot use a task after you clear it unless you recreate the task.

If you use the [DAQmx Create Task](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_mxcreatetask) VI or the [DAQmx Create Virtual Channel](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_mxcreatechannel) VI within a loop, use this VI within the loop after you are finished with the task to avoid allocating unnecessary memory. Refer to [Task Creation and Destruction](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_taskcreationanddestruction) for more information about when to use this VI.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-clear-task-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task in — task in is the name of the task to clear. Unlike the task/channels in input on some VIs, you cannot wire a virtual channel to this input. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. error out — error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
| --- |

Parent topic:

DAQmx - Data Acquisition

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/task-llb/daqmx-configure-logging-tdms-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02078: DAQmx Configure Logging (TDMS) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/task-llb/daqmx-configure-logging-tdms-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/task-llb/daqmx-configure-logging-tdms-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Configures TDMS file logging for the task. To maximize performance and to minimize file size, DAQmx TDMS files store raw data and scaling information separately, and do not need defragmentation. Attempting to pass a DAQmx TDMS file to the TDMS Defragment VI results in an error. icon Inputs/Outputs c

### DAQmx Configure Logging (TDMS) VI

Configures [TDMS file logging](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_datalogging) for the task.

Note

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-configure-logging-tdms-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| operation — operation specifies how to open the TDMS file. Open (10437) Open an existing TDMS file, and append data to that file. If the file does not exist, NI-DAQmx returns an error. Open or Create (15846) Open an existing TDMS file, and append data to that file. If the file does not exist, NI-DAQmx creates a new TDMS file. Create or Replace (15847) Create a new TDMS file, or replace an existing TDMS file. Create (15848) Create a new TDMS file. If the file already exists, NI-DAQmx returns an error. task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. file path — file path specifies the path to the TDMS file to which you want to log data. group name — group name specifies the name of the group to create within the TDMS file for data from this task. If you append data to an existing file and the specified group already exists, NI-DAQmx appends a number symbol and a number to the group name, incrementing that number until finding a group name that does not exist. For example, if you specify a group name of Voltage Task, and that group already exists, NI-DAQmx assigns the group name Voltage Task #1, then Voltage Task #2. If you do not specify a group name, NI-DAQmx uses the name of the task. For digital input tasks, NI-DAQmx creates a group for each virtual channel in the task. For each created group, NI-DAQmx appends a hyphen and the virtual channel name to the group name. logging mode — logging mode specifies whether to enable logging and whether to allow reading data while logging. Log mode allows for the best performance. However, you cannot read data while logging if you specify this mode. If you want to read data while logging, specify Log and Read mode. Off (10231) Disable logging for the task. Log (15844) Enable logging for the task. You cannot read data using DAQmx Read when using this mode. If you require access to the data, read from the TDMS file. Log and Read (15842) Enable both logging and reading data for the task. You must use DAQmx Read to read samples for NI-DAQmx to stream them to disk. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Open (10437) | Open an existing TDMS file, and append data to that file. If the file does not exist, NI-DAQmx returns an error. |
| Open or Create (15846) | Open an existing TDMS file, and append data to that file. If the file does not exist, NI-DAQmx creates a new TDMS file. |
| Create or Replace (15847) | Create a new TDMS file, or replace an existing TDMS file. |
| Create (15848) | Create a new TDMS file. If the file already exists, NI-DAQmx returns an error. |
| Off (10231) | Disable logging for the task. |
| Log (15844) | Enable logging for the task. You cannot read data using DAQmx Read when using this mode. If you require access to the data, read from the TDMS file. |
| Log and Read (15842) | Enable both logging and reading data for the task. You must use DAQmx Read to read samples for NI-DAQmx to stream them to disk. |

To maximize performance and to minimize file size, DAQmx TDMS files store raw data and scaling information separately, and do not need defrag

The DAQmx Configure Logging properties include additional configuration options for configuring logging operations.

Note

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-configure-logging-vi.png']

## TOPIC 02080: DAQmx Configure Watchdog Expiration States (Analog Output) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/task-llb/daqmx-configure-watchdog-expiration-states-analog-output-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/task-llb/daqmx-configure-watchdog-expiration-states-analog-output-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Configures the expiration states for an analog watchdog timer task. The DAQmx Watchdog properties contain additional watchdog timer configuration options. icon Inputs/Outputs cgenclassrntag.png task in task in is the name of the task to clear. Unlike the task/channels in input on some VIs, you canno

### DAQmx Configure Watchdog Expiration States (Analog Output) VI

Configures the expiration states for an analog watchdog timer task.

The [DAQmx Watchdog](/csh?context=nidaqmx_nidaq_daqmx_watchdog_p) properties contain additional watchdog timer configuration options.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-configure-watchdog-expiration-states-analog-output-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task in — task in is the name of the task to clear. Unlike the task/channels in input on some VIs, you cannot wire a virtual channel to this input. expiration states — expiration states contains the states to which to set analog physical channels when the watchdog timer expires. Each element of the array contains an analog physical channel name, the corresponding expiration state, and the output type for that analog physical channel. The units of expiration state must be specified in volts for an analog output voltage expiration state, or amps for an analog output current expiration state. physical channel — physical channel is the analog output channel to modify. You cannot modify dedicated analog input lines. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all lines and ports for devices installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of digital lines or ports to this input. If you have an array of lines or ports, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. expiration state — expiration state specifies the value to set the channel to upon expiration. output type — output type specifies the output type of the physical channel. Voltage (10322) Voltage output. Current (10134) Current output. No Change (10160) Expiration does not affect the port. Do not change the state of any lines in the port, and do not lock the port. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function completes execution. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| physical channel — physical channel is the analog output channel to modify. You cannot modify dedicated analog input lines. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all lines and ports for devices installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of digital lines or ports to this input. If you have an array of lines or ports, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. expiration state — expiration state specifies the value to set the channel to upon expiration. output type — output type specifies the output type of the physical channel. Voltage (10322) Voltage output. Current (10134) Current output. No Change (10160) Expiration does not affect the port. Do not change the state of any lines in the port, and do not lock the port. |  |
| Voltage (10322) | Voltage output. |
| Current (10134) | Current output. |
| No Change (10160) | Expiration does not affect the port. Do not change the state of any lines in the port, and do not lock the port. |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Configure Watchdog Expiration States VI

## TOPIC 02081: DAQmx Configure Watchdog Expiration States (Counter Output) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/task-llb/daqmx-configure-watchdog-expiration-states-counter-output-vi.html`

- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Configures the expiration states for a counter watchdog timer task. The DAQmx Watchdog properties contain additional watchdog timer configuration options. icon Inputs/Outputs cgenclassrntag.png task in task in is the name of the task to clear. Unlike the task/channels in input on some VIs, you canno

### DAQmx Configure Watchdog Expiration States (Counter Output) VI

Configures the expiration states for a counter watchdog timer task.

The [DAQmx Watchdog](/csh?context=nidaqmx_nidaq_daqmx_watchdog_p) properties contain additional watchdog timer configuration options.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-configure-watchdog-expiration-states-counter-output-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task in — task in is the name of the task to clear. Unlike the task/channels in input on some VIs, you cannot wire a virtual channel to this input. expiration states — expiration states contains the states to which to set counter physical channels when the watchdog timer expires. Each element of the array contains a counter physical channel name and the corresponding state for that counter physical channel. physical channel — physical channel is the counter output channel to modify. You cannot modify dedicated counter input lines. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all lines and ports for devices installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of digital lines or ports to this input. If you have an array of lines or ports, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. expiration state — expiration state specifies the value to set the channel to upon expiration. Low (10214) Low logic. High (10192) High logic. No Change (10160) Expiration does not affect the state of the counter output. The channels retain their states at the time of the watchdog timer expiration, and no further counter generation runs. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function completes execution. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| physical channel — physical channel is the counter output channel to modify. You cannot modify dedicated counter input lines. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all lines and ports for devices installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of digital lines or ports to this input. If you have an array of lines or ports, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. expiration state — expiration state specifies the value to set the channel to upon expiration. Low (10214) Low logic. High (10192) High logic. No Change (10160) Expiration does not affect the state of the counter output. The channels retain their states at the time of the watchdog timer expiration, and no further counter generation runs. |  |
| Low (10214) | Low logic. |
| High (10192) | High logic. |
| No Change (10160) | Expiration does not affect the state of the counter output. The channels retain their states at the time of the watchdog timer expiration, and no further counter generation runs. |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Configure Watchdog Expiration States VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`

- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Configures the expiration states for a digital watchdog timer task. The DAQmx Watchdog properties contain additional watchdog timer configuration options. icon Inputs/Outputs cgenclassrntag.png task in task in is the name of the task to clear. Unlike the task/channels in input on some VIs, you canno

### DAQmx Configure Watchdog Expiration States (Digital Output) VI

Configures the expiration states for a digital watchdog timer task.

The [DAQmx Watchdog](/csh?context=nidaqmx_nidaq_daqmx_watchdog_p) properties contain additional watchdog timer configuration options.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-configure-watchdog-expiration-states-digital-output-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task in — task in is the name of the task to clear. Unlike the task/channels in input on some VIs, you cannot wire a virtual channel to this input. expiration states — expiration states contains the states to which to set digital physical channels when the watchdog timer expires. Each element of the array contains a digital physical channel name and the corresponding state for that digital physical channel. physical channel — physical channel is the digital line or port to modify. You cannot modify dedicated digital input lines. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all lines and ports for devices installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of digital lines or ports to this input. If you have an array of lines or ports, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. expiration state — expiration state specifies the state to which to set the digital physical channel when the watchdog timer expires. High (10192) High logic. Low (10214) Low logic. No Change (10160) Expiration does not affect the port. Do not change the state of any lines in the port, and do not lock the port. For example, if a line is high when the timer expires, that line stays high, and you can write new values to the line. You can select this value only for entire ports. Tristate (10310) High-impedance state. You can select this state only on devices with bidirectional ports, and you can select it only for entire ports. You cannot select this state for dedicated digital output lines. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function completes execution. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| physical channel — physical channel is the digital line or port to modify. You cannot modify dedicated digital input lines. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all lines and ports for devices installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of digital lines or ports to this input. If you have an array of lines or ports, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. expiration state — expiration state specifies the state to which to set the digital physical channel when the watchdog timer expires. High (10192) High logic. Low (10214) Low logic. No Change (10160) Expiration does not affect the port. Do not change the state of any lines in the port, and do not lock the port. For example, if a line is high when the timer expires, that line stays high, and you can write new values to the line. You can select this value only for entire ports. Tristate (10310) High-impedance state. You can select this state only on devices with bidirectional ports, and you can select it only for entire ports. You cannot select this state for dedicated digital output lines. |  |
| High (10192) | High logic. |
| Low (10214) | Low logic. |
| No Change (10160) | Expiration does not affect the port. Do not change the state of any lines in the port, and do not lock the port. For example, if a line is high when the timer expires, that line stays high, and you can write new values to the line. You can select this value only for entire ports. |
| Tristate (10310) | High-impedance state. You can select this state only on devices with bidirectional ports, and you can select it only for entire ports. You cannot select this state for dedicated digital output lines. |

## TOPIC 02084: DAQmx Control Task VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/task-llb/daqmx-control-task-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/task-llb/daqmx-control-task-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Alters the state of a task according to the action you specify. If error in indicates that an error occurred previously, this VI executes normally if action is unreserve or abort. icon Inputs/Outputs cgenclassrntag.png task/channels in task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual ch

### DAQmx Control Task VI

Alters the [state](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_taskstatemodel) of a task according to the **action** you specify.

If **error in** indicates that an error occurred previously, this VI executes normally if **action** is **unreserve** or **abort**.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-control-task-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. action — action specifies how to alter the task state. verify (2) Verifies that all task parameters are valid for the hardware. reserve (4) Reserves the hardware resources needed for the task. No other tasks can reserve these same resources. commit (3) Programs the hardware as much as possible according to the task configuration. unreserve (5) Releases all previously reserved resources. abort (6) Aborts execution of the task. Aborting a task immediately terminates the currently active operation, such as a read or a write. Aborting a task puts the task into an unstable but recoverable state. To recover the task, use DAQmx Start to restart the task or use DAQmx Stop to reset the task without starting it. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| verify (2) | Verifies that all task parameters are valid for the hardware. |
| reserve (4) | Reserves the hardware resources needed for the task. No other tasks can reserve these same resources. |
| commit (3) | Programs the hardware as much as possible according to the task configuration. |
| unreserve (5) | Releases all previously reserved resources. |
| abort (6) | Aborts execution of the task. Aborting a task immediately terminates the currently active operation, such as a read or a write. Aborting a task puts the task into an unstable but recoverable state. To recover the task, use DAQmx Start to restart the task or use DAQmx Stop to reset the task without starting it. |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Advanced Task Options

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/task-llb/daqmx-control-watchdog-task-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02085: DAQmx Control Watchdog Task VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/task-llb/daqmx-control-watchdog-task-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/task-llb/daqmx-control-watchdog-task-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Controls the watchdog timer task according to the action you specify. This VI does not program the watchdog timer on a real-time controller. Use the Real-Time Watchdog VIs to program the watchdog timer on a real-time controller. icon Inputs/Outputs cgenclassrntag.png task in task in is the name of t

### DAQmx Control Watchdog Task VI

Controls the watchdog timer task according to the action you specify. This VI does not program the watchdog timer on a real-time controller. Use the Real-Time Watchdog VIs to program the watchdog timer on a real-time controller.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-control-watchdog-task-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task in — task in is the name of the task to which this operation applies. action — action specifies how to control the watchdog timer task. clear expiration (1) Unlock a device whose watchdog timer expired. reset timer (0) Reset the internal timer. You must continually reset the internal timer to prevent it from timing out and locking the device. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function completes execution. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| clear expiration (1) | Unlock a device whose watchdog timer expired. |
| reset timer (0) | Reset the internal timer. You must continually reset the internal timer to prevent it from timing out and locking the device. |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Watchdog

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/task-llb/daqmx-is-task-done-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02086: DAQmx Is Task Done VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/task-llb/daqmx-is-task-done-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/task-llb/daqmx-is-task-done-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Queries the status of the task and indicates if it completed execution. Use this VI to ensure that the specified operation is complete before you stop the task. icon Inputs/Outputs cgenclassrntag.png task/channels in task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the

### DAQmx Is Task Done VI

Queries the status of the task and indicates if it completed execution. Use this VI to [ensure that the specified operation is complete](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_istaskdone) before you stop the task.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-is-task-done-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. task done? — task done? indicates if the measurement or generation completed. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Advanced Task Options

- content_type: `reference`

If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automaticall

### DAQmx Start New File VI

Starts a [new TDMS file](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_multiplefiles) the next time data is written to disk.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-start-new-file-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. file path — file path specifies the path to the TDMS file to which you want to log data. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/task-llb/daqmx-start-task-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02088: DAQmx Start Task VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/task-llb/daqmx-start-task-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/task-llb/daqmx-start-task-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Transitions the task to the running state to begin the measurement or generation. Using this VI is required for some applications and is optional for others. If you do not use this VI, a measurement task starts automatically when the DAQmx Read VI runs. The autostart input of the DAQmx Write VI dete

### DAQmx Start Task VI

Transitions the [task](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_tasksnidaqmx) to the running [state](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_taskstatemodel) to begin the measurement or generation. [Using this VI](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_startfunction) is required for some applications and is optional for others.

If you do not use this VI, a measurement task starts automatically when the [DAQmx Read](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_mxread) VI runs. The **autostart** input of the [DAQmx Write](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_mxwrite) VI determines if a generation task starts automatically when the DAQmx Write VI runs.

If you do not use the DAQmx Start Task VI and the [DAQmx Stop Task](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_mxstop) VI when you use the DAQmx Read VI or the DAQmx Write VI multiple times, such as in a loop, the task starts and stops repeatedly. Starting and stopping a task repeatedly reduces the performance of the application.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-start-task-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

Parent topic:

DAQmx - Data Acquisition

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/task-llb/daqmx-stop-task-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02089: DAQmx Stop Task VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/task-llb/daqmx-stop-task-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/task-llb/daqmx-stop-task-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Stops the task and returns it to the state the task was in before the DAQmx Start Task VI ran or the DAQmx Write VI ran with the autostart input set to TRUE. If you do not use the DAQmx Start Task VI and the DAQmx Stop Task VI when you use the DAQmx Read VI or the DAQmx Write VI multiple times, such

### DAQmx Stop Task VI

Stops the [task](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_tasksnidaqmx) and returns it to the [state](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_taskstatemodel) the task was in before the [DAQmx Start Task](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_mxstart) VI ran or the [DAQmx Write](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_mxwrite) VI ran with the **autostart** input set to TRUE.

If you do not use the DAQmx Start Task VI and the DAQmx Stop Task VI when you use the [DAQmx Read](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_mxread) VI or the [DAQmx Write](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_mxwrite) VI multiple times, such as in a loop, the task starts and stops repeatedly. Starting and stopping a task repeatedly reduces the performance of the application.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-stop-task-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. error out — error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |
| --- |

Parent topic:

DAQmx - Data Acquisition

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/task-llb/daqmx-task-property-node-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02090: DAQmx Task Property Node

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/task-llb/daqmx-task-property-node-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/task-llb/daqmx-task-property-node-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: A Property Node with the DAQmx Task class preselected. Right-click the Property Node and choose Select Filter from the shortcut menu to make the Property Node show only the properties supported by a particular device installed in the system or supported by all the devices installed in the system. ic

### DAQmx Task Property Node

A Property Node with the [DAQmx Task](/csh?context=nidaqmx_nidaq_daqmx_task_p) class preselected. Right-click the Property Node and choose **Select Filter** from the shortcut menu to make the Property Node show only the properties supported by a particular device installed in the system or supported by all the devices installed in the system.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-task-property-node-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. error in (no error) — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. Name — Name is an example of a property you want to get (read) or set (write). |
| --- |

DAQmx Advanced Task Options

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/task-llb/daqmx-wait-for-valid-timestamp-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02091: DAQmx Wait for Valid Timestamp VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/task-llb/daqmx-wait-for-valid-timestamp-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/task-llb/daqmx-wait-for-valid-timestamp-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Wait until the specified timestamp has a value. Use this VI to ensure the timestamp has a valid value to prevent an error when querying a timestamp value. icon Inputs/Outputs cgenclassrntag.png task/channels in task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the opera

### DAQmx Wait for Valid Timestamp VI

Wait until the specified timestamp has a value. Use this VI to ensure the timestamp has a valid value to prevent an error when querying a timestamp value.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-wait-for-valid-timestamp-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. timestamp — timestamp specifies the timestamp type to wait on. Start Trigger (12491) Start Trigger Timestamp. Reference Trigger (12490) Reference Trigger Timestamp. Arm Start Trigger (14641) Arm Start Trigger Timestamp. First Sample (16130) First Sample Timestamp. timeout — timeout (sec) specifies the maximum amount of time in seconds to wait for a valid timestamp. This VI returns an error if the time elapses. The default is 10. If you set timeout (sec) to -1, the VI waits indefinitely. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. timestamp value — error out — error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Start Trigger (12491) | Start Trigger Timestamp. |
| Reference Trigger (12490) | Reference Trigger Timestamp. |
| Arm Start Trigger (14641) | Arm Start Trigger Timestamp. |
| First Sample (16130) | First Sample Timestamp. |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Advanced Task Options

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/task-llb/daqmx-wait-until-done-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02092: DAQmx Wait Until Done VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/task-llb/daqmx-wait-until-done-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/task-llb/daqmx-wait-until-done-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Waits for the measurement or generation to complete. Use this VI to ensure that the specified operation is complete before you stop the task. icon Inputs/Outputs cgenclassrntag.png task/channels in task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies.

### DAQmx Wait Until Done VI

Waits for the measurement or generation to complete. Use this VI to [ensure that the specified operation is complete](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_waituntildone) before you stop the task.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-wait-until-done-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. timeout (sec) — timeout (sec) specifies the maximum amount of time in seconds to wait for the measurement or generation to complete. This VI returns an error if the time elapses. The default is 10. If you set timeout (sec) to -1, the VI waits indefinitely. If you set timeout (sec) to 0, the VI checks once and returns an error if the measurement or generation is not done. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

Parent topic:

DAQmx - Data Acquisition

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/task-llb/daqmx-watchdog-property-node-vi.html language=enus -->

| task — task is the name of the task to which this operation applies. error in (no error) — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. Timeout — Timeout is an example of a property you want to get (read) or set (write). task out — task is a reference to the task after this function completes execution. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |

## TOPIC 02094: DAQmx Real-Time Property Node

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-real-time-property-node-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-real-time-property-node-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: A Property Node with the DAQmx Real-Time class preselected. Right-click the Property Node and choose Select Filter from the shortcut menu to make the Property Node show only the properties supported by a particular device installed in the system or supported by all the devices installed in the syste

### DAQmx Real-Time Property Node

A Property Node with the [DAQmx Real-Time](/csh?context=nidaqmx_nidaq_daqmx_real-time_p) class preselected. Right-click the Property Node and choose **Select Filter** from the shortcut menu to make the Property Node show only the properties supported by a particular device installed in the system or supported by all the devices installed in the system.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-real-time-property-node-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. error in (no error) — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. Convert Late Errors To Warnings — Convert Late Errors To Warnings is an example of a property you want to get (read) or set (write). task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Real-Time

Parent topic:

DAQmx Constants & Property Nodes

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-timing-burst-export-clock-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02095: DAQmx Timing (Burst Export Clock) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-timing-burst-export-clock-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-timing-burst-export-clock-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Configures when the DAQ device transfers data to a peripheral device, using the onboard Sample Clock of the DAQ device to control burst handshake timing and exporting that clock for use by the peripheral device. icon Inputs/Outputs ci32.png samples per channel samples per channel specifies the numbe

### DAQmx Timing (Burst Export Clock) VI

Configures when the DAQ device transfers data to a peripheral device, using the onboard Sample Clock of the DAQ device to control [burst handshake timing](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_smpletimingtype) and exporting that clock for use by the peripheral device.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-timing-burst-export-clock-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| samples per channel — samples per channel specifies the number of samples to acquire or generate for each channel in the task if sample mode is Finite Samples. If sample mode is Continuous Samples, NI-DAQmx uses this value to determine the buffer size. This VI returns an error if the specified value is negative. sample mode — sample mode specifies if the task acquires or generates samples continuously or if it acquires or generates a finite number of samples. Continuous Samples (10123) Acquire or generate samples until the DAQmx Stop Task VI runs. Finite Samples (10178) Acquire or generate a finite number of samples. Hardware Timed Single Point (12522) Acquire or generate samples continuously using hardware timing without a buffer. Hardware timed single point sample mode is supported only for the sample clock and change detection timing types. task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. sample clock rate — sample clock rate specifies in hertz the rate of the Sample Clock. sample clock output terminal — sample clock output terminal specifies the terminal to which to export the Sample Clock. sample clock pulse polarity — sample clock pulse polarity specifies the polarity of the exported Sample Clock. Active High (10095) Active high. Active Low (10096) Active low. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. pause when — pause when specifies whether the task pauses while the trigger signal is high or low. High (10192) Pause the task while the trigger signal is high. Low (10214) Pause the task while the trigger signal is low. ready for transfer event active level — ready for transfer event active level specifies the polarity of the Ready for Transfer Event. Active High (10095) Active high. Active Low (10096) Active low. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Continuous Samples (10123) | Acquire or generate samples until the DAQmx Stop Task VI runs. |
| Finite Samples (10178) | Acquire or generate a finite number of samples. |
| Hardware Timed Single Point (12522) | Acquire or generate samples continuously using hardware timing without a buffer. Hardware timed single point sample mode is supported only for the sample clock and change detection timing types. |
| Active High (10095) | Active high. |
| Active Low (10096) | Active low. |
| High (10192) | Pause the task while the trigger signal is high. |
| Low (10214) | Pause the task while the trigger signal is low. |
| Active High (10095) | Active high. |
| Active Low (10096) | Active low. |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Timing VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-timing-burst-import-clock-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02096: DAQmx Timing (Burst Import Clock) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-timing-burst-import-clock-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-timing-burst-import-clock-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Configures when the DAQ device transfers data to a peripheral device, using an imported sample clock to control burst handshake timing. icon Inputs/Outputs ci32.png samples per channel samples per channel specifies the number of samples to acquire or generate for each channel in the task if sample m

### DAQmx Timing (Burst Import Clock) VI

Configures when the DAQ device transfers data to a peripheral device, using an imported sample clock to control [burst handshake timing](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_smpletimingtype).

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-timing-burst-import-clock-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| samples per channel — samples per channel specifies the number of samples to acquire or generate for each channel in the task if sample mode is Finite Samples. If sample mode is Continuous Samples, NI-DAQmx uses this value to determine the buffer size. This VI returns an error if the specified value is negative. sample mode — sample mode specifies if the task acquires or generates samples continuously or if it acquires or generates a finite number of samples. Continuous Samples (10123) Acquire or generate samples until the DAQmx Stop Task VI runs. Finite Samples (10178) Acquire or generate a finite number of samples. Hardware Timed Single Point (12522) Acquire or generate samples continuously using hardware timing without a buffer. Hardware timed single point sample mode is supported only for the sample clock and change detection timing types. task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. sample clock rate — sample clock rate specifies in hertz the rate of the Sample Clock. sample clock source — sample clock source specifies the source terminal of the Sample Clock. Leave this input unwired to use the default onboard clock of the device. sample clock active edge — sample clock active edge specifies on which edges of Sample Clock pulses to acquire or generate samples. Falling (10171) Acquire or generate samples on falling edges of the Sample Clock. Rising (10280) Acquire or generate samples on rising edges of the Sample Clock. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. pause when — pause when specifies whether the task pauses while the trigger signal is high or low. High (10192) Pause the task while the trigger signal is high. Low (10214) Pause the task while the trigger signal is low. ready for transfer event active level — ready for transfer event active level specifies the polarity of the Ready for Transfer Event. Active High (10095) Active high. Active Low (10096) Active low. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Continuous Samples (10123) | Acquire or generate samples until the DAQmx Stop Task VI runs. |
| Finite Samples (10178) | Acquire or generate a finite number of samples. |
| Hardware Timed Single Point (12522) | Acquire or generate samples continuously using hardware timing without a buffer. Hardware timed single point sample mode is supported only for the sample clock and change detection timing types. |
| Falling (10171) | Acquire or generate samples on falling edges of the Sample Clock. |
| Rising (10280) | Acquire or generate samples on rising edges of the Sample Clock. |
| High (10192) | Pause the task while the trigger signal is high. |
| Low (10214) | Pause the task while the trigger signal is low. |
| Active High (10095) | Active high. |
| Active Low (10096) | Active low. |

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-timing-change-detection-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02097: DAQmx Timing (Change Detection) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-timing-change-detection-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-timing-change-detection-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Configures the task to acquire samples on the rising and/or falling edges of the lines or ports you specify. To detect both rising and falling edges on a line or port, wire the name of that line or port to both rising edge physical channels and falling edge physical channels. icon Inputs/Outputs ci3

### DAQmx Timing (Change Detection) VI

Configures the task to acquire samples on the rising and/or falling edges of the lines or ports you specify. To detect both rising and falling edges on a line or port, wire the name of that line or port to both **rising edge physical channels** and **falling edge physical channels**.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-timing-change-detection-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| samples per channel — samples per channel specifies the number of samples to acquire from each channel in the task if sample mode is Finite Samples. This VI returns an error if the specified value is negative. sample mode — sample mode specifies if the task acquires samples continuously or if it acquires a finite number of samples. Continuous Samples (10123) Acquire samples until the DAQmx Stop Task VI runs. Finite Samples (10178) Acquire a finite number of samples. Hardware Timed Single Point (12522) Acquire samples continuously using hardware timing without a buffer. Hardware timed single point sample mode is supported only for the sample clock and change detection timing types. task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. rising edge physical channels — rising edge physical channels specifies the names of the digital lines or ports on which to detect rising edges. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all lines and ports for devices installed in your system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of digital lines or ports to this input. falling edge physical channels — falling edge physical channels specifies the names of the digital lines or ports on which to detect falling edges. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all lines and ports for devices installed in your system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of digital lines or ports to this input. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Continuous Samples (10123) | Acquire samples until the DAQmx Stop Task VI runs. |
| Finite Samples (10178) | Acquire a finite number of samples. |
| Hardware Timed Single Point (12522) | Acquire samples continuously using hardware timing without a buffer. Hardware timed single point sample mode is supported only for the sample clock and change detection timing types. |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Timing VI

## TOPIC 02098: DAQmx Timing (Handshaking) VI
