- source_description: Determines the number of digital samples to acquire or generate using digital handshaking between the device and a peripheral device. icon Inputs/Outputs ci32.png samples per channel samples per channel specifies the number of samples to acquire or generate for each channel in the task if sample mod

### DAQmx Timing (Handshaking) VI

Determines the number of digital samples to acquire or generate using [digital handshaking](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_handshaking) between the device and a peripheral device.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-timing-handshaking-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| samples per channel — samples per channel specifies the number of samples to acquire or generate for each channel in the task if sample mode is Finite Samples. If sample mode is Continuous Samples, NI-DAQmx uses this value to determine the buffer size. This VI returns an error if the specified value is negative. sample mode — sample mode specifies if the task acquires or generates samples continuously or if it acquires or generates a finite number of samples. Continuous Samples (10123) Acquire or generate samples until the DAQmx Stop Task VI runs. Finite Samples (10178) Acquire or generate a finite number of samples. Hardware Timed Single Point (12522) Acquire or generate samples continuously using hardware timing without a buffer. Hardware timed single point sample mode is supported only for the sample clock and change detection timing types. task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Continuous Samples (10123) | Acquire or generate samples until the DAQmx Stop Task VI runs. |
| Finite Samples (10178) | Acquire or generate a finite number of samples. |
| Hardware Timed Single Point (12522) | Acquire or generate samples continuously using hardware timing without a buffer. Hardware timed single point sample mode is supported only for the sample clock and change detection timing types. |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Timing VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-timing-implicit-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02099: DAQmx Timing (Implicit) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-timing-implicit-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-timing-implicit-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Sets only the number of samples to acquire or generate without specifying timing. Typically, you should use this instance when the task does not require sample timing, such as tasks that use counters for buffered frequency measurement, buffered period measurement, or pulse train generation. For fini

### DAQmx Timing (Implicit) VI

Sets only the number of samples to acquire or generate without specifying timing. Typically, you should use this instance when the task does not require sample timing, such as tasks that use counters for buffered frequency measurement, buffered period measurement, or pulse train generation. For finite counter output tasks, **samples per channel** is the number of pulses to generate.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-timing-implicit-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| samples per channel — samples per channel specifies the number of samples to acquire or generate for each channel in the task if sample mode is Finite Samples. If sample mode is Continuous Samples, NI-DAQmx uses this value to determine the buffer size. This VI returns an error if the specified value is negative. sample mode — sample mode specifies if the task acquires or generates samples continuously or if it acquires or generates a finite number of samples. Continuous Samples (10123) Acquire or generate samples until the DAQmx Stop Task VI runs. Finite Samples (10178) Acquire or generate a finite number of samples. Hardware Timed Single Point (12522) Acquire or generate samples continuously using hardware timing without a buffer. Hardware timed single point sample mode is supported only for the sample clock and change detection timing types. task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Continuous Samples (10123) | Acquire or generate samples until the DAQmx Stop Task VI runs. |
| Finite Samples (10178) | Acquire or generate a finite number of samples. |
| Hardware Timed Single Point (12522) | Acquire or generate samples continuously using hardware timing without a buffer. Hardware timed single point sample mode is supported only for the sample clock and change detection timing types. |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Timing VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-timing-pipelined-sample-clock-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02100: DAQmx Timing (Pipelined Sample Clock) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-timing-pipelined-sample-clock-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-timing-pipelined-sample-clock-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Sets the source of the Sample Clock, the rate of the Sample Clock, and the number of samples to acquire or generate. The device acquires or generates samples on each Sample Clock edge, but it does not respond to certain triggers until a few Sample Clock edges later. Pipelining allows higher data tra

### DAQmx Timing (Pipelined Sample Clock) VI

Sets the source of the [Sample Clock](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_sampclock), the rate of the Sample Clock, and the number of samples to acquire or generate.
 The device acquires or generates samples on each Sample Clock edge, but it does not respond to certain triggers until a few Sample Clock edges later.
 Pipelining allows higher data transfer rates at the cost of increased trigger response latency. 
 Refer to the device documentation for information about which triggers pipelining affects.

This timing type allows handshaking using the Pause trigger and either the Ready for Transfer event or the Data Active event.
 Refer to the device documentation for more information.

This timing type is supported only by the NI 6536 and NI 6537.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-timing-pipelined-sample-clock-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| samples per channel — samples per channel specifies the number of samples to acquire or generate for each channel in the task if sample mode is Finite Samples. If sample mode is Continuous Samples, NI-DAQmx uses this value to determine the buffer size. This VI returns an error if the specified value is negative. sample mode — sample mode specifies if the task acquires or generates samples continuously or if it acquires or generates a finite number of samples. Continuous Samples (10123) Acquire or generate samples until the DAQmx Stop Task VI runs. Finite Samples (10178) Acquire or generate a finite number of samples. Hardware Timed Single Point (12522) Acquire or generate samples continuously using hardware timing without a buffer. Hardware timed single point sample mode is supported only for the sample clock and change detection timing types. task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. rate — rate specifies the sampling rate in samples per channel per second. If you use an external source for the Sample Clock, set this input to the maximum expected rate of that clock. source — source specifies the source terminal of the Sample Clock. Leave this input unwired to use the default onboard clock of the device. active edge — active edge specifies on which edges of Sample Clock pulses to acquire or generate samples. Falling (10171) Acquire or generate samples on falling edges of the Sample Clock. Rising (10280) Acquire or generate samples on rising edges of the Sample Clock. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Continuous Samples (10123) | Acquire or generate samples until the DAQmx Stop Task VI runs. |
| Finite Samples (10178) | Acquire or generate a finite number of samples. |
| Hardware Timed Single Point (12522) | Acquire or generate samples continuously using hardware timing without a buffer. Hardware timed single point sample mode is supported only for the sample clock and change detection timing types. |
| Falling (10171) | Acquire or generate samples on falling edges of the Sample Clock. |
| Rising (10280) | Acquire or generate samples on rising edges of the Sample Clock. |

DAQmx Timing VI

## TOPIC 02101: DAQmx Timing Property Node

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-timing-property-node-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-timing-property-node-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: A Property Node with the DAQmx Timing class preselected. Right-click the Property Node and choose Select Filter from the shortcut menu to make the Property Node show only the properties supported by a particular device installed in the system or supported by all the devices installed in the system.

### DAQmx Timing Property Node

A Property Node with the [DAQmx Timing](/csh?context=nidaqmx_nidaq_daqmx_timing_p) class preselected. Right-click the Property Node and choose **Select Filter** from the shortcut menu to make the Property Node show only the properties supported by a particular device installed in the system or supported by all the devices installed in the system.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-timing-property-node-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. error in (no error) — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. Sample Quantity:Sample Mode — Sample Quantity:Sample Mode is an example of a property you want to get (read) or set (write). task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-timing-sample-clock-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02102: DAQmx Timing (Sample Clock) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-timing-sample-clock-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-timing-sample-clock-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Sets the source of the Sample Clock, the rate of the Sample Clock, and the number of samples to acquire or generate. icon Inputs/Outputs ci32.png samples per channel samples per channel specifies the number of samples to acquire or generate for each channel in the task if sample mode is Finite Sampl

### DAQmx Timing (Sample Clock) VI

Sets the source of the [Sample Clock](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_sampclock), the rate of the Sample Clock, and the number of samples to acquire or generate.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-timing-sample-clock-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| samples per channel — samples per channel specifies the number of samples to acquire or generate for each channel in the task if sample mode is Finite Samples. If sample mode is Continuous Samples, NI-DAQmx uses this value to determine the buffer size. This VI returns an error if the specified value is negative. sample mode — sample mode specifies if the task acquires or generates samples continuously or if it acquires or generates a finite number of samples. Continuous Samples (10123) Acquire or generate samples until the DAQmx Stop Task VI runs. Finite Samples (10178) Acquire or generate a finite number of samples. Hardware Timed Single Point (12522) Acquire or generate samples continuously using hardware timing without a buffer. Hardware timed single point sample mode is supported only for the sample clock and change detection timing types. task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. rate — rate specifies the sampling rate in samples per channel per second. If you use an external source for the Sample Clock, set this input to the maximum expected rate of that clock. source — source specifies the source terminal of the Sample Clock. Leave this input unwired to use the default onboard clock of the device. active edge — active edge specifies on which edges of Sample Clock pulses to acquire or generate samples. Falling (10171) Acquire or generate samples on falling edges of the Sample Clock. Rising (10280) Acquire or generate samples on rising edges of the Sample Clock. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Continuous Samples (10123) | Acquire or generate samples until the DAQmx Stop Task VI runs. |
| Finite Samples (10178) | Acquire or generate a finite number of samples. |
| Hardware Timed Single Point (12522) | Acquire or generate samples continuously using hardware timing without a buffer. Hardware timed single point sample mode is supported only for the sample clock and change detection timing types. |
| Falling (10171) | Acquire or generate samples on falling edges of the Sample Clock. |
| Rising (10280) | Acquire or generate samples on rising edges of the Sample Clock. |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Timing VI

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-timing-source-property-node-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| error in (no error) — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. Active Timing Source — Active Timing Source is an example of a property you want to get (read) or set (write). error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. <!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-timing-use-waveform-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02104: DAQmx Timing (Use Waveform) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-timing-use-waveform-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-timing-use-waveform-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Uses the dt component of the waveform input to determine the Sample Clock rate. dt is the time in seconds between samples. If sample mode is Finite Samples, NI-DAQmx generates the number of samples in the waveform. This VI does not actually generate any samples. You must wire the same waveform to th

### DAQmx Timing (Use Waveform) VI

Uses the **dt** component of the **waveform** input to determine the Sample Clock rate. **dt** is the time in seconds between samples. If **sample mode** is **Finite Samples**, NI-DAQmx generates the number of samples in the waveform. This VI does not actually generate any samples. You must wire the same waveform to the [DAQmx Write](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_mxwrite) VI to generate the samples.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-timing-use-waveform-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| sample mode — sample mode specifies if the task acquires or generates samples continuously or if it acquires or generates a finite number of samples. Continuous Samples (10123) Acquire or generate samples until the DAQmx Stop Task VI runs. Finite Samples (10178) Acquire or generate a finite number of samples. Hardware Timed Single Point (12522) Acquire or generate samples continuously using hardware timing without a buffer. Hardware timed single point sample mode is supported only for the sample clock and change detection timing types. task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. waveform — waveform is the waveform to use for timing. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Continuous Samples (10123) | Acquire or generate samples until the DAQmx Stop Task VI runs. |
| Finite Samples (10178) | Acquire or generate a finite number of samples. |
| Hardware Timed Single Point (12522) | Acquire or generate samples continuously using hardware timing without a buffer. Hardware timed single point sample mode is supported only for the sample clock and change detection timing types. |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Timing VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-timing-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02105: DAQmx Timing VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-timing-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-timing-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Configures the number of samples to acquire or generate and creates a buffer when needed. The instances of this polymorphic VI correspond to the type of timing to use for the task. The DAQmx Timing properties include all timing options included in this VI and additional timing options. icon

### DAQmx Timing VI

Configures the number of samples to acquire or generate and creates a buffer when needed. The instances of this [polymorphic VI](/csh?context=lvcore_lvhelp_using_polymorphic_vis) correspond to the type of timing to use for the [task](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_tasksnidaqmx).

The [DAQmx Timing](/csh?context=nidaqmx_nidaq_daqmx_timing_p) properties include all timing options included in this VI and additional timing options.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-timing-vi.png']

- [DAQmx Timing (Sample Clock) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-timing-sample-clock-vi.html) Sets the source of the Sample Clock , the rate of the Sample Clock, and the number of samples to acquire or generate.
- [DAQmx Timing (Handshaking) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-timing-handshaking-vi.html) Determines the number of digital samples to acquire or generate using digital handshaking between the device and a peripheral device.
- [DAQmx Timing (Burst Import Clock) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-timing-burst-import-clock-vi.html) Configures when the DAQ device transfers data to a peripheral device, using an imported sample clock to control burst handshake timing .
- [DAQmx Timing (Burst Export Clock) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-timing-burst-export-clock-vi.html) Configures when the DAQ device transfers data to a peripheral device, using the onboard Sample Clock of the DAQ device to control burst handshake timing and exporting that clock for use by the peripheral device.
- [DAQmx Timing (Implicit) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-timing-implicit-vi.html) Sets only the number of samples to acquire or generate without specifying timing. Typically, you should use this instance when the task does not require sample timing, such as tasks that use counters for buffered frequency measurement, buffered period measurement, or pulse train generation. For finite counter output tasks, samples per channel is the number of pulses to generate.
- [DAQmx Timing (Use Waveform) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-timing-use-waveform-vi.html) Uses the dt component of the waveform input to determine the Sample Clock rate. dt is the time in seconds between samples. If sample mode is Finite Samples , NI-DAQmx generates the number of samples in the waveform. This VI does not actually generate any samples. You must wire the same waveform to the DAQmx Write VI to generate the samples.
- [DAQmx Timing (Change Detection) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-timing-change-detection-vi.html) Configures the task to acquire samples on the rising and/or falling edges of the lines or ports you specify. To detect both rising and falling edges on a line or port, wire the name of that line or port to both rising edge physical channels and falling edge physical channels .
- [DAQmx Timing (Pipelined Sample Clock) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-timing-pipelined-sample-clock-vi.html) Sets the source of the Sample Clock , the rate of the Sample Clock, and the number of samples to acquire or generate. The device acquires or generates samples on each Sample Clock edge, but it does not respond to certain triggers until a few Sample Clock edges later. Pipelining allows higher data transfer rates at the cost of increased trigger response latency. Refer to the device documentation for information about which triggers pipelining affects.

Parent topic:

DAQmx - Data Acquisition

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-wait-for-next-sample-clock-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02106: DAQmx Wait For Next Sample Clock VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-wait-for-next-sample-clock-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/timing-llb/daqmx-wait-for-next-sample-clock-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Waits until the next pulse of the Sample Clock occurs. If an extra Sample Clock pulse occurs between calls to this VI, the second call returns an error or warning and waits for the next Sample Clock pulse. Use the Convert Late Errors to Warnings DAQmx Real-Time property to specify whether this VI re

### DAQmx Wait For Next Sample Clock VI

Waits until the next pulse of the [Sample Clock](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_sampclock) occurs. If an extra Sample Clock pulse occurs between calls to this VI, the second call returns an error or warning and waits for the next Sample Clock pulse. Use the [Convert Late Errors to Warnings](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqmxprop_attr22ee) DAQmx Real-Time property to specify whether this VI returns errors or warnings. If that property is TRUE, any warnings this VI returns do not include the **source** string.

Use this VI to ensure [I/O cycles](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_iocycles) complete within Sample Clock periods. National Instruments recommends you use this VI for certain [applications](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_readwritelate) only.

Use the [DAQmx Real-Time](/csh?context=nidaqmx_nidaq_daqmx_real-time_p) properties to configure error reporting and waiting options.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-wait-for-next-sample-clock-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. timeout (sec) — timeout (sec) specifies the maximum amount of time in seconds to wait for the next Sample Clock pulse. If the time elapses, this VI returns an error. The default timeout is 10 seconds. If you set timeout to -1, this VI waits indefinitely. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. is late? — is late? indicates if this VI detected an extra Sample Clock pulse after the specified number of warmup iterations execute. Use the Number of Warmup Iterations DAQmx Real Time property to specify the number of warmup iterations. This output is always FALSE until that number of loop iterations execute. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Real-Time

## TOPIC 02107: DAQmx Advance Trigger (Digital Edge) VI

If you pro

### DAQmx Advance Trigger (Digital Edge) VI

(Deprecated) Configures a switch task to advance to the next entry in a scan list on a rising or falling edge of a digital signal.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-advance-trigger-digital-edge-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. source — source specifies the name of a terminal where there is a digital signal to use as the source of the trigger. edge — edge specifies on which edge of a digital signal to advance to the next entry in the scan list. Falling (10171) Advance to the next scan list entry on a falling edge of the digital signal. Rising (10280) Advance to the next scan list entry on a rising edge of the digital signal. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Falling (10171) | Advance to the next scan list entry on a falling edge of the digital signal. |
| Rising (10280) | Advance to the next scan list entry on a rising edge of the digital signal. |

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-reference-trigger-analog-edge-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02109: DAQmx Reference Trigger (Analog Edge) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-reference-trigger-analog-edge-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-reference-trigger-analog-edge-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Configures the task to stop the acquisition when the device acquires all pretrigger samples; an analog signal reaches the level you specify; and the device acquires all post-trigger samples. When you use a Reference Trigger, the default for the read RelativeTo property is First Pretrigger Sample wit

### DAQmx Reference Trigger (Analog Edge) VI

Configures the task to stop the acquisition when the device acquires all pretrigger samples; an analog signal reaches the level you specify; and the device acquires all post-trigger samples. When you use a [Reference Trigger](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_triggering), the default for the read [RelativeTo](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqmxprop_attr190a) property is **First Pretrigger Sample** with a read [Offset](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqmxprop_attr190b) of 0.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-reference-trigger-analog-edge-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. source — source is the name of a virtual channel or terminal where there is an analog signal to use as the source of the trigger. For E Series devices, if you use a virtual channel, it must be the only channel in the task. The only terminal you can use for E Series devices is PFI0. slope — slope specifies on which slope of the signal the Reference Trigger occurs. Falling (10171) Trigger when the signal crosses level on a falling slope. Rising (10280) Trigger when the signal crosses level on a rising slope. level — level specifies at what threshold to trigger. Specify this value in the units of the measurement or generation. Use slope to specify on which slope to trigger at this threshold. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. pretrigger samples per channel — pretrigger samples per channel specifies the minimum number of samples to acquire per channel before recognizing the Reference Trigger. The number of post-trigger samples per channel is equal to number of samples per channel in the DAQmx Timing VI minus pretrigger samples per channel. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Falling (10171) | Trigger when the signal crosses level on a falling slope. |
| Rising (10280) | Trigger when the signal crosses level on a rising slope. |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Trigger VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-reference-trigger-analog-multi-edge-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02110: DAQmx Reference Trigger (Analog Multi Edge) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-reference-trigger-analog-multi-edge-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-reference-trigger-analog-multi-edge-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Configures the task to stop the acquisition when the device acquires all pretrigger samples; any of the configured analog signals cross the respective levels you specified; and the device acquires all post-trigger samples. When you use a Reference Trigger, the default for the read RelativeTo propert

### DAQmx Reference Trigger (Analog Multi Edge) VI

Configures the task to stop the acquisition when the device acquires all pretrigger samples; any of the configured analog signals cross the respective levels you specified; and the device acquires all post-trigger samples. When you use a [Reference Trigger](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_triggering), the default for the read [RelativeTo](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqmxprop_attr190a) property is **First Pretrigger Sample** with a read [Offset](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqmxprop_attr190b) of 0. Multi-edge triggering treats the specified triggers as if a logical OR is applied.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-reference-trigger-analog-multi-edge-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. edge configurations — edge configurations specifies configurations of the analog edges to trigger on. source — source is the name of a virtual channel or terminal where there is an analog signal to use as the source of the trigger. For E Series devices, if you use a virtual channel, it must be the only channel in the task. The only terminal you can use for E Series devices is PFI0. slope — slope specifies on which slope of the signal the Reference Trigger occurs. Falling (10171) Trigger when the signal crosses level on a falling slope. Rising (10280) Trigger when the signal crosses level on a rising slope. level — level specifies at what threshold to trigger. Specify this value in the units of the measurement or generation. Use slope to specify on which slope to trigger at this threshold. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. pretrigger samples per channel — pretrigger samples per channel specifies the minimum number of samples to acquire per channel before recognizing the Reference Trigger. The number of post-trigger samples per channel is equal to number of samples per channel in the DAQmx Timing VI minus pretrigger samples per channel. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| source — source is the name of a virtual channel or terminal where there is an analog signal to use as the source of the trigger. For E Series devices, if you use a virtual channel, it must be the only channel in the task. The only terminal you can use for E Series devices is PFI0. slope — slope specifies on which slope of the signal the Reference Trigger occurs. Falling (10171) Trigger when the signal crosses level on a falling slope. Rising (10280) Trigger when the signal crosses level on a rising slope. level — level specifies at what threshold to trigger. Specify this value in the units of the measurement or generation. Use slope to specify on which slope to trigger at this threshold. |  |
| Falling (10171) | Trigger when the signal crosses level on a falling slope. |
| Rising (10280) | Trigger when the signal crosses level on a rising slope. |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Trigger VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-reference-trigger-analog-window-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02111: DAQmx Reference Trigger (Analog Window) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-reference-trigger-analog-window-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-reference-trigger-analog-window-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Configures the task to stop the acquisition when the device acquires all pretrigger samples; an analog signal enters or leaves a range you specify; and the device acquires all post-trigger samples. When you use a Reference Trigger, the default for the read RelativeTo property is First Pretrigger Sam

### DAQmx Reference Trigger (Analog Window) VI

Configures the task to stop the acquisition when the device acquires all pretrigger samples; an analog signal enters or leaves a range you specify; and the device acquires all post-trigger samples. When you use a [Reference Trigger](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_referencetrigger), the default for the read [RelativeTo](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqmxprop_attr190a) property is **First Pretrigger Sample** with a read [Offset](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqmxprop_attr190b) of 0.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-reference-trigger-analog-window-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| when — when specifies whether the Reference Trigger occurs when the signal enters the window or when it leaves the window. Use window bottom and window top to specify the limits of the window. Entering Window (10163) Trigger when the signal enters the window. Leaving Window (10208) Trigger when the signal leaves the window. task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. source — source is the name of a virtual channel or terminal where there is an analog signal to use as the source of the trigger. For E Series devices, if you use a virtual channel, it must be the only channel in the task. The only terminal you can use for E Series devices is PFI0. window top — window top is the upper limit of the window. Specify this value in the units of the measurement or generation. window bottom — window bottom is the lower limit of the window. Specify this value in the units of the measurement or generation. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. pretrigger samples per channel — pretrigger samples per channel specifies the minimum number of samples to acquire per channel before recognizing the Reference Trigger. The number of post-trigger samples per channel is equal to number of samples per channel in the DAQmx Timing VI minus pretrigger samples per channel. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Entering Window (10163) | Trigger when the signal enters the window. |
| Leaving Window (10208) | Trigger when the signal leaves the window. |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Trigger VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-reference-trigger-digital-edge-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02112: DAQmx Reference Trigger (Digital Edge) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-reference-trigger-digital-edge-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-reference-trigger-digital-edge-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Configures the task to stop the acquisition when the device acquires all pretrigger samples, detects a rising or falling edge of a digital signal, and acquires all posttrigger samples. When you use a Reference Trigger, the default for the read RelativeTo property is First Pretrigger Sample with a re

### DAQmx Reference Trigger (Digital Edge) VI

Configures the task to stop the acquisition when the device acquires all pretrigger samples, detects a rising or falling edge of a digital signal, and acquires all posttrigger samples. When you use a [Reference Trigger](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_referencetrigger), the default for the read [RelativeTo](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqmxprop_attr190a) property is **First Pretrigger Sample** with a read [Offset](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqmxprop_attr190b) of 0.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-reference-trigger-digital-edge-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. source — source specifies the name of a terminal where there is a digital signal to use as the source of the trigger. edge — edge specifies on which edge of the digital signal the Reference Trigger occurs. Falling (10171) Trigger on a falling edge of the digital signal. Rising (10280) Trigger on a rising edge of the digital signal. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. pretrigger samples per channel — pretrigger samples per channel specifies the minimum number of samples to acquire per channel before recognizing the Reference Trigger. The number of post-trigger samples per channel is equal to number of samples per channel in the DAQmx Timing VI minus pretrigger samples per channel. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Falling (10171) | Trigger on a falling edge of the digital signal. |
| Rising (10280) | Trigger on a rising edge of the digital signal. |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Trigger VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-reference-trigger-digital-pattern-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02113: DAQmx Reference Trigger (Digital Pattern) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-reference-trigger-digital-pattern-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-reference-trigger-digital-pattern-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Configures the task to stop the acquisition when the device acquires all pretrigger samples, matches a digital pattern, and acquires all posttrigger samples. When you use a Reference Trigger, the default for the read RelativeTo property is First PretriggerSample with a read Offset of zero. icon Inpu

### DAQmx Reference Trigger (Digital Pattern) VI

Configures the task to stop the acquisition when the device acquires all pretrigger samples, matches a [digital pattern](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_digpattern), and acquires all posttrigger samples. When you use a Reference Trigger, the default for the read RelativeTo property is First PretriggerSample with a read Offset of zero.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-reference-trigger-digital-pattern-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| trigger when — trigger when specifies the condition under which the trigger occurs. Pattern Does Not Match (10253) Trigger when the physical channels do not match the specified pattern. Pattern Matches (10254) Trigger when the physical channels match the specified pattern. task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. source — source specifies the physical channels to use for pattern matching. The order of the physical channels determines the order of the pattern. If a port is included, the order of the physical channels within the port is in ascending order. pattern — pattern specifies the digital pattern that must be met for the trigger to occur. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. pretrigger samples per channel — pretrigger samples per channel specifies the minimum number of samples to acquire per channel before recognizing the Reference Trigger. The number of post-trigger samples per channel is equal to number of samples per channel in the DAQmx Timing VI minus pretrigger samples per channel. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Pattern Does Not Match (10253) | Trigger when the physical channels do not match the specified pattern. |
| Pattern Matches (10254) | Trigger when the physical channels match the specified pattern. |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Trigger VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-reference-trigger-none-vi.html language=enus -->

If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. cerrcode

### DAQmx Reference Trigger (None) VI

Disables reference triggering for the measurement.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-reference-trigger-none-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |

## TOPIC 02115: DAQmx Start Trigger (Analog Edge) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-start-trigger-analog-edge-vi.html`

- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Configures the task to start acquiring or generating samples when an analog signal crosses the level you specify. icon Inputs/Outputs cgenclassrntag.png task/channels in task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of vi

### DAQmx Start Trigger (Analog Edge) VI

Configures the task to start acquiring or generating samples when an analog signal crosses the level you specify.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-start-trigger-analog-edge-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. source — source is the name of a virtual channel or terminal where there is an analog signal to use as the source of the trigger. For E Series devices, if you use a channel name, the channel must be the first channel in the task. The only terminal you can use for E Series devices is PFI0. slope — slope specifies on which slope of the signal to start acquiring or generating samples when the signal crosses level. Falling (10171) Start acquiring or generating samples when the signal crosses level on a falling slope. Rising (10280) Start acquiring or generating samples when the signal crosses level on a rising slope. level — level specifies at what threshold to start acquiring or generating samples. Specify this value in the units of the measurement or generation. Use slope to specify on which slope to trigger at this threshold. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Falling (10171) | Start acquiring or generating samples when the signal crosses level on a falling slope. |
| Rising (10280) | Start acquiring or generating samples when the signal crosses level on a rising slope. |

## TOPIC 02117: DAQmx Start Trigger (Analog Window) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`

- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Configures the task to start acquiring or generating samples when an analog signal enters or leaves a range you specify. icon Inputs/Outputs ci32.png when when specifies whether the task starts measuring or generating samples when the signal enters the window or when it leaves the window. Use window

### DAQmx Start Trigger (Analog Window) VI

Configures the task to start acquiring or generating samples when an analog signal enters or leaves a range you specify.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-start-trigger-analog-window-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| when — when specifies whether the task starts measuring or generating samples when the signal enters the window or when it leaves the window. Use window bottom and window top to specify the limits of the window. Entering Window (10163) Start acquiring or generating samples when the signal enters the window. Leaving Window (10208) Start acquiring or generating samples when the signal leaves the window. task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. source — source is the name of a virtual channel or terminal where there is an analog signal to use as the source of the trigger. For E Series devices, if you use a channel name, the channel must be the first channel in the task. The only terminal you can use for E Series devices is PFI0. window top — window top is the upper limit of the window. Specify this value in the units of the measurement or generation. window bottom — window bottom is the lower limit of the window. Specify this value in the units of the measurement or generation. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Entering Window (10163) | Start acquiring or generating samples when the signal enters the window. |
| Leaving Window (10208) | Start acquiring or generating samples when the signal leaves the window. |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Trigger VI

- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Configures the task to start acquiring or generating samples on a rising or falling edge of a digital signal. icon Inputs/Outputs cgenclassrntag.png task/channels in task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtua

### DAQmx Start Trigger (Digital Edge) VI

Configures the task to start acquiring or generating samples on a rising or falling edge of a digital signal.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-start-trigger-digital-edge-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. source — source specifies the name of a terminal where there is a digital signal to use as the source of the trigger. edge — edge specifies on which edge of the digital signal to start acquiring or generating samples. Falling (10171) Start acquiring or generating samples on a falling edge of the digital signal. Rising (10280) Start acquiring or generating samples on a rising edge of the digital signal. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Falling (10171) | Start acquiring or generating samples on a falling edge of the digital signal. |
| Rising (10280) | Start acquiring or generating samples on a rising edge of the digital signal. |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Trigger VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-start-trigger-digital-pattern-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02119: DAQmx Start Trigger (Digital Pattern) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-start-trigger-digital-pattern-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-start-trigger-digital-pattern-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Configures a task to start acquiring or generating samples when a digital pattern is matched. icon Inputs/Outputs ci32.png trigger when trigger when specifies the condition under which the trigger occurs. Pattern Does Not Match (10253) Trigger when the physical channels do not match the specified pa

### DAQmx Start Trigger (Digital Pattern) VI

Configures a task to start acquiring or generating samples when a [digital pattern](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_digpattern) is matched.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-start-trigger-digital-pattern-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| trigger when — trigger when specifies the condition under which the trigger occurs. Pattern Does Not Match (10253) Trigger when the physical channels do not match the specified pattern. Pattern Matches (10254) Trigger when the physical channels match the specified pattern. task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. source — source specifies the physical channels to use for pattern matching. The order of the physical channels determines the order of the pattern. If a port is included, the order of the physical channels within the port is in ascending order. pattern — pattern specifies the digital pattern that must be met for the trigger to occur. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Pattern Does Not Match (10253) | Trigger when the physical channels do not match the specified pattern. |
| Pattern Matches (10254) | Trigger when the physical channels match the specified pattern. |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Trigger VI

If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates

### DAQmx Start Trigger (Time) VI

Configures the task to start acquiring or generating samples at a specified time.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-start-trigger-time-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. when — when specifies when to trigger. timescale — timescale specifies the start trigger timestamp time scale. Host Time (16126) Use the host device. I/O Device Time (16127) Use the I/O device. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |

| Host Time (16126) | Use the host device. |

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-trigger-property-node-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02122: DAQmx Trigger Property Node

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-trigger-property-node-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-trigger-property-node-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: A Property Node with the DAQmx Trigger class preselected. Right-click the Property Node and choose Select Filter from the shortcut menu to make the Property Node show only the properties supported by a particular device installed in the system or supported by all the devices installed in the system.

### DAQmx Trigger Property Node

A Property Node with the [DAQmx Trigger](/csh?context=nidaqmx_nidaq_daqmx_trigger_p) class preselected. Right-click the Property Node and choose **Select Filter** from the shortcut menu to make the Property Node show only the properties supported by a particular device installed in the system or supported by all the devices installed in the system.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-trigger-property-node-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. error in (no error) — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. Start:Trigger Type — Start:Trigger Type is an example of a property you want to get (read) or set (write). task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |
