<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-trigger-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02123: DAQmx Trigger VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-trigger-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-trigger-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Configures triggering for the task. The instances of this polymorphic VI correspond to the trigger and trigger type to configure. The DAQmx Trigger properties include all triggering options included in this VI, as well as additional triggering options. icon

### DAQmx Trigger VI

Configures [triggering](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_triggering) for the task. The instances of this [polymorphic VI](/csh?context=lvcore_lvhelp_using_polymorphic_vis) correspond to the trigger and trigger type to configure.

The [DAQmx Trigger](/csh?context=nidaqmx_nidaq_daqmx_trigger_p) properties include all triggering options included in this VI, as well as additional triggering options.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-trigger-vi.png']

- [DAQmx Start Trigger (None) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-start-trigger-none-vi.html) Configures the task to start acquiring or generating samples immediately upon starting the task.
- [DAQmx Start Trigger (Digital Edge) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-start-trigger-digital-edge-vi.html) Configures the task to start acquiring or generating samples on a rising or falling edge of a digital signal.
- [DAQmx Start Trigger (Digital Pattern) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-start-trigger-digital-pattern-vi.html) Configures a task to start acquiring or generating samples when a digital pattern is matched.
- [DAQmx Start Trigger (Analog Edge) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-start-trigger-analog-edge-vi.html) Configures the task to start acquiring or generating samples when an analog signal crosses the level you specify.
- [DAQmx Start Trigger (Analog Multi Edge) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-start-trigger-analog-multi-edge-vi.html) Configures the task to start acquiring or generating samples when any of the configured analog signals cross the respective levels you specified. Multi-edge triggering treats the configured triggers as if a logical OR is applied.
- [DAQmx Start Trigger (Analog Window) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-start-trigger-analog-window-vi.html) Configures the task to start acquiring or generating samples when an analog signal enters or leaves a range you specify.
- [DAQmx Start Trigger (Time) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-start-trigger-time-vi.html) Configures the task to start acquiring or generating samples at a specified time.
- [DAQmx Reference Trigger (None) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-reference-trigger-none-vi.html) Disables reference triggering for the measurement.
- [DAQmx Reference Trigger (Digital Edge) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-reference-trigger-digital-edge-vi.html) Configures the task to stop the acquisition when the device acquires all pretrigger samples, detects a rising or falling edge of a digital signal, and acquires all posttrigger samples. When you use a Reference Trigger , the default for the read RelativeTo property is First Pretrigger Sample with a read Offset of 0.
- [DAQmx Reference Trigger (Digital Pattern) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-reference-trigger-digital-pattern-vi.html) Configures the task to stop the acquisition when the device acquires all pretrigger samples, matches a digital pattern , and acquires all posttrigger samples. When you use a Reference Trigger, the default for the read RelativeTo property is First PretriggerSample with a read Offset of zero.
- [DAQmx Reference Trigger (Analog Edge) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-reference-trigger-analog-edge-vi.html) Configures the task to stop the acquisition when the device acquires all pretrigger samples; an analog signal reaches the level you specify; and the device acquires all post-trigger samples. When you use a Reference Trigger , the default for the read RelativeTo property is First Pretrigger Sample with a read Offset of 0.
- [DAQmx Reference Trigger (Analog Multi Edge) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-reference-trigger-analog-multi-edge-vi.html) Configures the task to stop the acquisition when the device acquires all pretrigger samples; any of the configured analog signals cross the respective levels you specified; and the device acquires all post-trigger samples. When you use a Reference Trigger , the default for the read RelativeTo property is First Pretrigger Sample with a read Offset of 0. Multi-edge triggering treats the specified triggers as if a logical OR is applied.
- [DAQmx Reference Trigger (Analog Window) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-reference-trigger-analog-window-vi.html) Configures the task to stop the acquisition when the device acquires all pretrigger samples; an analog signal enters or leaves a range you specify; and the device acquires all post-trigger samples. When you use a Reference Trigger , the default for the read RelativeTo property is First Pretrigger Sample with a read Offset of 0.
- [DAQmx Advance Trigger (None) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-advance-trigger-none-vi.html) (Deprecated) Disables advance triggering for the task.
- [DAQmx Advance Trigger (Digital Edge) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/configure/trigger-llb/daqmx-advance-trigger-digital-edge-vi.html) (Deprecated) Configures a switch task to advance to the next entry in a scan list on a rising or falling edge of a digital signal.

Parent topic:

DAQmx - Data Acquisition

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-acceleration-4-wire-dc-voltage-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02124: DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Acceleration-4 Wire DC Voltage) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-acceleration-4-wire-dc-voltage-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-acceleration-4-wire-dc-voltage-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) to measure acceleration. Use this instance for custom sensors that require excitation. You can use the excitation to scale the measurement. icon Inputs/Outputs cdbl.png voltage excitation value voltage excitation value specifies in volts the amount of excitation supplied to the se

### DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Acceleration-4 Wire DC Voltage) VI

Creates channel(s) to [measure acceleration](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_accel). Use this instance for custom sensors that require excitation. You can use the excitation to scale the measurement.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ai-acceleration-4-wire-dc-voltage-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| voltage excitation value — voltage excitation value specifies in volts the amount of excitation supplied to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine appropriate excitation values. voltage excitation source — voltage excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. use excitation for scaling — use excitation for scaling specifies if NI-DAQmx divides the measurement by the excitation. You should typically set use excitation for scaling to TRUE for ratiometric transducers. If you set use excitation for scaling to TRUE, set maximum value and minimum value to reflect the scaling. For example, if you expect to acquire a voltage between -5 and 5, and you use an excitation of 0.10 volts to scale the measurement, set minimum value to -50 and set maximum value to 50. input terminal configuration — input terminal configuration specifies the input terminal configuration for the channel. default (-1) At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. Differential (10106) Differential mode. NRSE (10078) Non-referenced single-ended mode. Pseudodifferential (12529) Pseudodifferential mode. RSE (10083) Referenced single-ended mode. minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units to use to return acceleration measurements from the channel. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. g (10186) 1 g is approximately equal to 9.81 m/s/s. m/s^2 (12470) Meters per second per second. in/s^2 (12471) Inches per second per second. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. sensitivity — sensitivity is the sensitivity of the sensor. This value is in the units you specify with the sensitivity units input. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine this value. sensitivity units — sensitivity units specifies the units of the sensitivity input. mVolts/g (12509) mVolts/g. Volts/g (12510) Volts/g. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| External (10167) | Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| Internal (10200) | Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| None (10230) | Supply no excitation to the channel. |
| default (-1) | At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. |
| Differential (10106) | Differential mode. |
| NRSE (10078) | Non-referenced single-ended mode. |
| Pseudodifferential (12529) | Pseudodifferential mode. |
| RSE (10083) | Referenced single-ended mode. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| g (10186) | 1 g is approximately equal to 9.81 m/s/s. |
| m/s^2 (12470) | Meters per second per second. |
| in/s^2 (12471) | Inches per second per second. |
| mVolts/g (12509) | mVolts/g. |
| Volts/g (12510) | Volts/g. |

DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-acceleration-accelerometer-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02125: DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Acceleration-Accelerometer) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-acceleration-accelerometer-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-acceleration-accelerometer-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) that use an accelerometer to measure acceleration. icon Inputs/Outputs cdbl.png current excitation value current excitation value specifies in amperes the amount of excitation to supply to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine this value. ci32.png current exci

### DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Acceleration-Accelerometer) VI

Creates channel(s) that use an [accelerometer](/csh?context=nidaqmx_measfunds_accelerometers) to [measure acceleration](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_accel).

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ai-acceleration-accelerometer-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| current excitation value — current excitation value specifies in amperes the amount of excitation to supply to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine this value. current excitation source — current excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. input terminal configuration — input terminal configuration specifies the input terminal configuration for the channel. default (-1) At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. Differential (10106) Differential mode. NRSE (10078) Non-referenced single-ended mode. Pseudodifferential (12529) Pseudodifferential mode. RSE (10083) Referenced single-ended mode. minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units to use to return acceleration measurements from the channel. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. g (10186) 1 g is approximately equal to 9.81 m/s/s. m/s^2 (12470) Meters per second per second. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. sensitivity — sensitivity is the sensitivity of the sensor. This value is in the units you specify with the sensitivity units input. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine this value. sensitivity units — sensitivity units specifies the units of the sensitivity input. mVolts/g (12509) mVolts/g. Volts/g (12510) Volts/g. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| External (10167) | Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| Internal (10200) | Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| None (10230) | Supply no excitation to the channel. |
| default (-1) | At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. |
| Differential (10106) | Differential mode. |
| NRSE (10078) | Non-referenced single-ended mode. |
| Pseudodifferential (12529) | Pseudodifferential mode. |
| RSE (10083) | Referenced single-ended mode. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| g (10186) | 1 g is approximately equal to 9.81 m/s/s. |
| m/s^2 (12470) | Meters per second per second. |
| mVolts/g (12509) | mVolts/g. |
| Volts/g (12510) | Volts/g. |

### DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Acceleration-Charge) VI

Creates channel(s) that use a charge-based sensor to [measure acceleration](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_accel).

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ai-acceleration-charge-vi.png']

| input terminal configuration — input terminal configuration specifies the input terminal configuration for the channel. default (-1) At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units to use to return acceleration measurements from the channel. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. g (10186) 1 g is approximately equal to 9.81 m/s/s. m/s^2 (12470) Meters per second per second. in/s^2 (12471) Inches per second per second. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. sensitivity — sensitivity is the sensitivity of the sensor. This value is in the units you specify with the sensitivity units input. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine this value. sensitivity units — sensitivity units specifies the units of the sensitivity input. pCoulomb/g (16099) pCoulomb/g. pCoulomb/(m/s^2) (16100) pCoulomb/(m/s^2). pCoulomb/(in/s^2) (16101) pCoulomb/(in/s^2). task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-bridge-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02127: DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Bridge) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-bridge-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-bridge-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) that measure voltage ratios from a Wheatstone bridge. Use this instance with bridge-based sensors that measure phenomena other than strain, force, pressure, or torque, or that scale data to physical units NI-DAQmx does not support. Use a custom scale or other scaling code to conve

### DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Bridge) VI

Creates channel(s) that measure [voltage ratios](/csh?context=nidaqmx_measfunds_bridgescaling) from a [Wheatstone bridge](/csh?context=nidaqmx_measfunds_bridgeforcepressuretorque). 
 Use this instance with bridge-based sensors that measure phenomena other than strain, force, pressure, or torque, or that scale data to physical units NI-DAQmx does not support.

Use a custom scale or other scaling code to convert the voltage ratios to physical units.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ai-bridge-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| bridge information — bridge information specifies information about the bridge configuration and measurement. bridge configuration — bridge configuration specifies the Wheatstone bridge configuration connected to the channel. Full Bridge (10182) Four active sensing elements in the bridge. Half Bridge (10187) Two active sensing elements in the bridge. Quarter Bridge (10270) One active sensing element in the bridge. voltage excitation source — voltage excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. voltage excitation value — voltage excitation value specifies in volts the amount of excitation supplied to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine appropriate excitation values. nominal bridge resistance — nominal bridge resistance specifies in ohms the resistance of the bridge while not under load. minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies in which unit to return voltage ratios from the channel. Volts/Volt (15896) Volts per volt. mVolts/Volt (15897) Millivolts per volt. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| bridge configuration — bridge configuration specifies the Wheatstone bridge configuration connected to the channel. Full Bridge (10182) Four active sensing elements in the bridge. Half Bridge (10187) Two active sensing elements in the bridge. Quarter Bridge (10270) One active sensing element in the bridge. voltage excitation source — voltage excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. voltage excitation value — voltage excitation value specifies in volts the amount of excitation supplied to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine appropriate excitation values. nominal bridge resistance — nominal bridge resistance specifies in ohms the resistance of the bridge while not under load. |  |
| Full Bridge (10182) | Four active sensing elements in the bridge. |
| Half Bridge (10187) | Two active sensing elements in the bridge. |
| Quarter Bridge (10270) | One active sensing element in the bridge. |
| External (10167) | Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| Internal (10200) | Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| None (10230) | Supply no excitation to the channel. |
| Volts/Volt (15896) | Volts per volt. |
| mVolts/Volt (15897) | Millivolts per volt. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |

## TOPIC 02128: DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Calculated Power) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-calculated-power-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-calculated-power-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) to measure calculated power. icon Inputs/Outputs cdbl.png external shunt resistor value external shunt resistor value specifies in ohms the resistance of an external shunt resistor. ci32.png input terminal configuration input terminal configuration specifies the input terminal con

### DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Calculated Power) VI

Creates channel(s) to [measure calculated power](/csh?topicname=javascript-launchmeasapp.html).

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ai-calculated-power-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| external shunt resistor value — external shunt resistor value specifies in ohms the resistance of an external shunt resistor. input terminal configuration — input terminal configuration specifies the input terminal configuration for the channel. default (-1) At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. Differential (10106) Differential mode. NRSE (10078) Non-referenced single-ended mode. Pseudodifferential (12529) Pseudodifferential mode. RSE (10083) Referenced single-ended mode. voltage input limits — voltage input limits specifies in units the voltage input limits you expect to measure. voltage minimum value — voltage minimum value specifies in units the voltage minimum value that the measurement expects. voltage maximum value — voltage maximum value specifies in units the voltage maximum value that the measurement expects. current input limits — current input limits specifies in units the current input limits you expect to measure. current minimum value — current minimum value specifies in units the current minimum value that the measurement expects. current maximum value — current maximum value specifies in units the current maximum value that the measurement expects. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. voltage physical channels — voltage physical channels specifies the names of the voltage physical channels to use to create virtual power channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. current physical channels — current physical channels specifies the names of the current physical channels to use to create virtual power channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx creates a default channel name. The format of this name is Power#, where # is an index number incremented to the first unique value. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. units — units specifies the units to use to return current measurements. Watts (16203) Watts. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. shunt resistor location — shunt resistor location specifies the location of the shunt resistor. For devices with built-in shunt resistors, specify the location as Internal. For devices that do not have built-in shunt resistors, you must attach an external one, set this input to External and use the external shunt resistor value input to specify the value of the resistor. default (-1) NI-DAQmx selects Internal for devices with an internal shunt resistor. NI-DAQmx selects External for devices without an internal shunt resistor. External (10167) Use a shunt resistor external to the device. You must use the external shunt resistor value input to specify the value of the shunt resistor. Internal (10200) Use the built-in shunt resistor of the device. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| default (-1) | At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. |
| Differential (10106) | Differential mode. |
| NRSE (10078) | Non-referenced single-ended mode. |
| Pseudodifferential (12529) | Pseudodifferential mode. |
| RSE (10083) | Referenced single-ended mode. |
| voltage minimum value — voltage minimum value specifies in units the voltage minimum value that the measurement expects. voltage maximum value — voltage maximum value specifies in units the voltage maximum value that the measurement expects. |  |
| current minimum value — current minimum value specifies in units the current minimum value that the measurement expects. current maximum value — current maximum value specifies in units the current maximum value that the measurement expects. |  |
| Watts (16203) | Watts. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| default (-1) | NI-DAQmx selects Internal for devices with an internal shunt resistor. NI-DAQmx selects External for devices without an internal shunt resistor. |
| External (10167) | Use a shunt resistor external to the device. You must use the external shunt resistor value input to specify the value of the shunt resistor. |
| Internal (10200) | Use the built-in shunt resistor of the device. |

## TOPIC 02129: DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Charge) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-charge-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-charge-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) that use a sensor with charge output. icon Inputs/Outputs ci32.png input terminal configuration input terminal configuration specifies the input terminal configuration for the channel. default (-1) At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. D

### DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Charge) VI

Creates channel(s) that use a sensor with charge output.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ai-charge-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| input terminal configuration — input terminal configuration specifies the input terminal configuration for the channel. default (-1) At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. Differential (10106) Differential mode. NRSE (10078) Non-referenced single-ended mode. Pseudodifferential (12529) Pseudodifferential mode. RSE (10083) Referenced single-ended mode. minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units to use to return charge measurements from the channel. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. pCoulombs (16103) Picocoulombs. Coulombs (16102) Coulombs. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| default (-1) | At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. |
| Differential (10106) | Differential mode. |
| NRSE (10078) | Non-referenced single-ended mode. |
| Pseudodifferential (12529) | Pseudodifferential mode. |
| RSE (10083) | Referenced single-ended mode. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| pCoulombs (16103) | Picocoulombs. |
| Coulombs (16102) | Coulombs. |

## TOPIC 02130: DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Current-Basic) VI

- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) to measure current. icon Inputs/Outputs cdbl.png external shunt resistor value external shunt resistor value specifies in ohms the resistance of an external shunt resistor. ci32.png input terminal configuration input terminal configuration specifies the input terminal configuratio

### DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Current-Basic) VI

Creates channel(s) to [measure current](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_measuring_current).

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ai-current-basic-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| external shunt resistor value — external shunt resistor value specifies in ohms the resistance of an external shunt resistor. input terminal configuration — input terminal configuration specifies the input terminal configuration for the channel. default (-1) At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. Differential (10106) Differential mode. NRSE (10078) Non-referenced single-ended mode. Pseudodifferential (12529) Pseudodifferential mode. RSE (10083) Referenced single-ended mode. minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units to use to return current measurements. Amps (10342) Amperes. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. shunt resistor location — shunt resistor location specifies the location of the shunt resistor. For devices with built-in shunt resistors, specify the location as Internal. For devices that do not have built-in shunt resistors, you must attach an external one, set this input to External and use the external shunt resistor value input to specify the value of the resistor. default (-1) NI-DAQmx selects Internal for devices with an internal shunt resistor. NI-DAQmx selects External for devices without an internal shunt resistor. External (10167) Use a shunt resistor external to the device. You must use the external shunt resistor value input to specify the value of the shunt resistor. Internal (10200) Use the built-in shunt resistor of the device. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| default (-1) | At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. |
| Differential (10106) | Differential mode. |
| NRSE (10078) | Non-referenced single-ended mode. |
| Pseudodifferential (12529) | Pseudodifferential mode. |
| RSE (10083) | Referenced single-ended mode. |
| Amps (10342) | Amperes. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| default (-1) | NI-DAQmx selects Internal for devices with an internal shunt resistor. NI-DAQmx selects External for devices without an internal shunt resistor. |
| External (10167) | Use a shunt resistor external to the device. You must use the external shunt resistor value input to specify the value of the shunt resistor. |
| Internal (10200) | Use the built-in shunt resistor of the device. |

## TOPIC 02132: DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Force-Bridge-Polynomial) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-force-bridge-polynomial-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-force-bridge-polynomial-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) that use a Wheatstone bridge to measure force or load. Use this instance with sensors whose specifications provide a polynomial to convert electrical values to physical values. When you use this scaling type, NI-DAQmx requires coefficients for a polynomial that converts electrical

### DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Force-Bridge-Polynomial) VI

Creates channel(s) that use a [Wheatstone bridge](/csh?context=nidaqmx_measfunds_bridgeforcepressuretorque) to [measure force or load](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_force). 
 Use this instance with sensors whose specifications provide a polynomial to convert electrical values to physical values. 
 When you use this [scaling type](/csh?context=nidaqmx_measfunds_bridgescalingtypes), NI-DAQmx requires coefficients for a polynomial that converts electrical values to physical values (forward), as well as coefficients for a polynomial that converts physical values to electrical values (reverse).
 If you only know one set of coefficients, use the [DAQmx Compute Reverse Polynomial Coefficients](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_mxcomputereversecoeff) VI to generate the other set.

Specify different values for **units** and **physical units** if the sensor specifications provide physical values in one unit, but you want NI-DAQmx to scale data to a different unit.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ai-force-bridge-polynomial-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| bridge information — bridge information specifies information about the bridge configuration and measurement. bridge configuration — bridge configuration specifies the Wheatstone bridge configuration connected to the channel. Full Bridge (10182) Four active sensing elements in the bridge. Half Bridge (10187) Two active sensing elements in the bridge. Quarter Bridge (10270) One active sensing element in the bridge. voltage excitation source — voltage excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. voltage excitation value — voltage excitation value specifies in volts the amount of excitation supplied to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine appropriate excitation values. nominal bridge resistance — nominal bridge resistance specifies in ohms the resistance of the bridge while not under load. minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies in which unit to return force measurements from the channel. Newtons (15875) Newtons. Pounds (15876) Pounds. kgf (15877) Kilograms-force From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. scaling information — scaling information specifies how to scale electrical values from the sensor to physical units. forward coeff — forward coeff is an array of coefficients for the polynomial that converts electrical values to physical values. Each element of the array corresponds to a term of the equation. For example, if index three of the array is 9, the fourth term of the equation is 9x^3. reverse coeff — reverse coeff is an array of coefficients for the polynomial that converts physical values to electrical values. Each element of the array corresponds to a term of the equation. For example, if index three of the array is 9, the fourth term of the equation is 9x^3. electrical units — electrical units specifies from which electrical unit to scale data. Select the same unit that the sensor data sheet or calibration certificate uses for electrical values. Volts/Volt (15896) Volts per volt. mVolts/Volt (15897) Millivolts per volt. physical units — physical units specifies to which physical unit to scale electrical data. Select the same unit that the sensor data sheet or calibration certificate uses for physical values. Newtons (15875) Newtons. Pounds (15876) Pounds. kgf (15877) Kilograms-force task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| bridge configuration — bridge configuration specifies the Wheatstone bridge configuration connected to the channel. Full Bridge (10182) Four active sensing elements in the bridge. Half Bridge (10187) Two active sensing elements in the bridge. Quarter Bridge (10270) One active sensing element in the bridge. voltage excitation source — voltage excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. voltage excitation value — voltage excitation value specifies in volts the amount of excitation supplied to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine appropriate excitation values. nominal bridge resistance — nominal bridge resistance specifies in ohms the resistance of the bridge while not under load. |  |
| Full Bridge (10182) | Four active sensing elements in the bridge. |
| Half Bridge (10187) | Two active sensing elements in the bridge. |
| Quarter Bridge (10270) | One active sensing element in the bridge. |
| External (10167) | Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| Internal (10200) | Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| None (10230) | Supply no excitation to the channel. |
| Newtons (15875) | Newtons. |
| Pounds (15876) | Pounds. |
| kgf (15877) | Kilograms-force |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| forward coeff — forward coeff is an array of coefficients for the polynomial that converts electrical values to physical values. Each element of the array corresponds to a term of the equation. For example, if index three of the array is 9, the fourth term of the equation is 9x^3. reverse coeff — reverse coeff is an array of coefficients for the polynomial that converts physical values to electrical values. Each element of the array corresponds to a term of the equation. For example, if index three of the array is 9, the fourth term of the equation is 9x^3. electrical units — electrical units specifies from which electrical unit to scale data. Select the same unit that the sensor data sheet or calibration certificate uses for electrical values. Volts/Volt (15896) Volts per volt. mVolts/Volt (15897) Millivolts per volt. physical units — physical units specifies to which physical unit to scale electrical data. Select the same unit that the sensor data sheet or calibration certificate uses for physical values. Newtons (15875) Newtons. Pounds (15876) Pounds. kgf (15877) Kilograms-force |  |
| Volts/Volt (15896) | Volts per volt. |
| mVolts/Volt (15897) | Millivolts per volt. |
| Newtons (15875) | Newtons. |
| Pounds (15876) | Pounds. |
| kgf (15877) | Kilograms-force |

## TOPIC 02133: DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Force-Bridge-Table) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-force-bridge-table-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-force-bridge-table-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) that use a Wheatstone bridge to measure force or load. Use this instance with sensors whose specifications provide a table of electrical values and the corresponding physical values. When you use this scaling type, NI-DAQmx performs linear scaling between each pair of electrical a

### DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Force-Bridge-Table) VI

Creates channel(s) that use a [Wheatstone bridge](/csh?context=nidaqmx_measfunds_bridgeforcepressuretorque) to [measure force or load](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_force). 
 Use this instance with sensors whose specifications provide a table of electrical values and the corresponding physical values. 
 When you use this [scaling type](/csh?context=nidaqmx_measfunds_bridgescalingtypes), NI-DAQmx performs linear scaling between each pair of electrical and physical values. 
 The input limits specified with **minimum value** and **maximum value** must fall within the smallest and largest physical values.
 For any data outside those endpoints, NI-DAQmx coerces that data to the endpoints.

Specify different values for **units** and **physical units** if the sensor specifications provide physical values in one unit, but you want NI-DAQmx to scale data to a different unit.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ai-force-bridge-table-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| bridge information — bridge information specifies information about the bridge configuration and measurement. bridge configuration — bridge configuration specifies the Wheatstone bridge configuration connected to the channel. Full Bridge (10182) Four active sensing elements in the bridge. Half Bridge (10187) Two active sensing elements in the bridge. Quarter Bridge (10270) One active sensing element in the bridge. voltage excitation source — voltage excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. voltage excitation value — voltage excitation value specifies in volts the amount of excitation supplied to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine appropriate excitation values. nominal bridge resistance — nominal bridge resistance specifies in ohms the resistance of the bridge while not under load. minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies in which unit to return force measurements from the channel. Newtons (15875) Newtons. Pounds (15876) Pounds. kgf (15877) Kilograms-force From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. scaling information — scaling information specifies how to scale electrical values from the sensor to physical units. electrical values — electrical values is the array of electrical values that map to the values in physical values. Specify this value in the unit indicated by electrical units. electrical units — electrical units specifies from which electrical unit to scale data. Select the same unit that the sensor data sheet or calibration certificate uses for electrical values. Volts/Volt (15896) Volts per volt. mVolts/Volt (15897) Millivolts per volt. physical values — physical values is the array of physical values that map to the values in electrical values. Specify this value in the unit indicated by physical units. physical units — physical units specifies to which physical unit to scale electrical data. Select the same unit that the sensor data sheet or calibration certificate uses for physical values. Newtons (15875) Newtons. Pounds (15876) Pounds. kgf (15877) Kilograms-force task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| bridge configuration — bridge configuration specifies the Wheatstone bridge configuration connected to the channel. Full Bridge (10182) Four active sensing elements in the bridge. Half Bridge (10187) Two active sensing elements in the bridge. Quarter Bridge (10270) One active sensing element in the bridge. voltage excitation source — voltage excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. voltage excitation value — voltage excitation value specifies in volts the amount of excitation supplied to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine appropriate excitation values. nominal bridge resistance — nominal bridge resistance specifies in ohms the resistance of the bridge while not under load. |  |
