## TOPIC 02134: DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Force-Bridge-Two-Point-Linear) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-force-bridge-two-point-linear-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-force-bridge-two-point-linear-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) that use a Wheatstone bridge to measure force or load. Use this instance with sensors whose specifications do not provide a polynomial for scaling or a table of electrical and physical values. When you use this scaling type, NI-DAQmx uses two points of electrical and physical valu

### DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Force-Bridge-Two-Point-Linear) VI

Creates channel(s) that use a [Wheatstone bridge](/csh?context=nidaqmx_measfunds_bridgeforcepressuretorque) to [measure force or load](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_force). 
 Use this instance with sensors whose specifications do not provide a polynomial for scaling or a table of electrical and physical values. 
 When you use this [scaling type](/csh?context=nidaqmx_measfunds_bridgescalingtypes), NI-DAQmx uses two points of electrical and physical values to calculate the slope and y-intercept of a linear equation and uses that equation to scale electrical values to physical values.

Specify different values for **units** and **physical units** if the sensor specifications provide physical values in one unit, but you want NI-DAQmx to scale data to a different unit.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ai-force-bridge-two-point-linear-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| bridge information — bridge information specifies information about the bridge configuration and measurement. bridge configuration — bridge configuration specifies the Wheatstone bridge configuration connected to the channel. Full Bridge (10182) Four active sensing elements in the bridge. Half Bridge (10187) Two active sensing elements in the bridge. Quarter Bridge (10270) One active sensing element in the bridge. voltage excitation source — voltage excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. voltage excitation value — voltage excitation value specifies in volts the amount of excitation supplied to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine appropriate excitation values. nominal bridge resistance — nominal bridge resistance specifies in ohms the resistance of the bridge while not under load. minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies in which unit to return force measurements from the channel. Newtons (15875) Newtons. Pounds (15876) Pounds. kgf (15877) Kilograms-force From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. scaling information — scaling information specifies how to scale electrical values from the sensor to physical units. first electrical value — first electrical value is the first electrical value, corresponding to first physical value. Specify this value in the unit indicated by electrical units. second electrical value — second electrical value is the second electrical value, corresponding to second physical value. Specify this value in the unit indicated by electrical units. electrical units — electrical units specifies from which electrical unit to scale data. Select the same unit that the sensor data sheet or calibration certificate uses for electrical values. Volts/Volt (15896) Volts per volt. mVolts/Volt (15897) Millivolts per volt. first physical value — first physical value is the first physical value, corresponding to first electrical value. Specify this value in the unit indicated by physical units. second physical value — second physical value is the second physical value, corresponding to second electrical value. Specify this value in the unit indicated by physical units. physical units — physical units specifies to which physical unit to scale electrical data. Select the same unit that the sensor data sheet or calibration certificate uses for physical values. Newtons (15875) Newtons. Pounds (15876) Pounds. kgf (15877) Kilograms-force task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| bridge configuration — bridge configuration specifies the Wheatstone bridge configuration connected to the channel. Full Bridge (10182) Four active sensing elements in the bridge. Half Bridge (10187) Two active sensing elements in the bridge. Quarter Bridge (10270) One active sensing element in the bridge. voltage excitation source — voltage excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. voltage excitation value — voltage excitation value specifies in volts the amount of excitation supplied to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine appropriate excitation values. nominal bridge resistance — nominal bridge resistance specifies in ohms the resistance of the bridge while not under load. |  |
| Full Bridge (10182) | Four active sensing elements in the bridge. |
| Half Bridge (10187) | Two active sensing elements in the bridge. |
| Quarter Bridge (10270) | One active sensing element in the bridge. |
| External (10167) | Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |

Specify this value in the unit indicated by electrical units. second electrical value — second electrical value is the second electrical value, corresponding to second physical value. Specify this value in the unit indicated by electrical units. electrical units — electrical units specifies from which electrical unit to scale data. Select the same unit that the sensor data sheet or calibration certificate uses for electrical values. Volts/Volt (15896) Volts per volt. mVolts/Volt (15897) Millivolts per volt. first physical value — first physical value is the first physical value, corresponding to first electrical value. Specify this value in the unit indicated by physical units. second physical value — second physical value is the second physical value, corresponding to second electrical value. Specify this value in the unit indicated by physical units. physical units — physical units specifies to which physical unit to scale electrical data. Select the same unit that the sensor data sheet or calibration certificate uses for physical values. Newtons (15875) Newtons. Pounds (15876) Pounds. kgf (15877) Kilograms-force |  |

## TOPIC 02135: DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Force-IEPE Sensor) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-force-iepe-sensor-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-force-iepe-sensor-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) that use an IEPE force sensor to measure force or load. icon Inputs/Outputs cdbl.png current excitation value current excitation value specifies in amperes the amount of excitation to supply to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine this value. ci32.png current

### DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Force-IEPE Sensor) VI

Creates channel(s) that use an [IEPE force sensor](/csh?context=nidaqmx_measfunds_forcepiezo) to [measure force or load](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_force).

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ai-force-iepe-sensor-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| current excitation value — current excitation value specifies in amperes the amount of excitation to supply to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine this value. current excitation source — current excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. input terminal configuration — input terminal configuration specifies the input terminal configuration for the channel. default (-1) At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. Differential (10106) Differential mode. NRSE (10078) Non-referenced single-ended mode. Pseudodifferential (12529) Pseudodifferential mode. RSE (10083) Referenced single-ended mode. minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies in which unit to return force measurements from the channel. Newtons (15875) Newtons. Pounds (15876) Pounds. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. sensitivity — sensitivity is the sensitivity of the sensor. This value is in the units you specify with the sensitivity units input. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine this value. sensitivity units — sensitivity units specifies the units of the sensitivity input. mVolts/N (14891) Millivolts per newton. mVolts/lb (14892) Millivolts per pound. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| External (10167) | Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| Internal (10200) | Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| None (10230) | Supply no excitation to the channel. |
| default (-1) | At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. |
| Differential (10106) | Differential mode. |
| NRSE (10078) | Non-referenced single-ended mode. |
| Pseudodifferential (12529) | Pseudodifferential mode. |
| RSE (10083) | Referenced single-ended mode. |
| Newtons (15875) | Newtons. |
| Pounds (15876) | Pounds. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| mVolts/N (14891) | Millivolts per newton. |
| mVolts/lb (14892) | Millivolts per pound. |

## TOPIC 02136: DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Frequency-Voltage) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-frequency-voltage-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-frequency-voltage-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) that use a frequency-to-voltage converter to measure frequency. icon Inputs/Outputs cdbl.png hysteresis hysteresis specifies in volts a window below level. The input voltage must pass below threshold level minus hysteresis before NI-DAQmx recognizes a waveform repetition. Hysteres

### DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Frequency-Voltage) VI

Creates channel(s) that use a frequency-to-voltage converter to [measure frequency](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_meas_analog_freq).

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ai-frequency-voltage-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| hysteresis — hysteresis specifies in volts a window below level. The input voltage must pass below threshold level minus hysteresis before NI-DAQmx recognizes a waveform repetition. Hysteresis can improve measurement accuracy when the signal contains noise or jitter. threshold level — threshold level specifies in volts the level at which to recognize waveform repetitions. You should select a voltage level that occurs only once within the entire period of a waveform. You also can select a voltage that occurs only once while the voltage rises or falls. minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units to use to return frequency measurements. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. Hz (10373) Hertz. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| Hz (10373) | Hertz. |

## TOPIC 02137: DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Position-EddyCurrentProxProbe) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-position-eddycurrentproxprobe-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-position-eddycurrentproxprobe-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) that use an eddy current proximity probe to measure position. icon Inputs/Outputs cdbl.png minimum value minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. cdbl.png maximum value maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. cgenc

### DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Position-EddyCurrentProxProbe) VI

Creates channel(s) that use an [eddy current proximity probe](/csh?context=nidaqmx_measfunds_eddycurrentproximityprobe) to [measure position](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_measuring_proximity).

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ai-position-eddycurrentproxprobe-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units to use to return position measurements from the channel. Meters (10219) Meters. Inches (10379) Inches. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. sensitivity — sensitivity is the sensitivity of the sensor. This value is in the units you specify with the sensitivity units input. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine this value. sensitivity units — sensitivity units specifies the units of the sensitivity input. mVolts/mil (14836) mVolts/mil. Volts/mil (14837) Volts/mil. mVolts/mm (14838) mVolts/mm. Volts/mm (14839) Volts/mm. mVolts/micron (14840) mVolts/micron. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Meters (10219) | Meters. |
| Inches (10379) | Inches. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| mVolts/mil (14836) | mVolts/mil. |
| Volts/mil (14837) | Volts/mil. |
| mVolts/mm (14838) | mVolts/mm. |
| Volts/mm (14839) | Volts/mm. |
| mVolts/micron (14840) | mVolts/micron. |

## TOPIC 02138: DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Position-LVDT) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-position-lvdt-vi.html`

- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) that use an LVDT to measure linear position. icon Inputs/Outputs cdbl.png voltage excitation frequency voltage excitation frequency specifies in hertz the excitation frequency that the sensor requires. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine this value. cdbl.png voltage exc

### DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Position-LVDT) VI

Creates channel(s) that use an [LVDT](/csh?context=nidaqmx_measfunds_lvdts) to [measure linear position](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_lindisplacelvdt).

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ai-position-lvdt-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| voltage excitation frequency — voltage excitation frequency specifies in hertz the excitation frequency that the sensor requires. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine this value. voltage excitation value — voltage excitation value specifies in volts the amount of excitation supplied to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine appropriate excitation values. voltage excitation source — voltage excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. voltage excitation wire mode — voltage excitation wire mode is the number of leads on the sensor. Some sensors require you to tie leads together to create a four- or five- wire sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation for more information. 4-Wire (4) Four wires. 5-Wire (5) Five wires. 6-Wire (6) Six wires. minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units to use to return linear position measurements from the channel. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. Inches (10379) Inches. Meters (10219) Meters. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. sensitivity — sensitivity is the sensitivity of the sensor. This value is in the units you specify with the sensitivity units input. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine this value. sensitivity units — sensitivity units specifies the units of the sensitivity input. mVolts/Volt/0.001 Inch (12505) mVolts/Volt/0.001 Inch. mVolts/Volt/mMeter (12506) mVolts/Volt/mMeter. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| External (10167) | Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| Internal (10200) | Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| None (10230) | Supply no excitation to the channel. |
| 4-Wire (4) | Four wires. |
| 5-Wire (5) | Five wires. |
| 6-Wire (6) | Six wires. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| Inches (10379) | Inches. |
| Meters (10219) | Meters. |
| mVolts/Volt/0.001 Inch (12505) | mVolts/Volt/0.001 Inch. |
| mVolts/Volt/mMeter (12506) | mVolts/Volt/mMeter. |

You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. ## TOPIC 02140: DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Pressure-Bridge-Polynomial) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-pressure-bridge-polynomial-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-pressure-bridge-polynomial-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) that use a Wheatstone bridge to measure pressure. Use this instance with sensors whose specifications provide a polynomial to convert electrical values to physical values. When you use this scaling type, NI-DAQmx requires coefficients for a polynomial that converts electrical valu

### DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Pressure-Bridge-Polynomial) VI

Creates channel(s) that use a [Wheatstone bridge](/csh?context=nidaqmx_measfunds_bridgeforcepressuretorque) to [measure pressure](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_pressure). 
 Use this instance with sensors whose specifications provide a polynomial to convert electrical values to physical values. 
 When you use this [scaling type](/csh?context=nidaqmx_measfunds_bridgescalingtypes), NI-DAQmx requires coefficients for a polynomial that converts electrical values to physical values (forward), as well as coefficients for a polynomial that converts physical values to electrical values (reverse).
 If you only know one set of coefficients, use the [DAQmx Compute Reverse Polynomial Coefficients](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_mxcomputereversecoeff) VI to generate the other set.

Specify different values for **units** and **physical units** if the sensor specifications provide physical values in one unit, but you want NI-DAQmx to scale data to a different unit.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ai-pressure-bridge-polynomial-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| bridge information — bridge information specifies information about the bridge configuration and measurement. bridge configuration — bridge configuration specifies the Wheatstone bridge configuration connected to the channel. Full Bridge (10182) Four active sensing elements in the bridge. Half Bridge (10187) Two active sensing elements in the bridge. Quarter Bridge (10270) One active sensing element in the bridge. voltage excitation source — voltage excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. voltage excitation value — voltage excitation value specifies in volts the amount of excitation supplied to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine appropriate excitation values. nominal bridge resistance — nominal bridge resistance specifies in ohms the resistance of the bridge while not under load. minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies in which unit to return pressure measurements from the channel. psi (15879) Pounds per square inch. bar (15880) Bar. Pascals (10081) Pascals. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. scaling information — scaling information specifies how to scale electrical values from the sensor to physical units. forward coeff — forward coeff is an array of coefficients for the polynomial that converts electrical values to physical values. Each element of the array corresponds to a term of the equation. For example, if index three of the array is 9, the fourth term of the equation is 9x^3. reverse coeff — reverse coeff is an array of coefficients for the polynomial that converts physical values to electrical values. Each element of the array corresponds to a term of the equation. For example, if index three of the array is 9, the fourth term of the equation is 9x^3. electrical units — electrical units specifies from which electrical unit to scale data. Select the same unit that the sensor data sheet or calibration certificate uses for electrical values. Volts/Volt (15896) Volts per volt. mVolts/Volt (15897) Millivolts per volt. physical units — physical units specifies to which physical unit to scale electrical data. Select the same unit that the sensor data sheet or calibration certificate uses for physical values. psi (15879) Pounds per square inch. bar (15880) Bar. Pascals (15881) Pascals. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| bridge configuration — bridge configuration specifies the Wheatstone bridge configuration connected to the channel. Full Bridge (10182) Four active sensing elements in the bridge. Half Bridge (10187) Two active sensing elements in the bridge. Quarter Bridge (10270) One active sensing element in the bridge. voltage excitation source — voltage excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. voltage excitation value — voltage excitation value specifies in volts the amount of excitation supplied to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine appropriate excitation values. nominal bridge resistance — nominal bridge resistance specifies in ohms the resistance of the bridge while not under load. |  |
| Full Bridge (10182) | Four active sensing elements in the bridge. |
| Half Bridge (10187) | Two active sensing elements in the bridge. |
| Quarter Bridge (10270) | One active sensing element in the bridge. |
| External (10167) | Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| Internal (10200) | Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| None (10230) | Supply no excitation to the channel. |
| psi (15879) | Pounds per square inch. |
| bar (15880) | Bar. |
| Pascals (10081) | Pascals. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| forward coeff — forward coeff is an array of coefficients for the polynomial that converts electrical values to physical values. Each element of the array corresponds to a term of the equation. For example, if index three of the array is 9, the fourth term of the equation is 9x^3. reverse coeff — reverse coeff is an array of coefficients for the polynomial that converts physical values to electrical values. Each element of the array corresponds to a term of the equation. For example, if index three of the array is 9, the fourth term of the equation is 9x^3. electrical units — electrical units specifies from which electrical unit to scale data. Select the same unit that the sensor data sheet or calibration certificate uses for electrical values. Volts/Volt (15896) Volts per volt. mVolts/Volt (15897) Millivolts per volt. physical units — physical units specifies to which physical unit to scale electrical data. Select the same unit that the sensor data sheet or calibration certificate uses for physical values. psi (15879) Pounds per square inch. bar (15880) Bar. Pascals (15881) Pascals. |  |
| Volts/Volt (15896) | Volts per volt. |
| mVolts/Volt (15897) | Millivolts per volt. |
| psi (15879) | Pounds per square inch. |
| bar (15880) | Bar. |
| Pascals (15881) | Pascals. |

DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-pressure-bridge-table-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02141: DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Pressure-Bridge-Table) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-pressure-bridge-table-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-pressure-bridge-table-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) that use a Wheatstone bridge to measure pressure. Use this instance with sensors whose specifications provide a table of electrical values and the corresponding physical values. When you use this scaling type, NI-DAQmx performs linear scaling between each pair of electrical and ph

### DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Pressure-Bridge-Table) VI

Creates channel(s) that use a [Wheatstone bridge](/csh?context=nidaqmx_measfunds_bridgeforcepressuretorque) to [measure pressure](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_pressure). 
 Use this instance with sensors whose specifications provide a table of electrical values and the corresponding physical values. 
 When you use this [scaling type](/csh?context=nidaqmx_measfunds_bridgescalingtypes), NI-DAQmx performs linear scaling between each pair of electrical and physical values. 
 The input limits specified with **minimum value** and **maximum value** must fall within the smallest and largest physical values.
 For any data outside those endpoints, NI-DAQmx coerces that data to the endpoints.

Specify different values for **units** and **physical units** if the sensor specifications provide physical values in one unit, but you want NI-DAQmx to scale data to a different unit.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ai-pressure-bridge-table-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| bridge information — bridge information specifies information about the bridge configuration and measurement. bridge configuration — bridge configuration specifies the Wheatstone bridge configuration connected to the channel. Full Bridge (10182) Four active sensing elements in the bridge. Half Bridge (10187) Two active sensing elements in the bridge. Quarter Bridge (10270) One active sensing element in the bridge. voltage excitation source — voltage excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. voltage excitation value — voltage excitation value specifies in volts the amount of excitation supplied to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine appropriate excitation values. nominal bridge resistance — nominal bridge resistance specifies in ohms the resistance of the bridge while not under load. minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies in which unit to return pressure measurements from the channel. psi (15879) Pounds per square inch. bar (15880) Bar. Pascals (10081) Pascals. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. scaling information — scaling information specifies how to scale electrical values from the sensor to physical units. electrical values — electrical values is the array of electrical values that map to the values in physical values. Specify this value in the unit indicated by electrical units. electrical units — electrical units specifies from which electrical unit to scale data. Select the same unit that the sensor data sheet or calibration certificate uses for electrical values. Volts/Volt (15896) Volts per volt. mVolts/Volt (15897) Millivolts per volt. physical values — physical values is the array of physical values that map to the values in electrical values. Specify this value in the unit indicated by physical units. physical units — physical units specifies to which physical unit to scale electrical data. Select the same unit that the sensor data sheet or calibration certificate uses for physical values. psi (15879) Pounds per square inch. bar (15880) Bar. Pascals (15881) Pascals. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| bridge configuration — bridge configuration specifies the Wheatstone bridge configuration connected to the channel. Full Bridge (10182) Four active sensing elements in the bridge. Half Bridge (10187) Two active sensing elements in the bridge. Quarter Bridge (10270) One active sensing element in the bridge. voltage excitation source — voltage excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. voltage excitation value — voltage excitation value specifies in volts the amount of excitation supplied to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine appropriate excitation values. nominal bridge resistance — nominal bridge resistance specifies in ohms the resistance of the bridge while not under load. |  |

## TOPIC 02142: DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Pressure-Bridge-Two-Point-Linear) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-pressure-bridge-two-point-linear-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-pressure-bridge-two-point-linear-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) that use a Wheatstone bridge to measure pressure. Use this instance with sensors whose specifications do not provide a polynomial for scaling or a table of electrical and physical values. When you use this scaling type, NI-DAQmx uses two points of electrical and physical values to

### DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Pressure-Bridge-Two-Point-Linear) VI

Creates channel(s) that use a [Wheatstone bridge](/csh?context=nidaqmx_measfunds_bridgeforcepressuretorque) to [measure pressure](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_pressure). 
 Use this instance with sensors whose specifications do not provide a polynomial for scaling or a table of electrical and physical values. 
 When you use this [scaling type](/csh?context=nidaqmx_measfunds_bridgescalingtypes), NI-DAQmx uses two points of electrical and physical values to calculate the slope and y-intercept of a linear equation and uses that equation to scale electrical values to physical values.

Specify different values for **units** and **physical units** if the sensor specifications provide physical values in one unit, but you want NI-DAQmx to scale data to a different unit.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ai-pressure-bridge-two-point-linear-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| bridge information — bridge information specifies information about the bridge configuration and measurement. bridge configuration — bridge configuration specifies the Wheatstone bridge configuration connected to the channel. Full Bridge (10182) Four active sensing elements in the bridge. Half Bridge (10187) Two active sensing elements in the bridge. Quarter Bridge (10270) One active sensing element in the bridge. voltage excitation source — voltage excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. voltage excitation value — voltage excitation value specifies in volts the amount of excitation supplied to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine appropriate excitation values. nominal bridge resistance — nominal bridge resistance specifies in ohms the resistance of the bridge while not under load. minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies in which unit to return pressure measurements from the channel. psi (15879) Pounds per square inch. bar (15880) Bar. Pascals (10081) Pascals. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. scaling information — scaling information specifies how to scale electrical values from the sensor to physical units. first electrical value — first electrical value is the first electrical value, corresponding to first physical value. Specify this value in the unit indicated by electrical units. second electrical value — second electrical value is the second electrical value, corresponding to second physical value. Specify this value in the unit indicated by electrical units. electrical units — electrical units specifies from which electrical unit to scale data. Select the same unit that the sensor data sheet or calibration certificate uses for electrical values. Volts/Volt (15896) Volts per volt. mVolts/Volt (15897) Millivolts per volt. first physical value — first physical value is the first physical value, corresponding to first electrical value. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |

| bridge configuration — bridge configuration specifies the Wheatstone bridge configuration connected to the channel. Full Bridge (10182) Four active sensing elements in the bridge. Half Bridge (10187) Two active sensing elements in the bridge. ## TOPIC 02143: DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Resistance) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-resistance-vi.html`

Refer to the sensor documentation to determine this value. ci32.png resistance configuration resistance conf

### DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Resistance) VI

Creates channel(s) to [measure resistance](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_measuring_resistance).

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ai-resistance-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| current excitation value — current excitation value specifies in amperes the amount of excitation to supply to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine this value. resistance configuration — resistance configuration specifies the number of wires to use for resistive measurements. 2-Wire (2) 2-Wire mode. 3-Wire (3) 3-Wire mode. 4-Wire (4) 4-Wire mode. minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units to use to return resistance measurements. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. Ohms (10384) Ohms. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. current excitation source — current excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| 2-Wire (2) | 2-Wire mode. |
| 3-Wire (3) | 3-Wire mode. |
| 4-Wire (4) | 4-Wire mode. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| Ohms (10384) | Ohms. |
| External (10167) | Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| Internal (10200) | Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| None (10230) | Supply no excitation to the channel. |

## TOPIC 02144: DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Sound Pressure-Microphone) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`

- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) that use a microphone to measure sound pressure. icon Inputs/Outputs cdbl.png current excitation value current excitation value specifies in amperes the amount of excitation to supply to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine this value. ci32.png current excita

### DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Sound Pressure-Microphone) VI

Creates channel(s) that use a microphone to [measure sound pressure](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_soundpress).

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ai-sound-pressure-microphone-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| current excitation value — current excitation value specifies in amperes the amount of excitation to supply to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine this value. current excitation source — current excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. input terminal configuration — input terminal configuration specifies the input terminal configuration for the channel. default (-1) At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. Differential (10106) Differential mode. NRSE (10078) Non-referenced single-ended mode. Pseudodifferential (12529) Pseudodifferential mode. RSE (10083) Referenced single-ended mode. maximum sound pressure level — maximum sound pressure level is the maximum instantaneous sound pressure level you expect to measure. This value is in decibels, referenced to 20 micropascals. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units to use to return sound pressure measurements. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. Pascals (10081) Pascals. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. microphone sensitivity — microphone sensitivity is the sensitivity of the microphone. Specify this value in mV/Pa. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| External (10167) | Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| Internal (10200) | Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| None (10230) | Supply no excitation to the channel. |
| default (-1) | At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. |
| Differential (10106) | Differential mode. |
| NRSE (10078) | Non-referenced single-ended mode. |
| Pseudodifferential (12529) | Pseudodifferential mode. |
| RSE (10083) | Referenced single-ended mode. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| Pascals (10081) | Pascals. |
