## TOPIC 02155: DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Velocity-IEPE Sensor) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-velocity-iepe-sensor-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-velocity-iepe-sensor-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) that use an IEPE velocity sensor to measure velocity. icon Inputs/Outputs cdbl.png current excitation value current excitation value specifies in amperes the amount of excitation to supply to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine this value. ci32.png current e

### DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Velocity-IEPE Sensor) VI

Creates channel(s) that use an [IEPE velocity sensor](/csh?context=nidaqmx_measfunds_velocitytransducer) to [measure velocity](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_velocity).

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ai-velocity-iepe-sensor-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| current excitation value — current excitation value specifies in amperes the amount of excitation to supply to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine this value. current excitation source — current excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. input terminal configuration — input terminal configuration specifies the input terminal configuration for the channel. default (-1) At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. Differential (10106) Differential mode. NRSE (10078) Non-referenced single-ended mode. Pseudodifferential (12529) Pseudodifferential mode. RSE (10083) Referenced single-ended mode. minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies in which unit to return velocity measurements from the channel. in/s (15960) Inches per second. m/s (15959) Meters per second. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. sensitivity — sensitivity is the sensitivity of the sensor. This value is in the units you specify with the sensitivity units input. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine this value. sensitivity units — sensitivity units specifies the units of the sensitivity input. mVolts/in/s (15964) mVolts/in/s. mVolts/mm/s (15963) mVolts/mm/s. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| External (10167) | Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| Internal (10200) | Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| None (10230) | Supply no excitation to the channel. |
| default (-1) | At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. |
| Differential (10106) | Differential mode. |
| NRSE (10078) | Non-referenced single-ended mode. |
| Pseudodifferential (12529) | Pseudodifferential mode. |
| RSE (10083) | Referenced single-ended mode. |
| in/s (15960) | Inches per second. |
| m/s (15959) | Meters per second. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| mVolts/in/s (15964) | mVolts/in/s. |
| mVolts/mm/s (15963) | mVolts/mm/s. |

DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI

## TOPIC 02156: DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Voltage-Basic) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-voltage-basic-vi.html`

- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) to measure voltage. If the measurement requires the use of internal excitation or you need excitation to scale the voltage, use the instance of this VI. icon Inputs/Outputs ci32.png input terminal configuration input terminal configuration specifies the input terminal configuratio

### DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Voltage-Basic) VI

Creates channel(s) to [measure voltage](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_meas_volt). If the measurement requires the use of internal excitation or you need excitation to scale the voltage, use the instance of this VI.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ai-voltage-basic-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| input terminal configuration — input terminal configuration specifies the input terminal configuration for the channel. default (-1) At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. Differential (10106) Differential mode. NRSE (10078) Non-referenced single-ended mode. Pseudodifferential (12529) Pseudodifferential mode. RSE (10083) Referenced single-ended mode. minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units to use to return voltage measurements. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. V (10348) Volts. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| default (-1) | At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. |
| Differential (10106) | Differential mode. |
| NRSE (10078) | Non-referenced single-ended mode. |
| Pseudodifferential (12529) | Pseudodifferential mode. |
| RSE (10083) | Referenced single-ended mode. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| V (10348) | Volts. |

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-voltage-custom-with-excitation-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02157: DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Voltage-Custom with Excitation) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-voltage-custom-with-excitation-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-voltage-custom-with-excitation-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) to measure voltage. Use this instance for custom sensors that require excitation. You can use the excitation to scale the measurement. icon Inputs/Outputs cdbl.png voltage excitation value voltage excitation value specifies in volts the amount of excitation supplied to the sensor.

### DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Voltage-Custom with Excitation) VI

Creates channel(s) to measure voltage. Use this instance for custom sensors that require excitation. You can use the excitation to scale the measurement.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ai-voltage-custom-with-excitation-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| voltage excitation value — voltage excitation value specifies in volts the amount of excitation supplied to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine appropriate excitation values. use excitation for scaling — use excitation for scaling specifies if NI-DAQmx divides the measurement by the excitation. You should typically set use excitation for scaling to TRUE for ratiometric transducers. If you set use excitation for scaling to TRUE, set maximum value and minimum value to reflect the scaling. For example, if you expect to acquire a voltage between -5 and 5, and you use an excitation of 0.10 volts to scale the measurement, set minimum value to -50 and set maximum value to 50. If you set bridge configuration to No Bridge, use excitation for scaling has no effect on the measurement. input terminal configuration — input terminal configuration specifies the input terminal configuration for the channel. default (-1) At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. Differential (10106) Differential mode. NRSE (10078) Non-referenced single-ended mode. Pseudodifferential (12529) Pseudodifferential mode. RSE (10083) Referenced single-ended mode. minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units to use to return voltage measurements. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. V (10348) Volts. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. voltage excitation source — voltage excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. bridge configuration — bridge configuration specifies what type of Wheatstone bridge the sensor is. Full Bridge (10182) Sensor is a full bridge. If you set use excitation for scaling to TRUE, NI-DAQmx divides the measurement by the excitation value. Many sensors scale data to native units using scaling of volts per excitation. Half Bridge (10187) Sensor is a half bridge. If you set use excitation for scaling to TRUE, NI-DAQmx divides the measurement by the excitation value. Many sensors scale data to native units using scaling of volts per excitation. No Bridge (10228) Sensor is not a Wheatstone bridge. Quarter Bridge (10270) Sensor is a quarter bridge. If you set use excitation for scaling to TRUE, NI-DAQmx divides the measurement by the excitation value. Many sensors scale data to native units using scaling of volts per excitation. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| default (-1) | At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. |
| Differential (10106) | Differential mode. |
| NRSE (10078) | Non-referenced single-ended mode. |
| Pseudodifferential (12529) | Pseudodifferential mode. |
| RSE (10083) | Referenced single-ended mode. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| V (10348) | Volts. |
| External (10167) | Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| Internal (10200) | Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| None (10230) | Supply no excitation to the channel. |
| Full Bridge (10182) | Sensor is a full bridge. If you set use excitation for scaling to TRUE, NI-DAQmx divides the measurement by the excitation value. Many sensors scale data to native units using scaling of volts per excitation. |
| Half Bridge (10187) | Sensor is a half bridge. If you set use excitation for scaling to TRUE, NI-DAQmx divides the measurement by the excitation value. Many sensors scale data to native units using scaling of volts per excitation. |
| No Bridge (10228) | Sensor is not a Wheatstone bridge. |
| Quarter Bridge (10270) | Sensor is a quarter bridge. If you set use excitation for scaling to TRUE, NI-DAQmx divides the measurement by the excitation value. Many sensors scale data to native units using scaling of volts per excitation. |

From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. | default (-1) | At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. |
| Differential (10106) | Differential mode. |
| NRSE (10078) | Non-referenced single-ended mode. |
| Pseudodifferential (12529) | Pseudodifferential mode. |
| RSE (10083) | Referenced single-ended mode. |

## TOPIC 02160: DAQmx Create Channel (AO-FuncGen) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ao-funcgen-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ao-funcgen-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates a channel for continually generating a waveform on the selected physical channel. icon Inputs/Outputs cdbl.png offset offset is the voltage offset of the waveform to generate. cdbl.png amplitude amplitude is the zero-to-peak amplitude of the waveform to generate in volts. Zero and negative v

### DAQmx Create Channel (AO-FuncGen) VI

Creates a channel for continually generating a waveform on the selected physical channel.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ao-funcgen-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| offset — offset is the voltage offset of the waveform to generate. amplitude — amplitude is the zero-to-peak amplitude of the waveform to generate in volts. Zero and negative values are valid. frequency — frequency is the frequency of the waveform to generate in hertz. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. type — type specifies the kind of waveform to generate. Sine (14751) Sine wave. Triangle (14752) Triangle wave. Square (14753) Square wave. Sawtooth (14754) Sawtooth wave. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Sine (14751) | Sine wave. |
| Triangle (14752) | Triangle wave. |
| Square (14753) | Square wave. |
| Sawtooth (14754) | Sawtooth wave. |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ao-voltage-basic-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02161: DAQmx Create Channel (AO-Voltage-Basic) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ao-voltage-basic-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ao-voltage-basic-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) to generate voltage. icon Inputs/Outputs ci32.png output terminal configuration output terminal configuration specifies the output terminal configuration for the channel. Default (-1) At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. Differential (1

### DAQmx Create Channel (AO-Voltage-Basic) VI

Creates channel(s) to [generate voltage](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_genvolt).

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ao-voltage-basic-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| output terminal configuration — output terminal configuration specifies the output terminal configuration for the channel. Default (-1) At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. Differential (10106) Differential mode. Pseudodifferential (12529) Pseudodifferential mode. minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to generate. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to generate. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units to use to generate voltage. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. V (10348) Volts. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Default (-1) | At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. |
| Differential (10106) | Differential mode. |
| Pseudodifferential (12529) | Pseudodifferential mode. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| V (10348) | Volts. |

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-count-edges-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02162: DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Count Edges) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-count-edges-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-count-edges-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates a channel to count the number of rising or falling edges of a digital signal. With the exception of devices that support multi-counter tasks, you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple

### DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Count Edges) VI

Creates a channel to [count the number of rising or falling edges of a digital signal](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_countedge). With the exception of devices that support [multi-counter tasks](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxdevconsid_multicounterdevices), you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple counters simultaneously, use a separate task for each counter. Connect the input signal to the [default input terminal](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxdevconsid_counteroverview) of the counter unless you select a different input terminal.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ci-count-edges-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| edge — edge specifies on which edges of the input signal to increment or decrement the count. Falling (10171) Count falling edges. Rising (10280) Count rising edges. count direction — count direction specifies whether to increment or decrement the counter on each edge. Count Down (10124) Decrement counter. Count Up (10128) Increment counter. Externally Controlled (10326) The state of a digital line controls the count direction. Each counter has a default count direction terminal. initial count — initial count is the value from which to start counting. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. counter — counter specifies the name of the counter to use to create the virtual channel. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels, including counters, for devices installed in the system. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Falling (10171) | Count falling edges. |
| Rising (10280) | Count rising edges. |
| Count Down (10124) | Decrement counter. |
| Count Up (10128) | Increment counter. |
| Externally Controlled (10326) | The state of a digital line controls the count direction. Each counter has a default count direction terminal. |

DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-duty-cycle-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02163: DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Duty Cycle) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-duty-cycle-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-duty-cycle-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) to duty cycle of a digital pulse. Connect the input signal to the default input terminal of the counter unless you select a different input terminal. With the exception of devices that support multi-counter tasks, you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this V

### DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Duty Cycle) VI

Creates channel(s) to duty cycle of a digital pulse. Connect the input signal to the default input terminal of the counter unless you select a different input terminal. With the exception of devices that support [multi-counter tasks](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxdevconsid_multicounterdevices), you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple counters simultaneously, use a separate task for each counter.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ci-duty-cycle-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| minimum frequency — minimum frequency specifies the minimum frequency you expect to measure. maximum frequency — maximum frequency specifies the maximum frequency you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. counter — counter specifies the name of the counter to use to create the virtual channel. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels, including counters, for devices installed in the system. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. starting edge — starting edge specifies between which edges to measure the frequency or period of the signal. Falling (10171) Measure between falling edges. Rising (10280) Measure between rising edges. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Falling (10171) | Measure between falling edges. |
| Rising (10280) | Measure between rising edges. |

DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-frequency-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02164: DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Frequency) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-frequency-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-frequency-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates a channel to measure the frequency of a digital signal. With the exception of devices that support multi-counter tasks, you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple counters simultaneousl

### DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Frequency) VI

Creates a channel to [measure the frequency of a digital signal](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_measdigfreq). With the exception of devices that support [multi-counter tasks](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxdevconsid_multicounterdevices), you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple counters simultaneously, use a separate task for each counter. Connect the input signal to the [default input terminal](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxdevconsid_counteroverview) of the counter unless you select a different input terminal.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ci-frequency-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| divisor — divisor is the value by which to divide the input signal when measurement method is Large Range with 2 Counters. Leave this input unwired if measurement method is not Large Range with 2 Counters. The larger the divisor, the more accurate the measurement. However, too large a value could cause the count register to roll over, which results in an incorrect measurement. measurement method — measurement method specifies the method to use to calculate the period or frequency of the signal. High Frequency with 2 Counters (10157) Use two counters to count pulses of the signal to measure during measurement time. Large Range with 2 Counters (10205) Use one counter to divide the frequency of the input signal by divisor to create a lower-frequency signal that the second counter can more easily measure. Low Frequency with 1 Counter (10105) Use one counter that uses a constant timebase to measure the input signal. Dynamic Averaging (16065) Uses one counter and automatically configures the counter settings based on the range of frequencies to be measured. During the acquisition, the counter dynamically adjusts the number of periods that are averaged to balance measurement accuracy and latency. minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. counter — counter specifies the name of the counter to use to create the virtual channel. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels, including counters, for devices installed in the system. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units to use to return frequency measurements. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. Hz (10373) Hertz. Ticks (10304) Timebase ticks. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. starting edge — starting edge specifies between which edges to measure the frequency or period of the signal. Falling (10171) Measure between falling edges. Rising (10280) Measure between rising edges. measurement time — measurement time is the length of time in seconds to measure the frequency or period of the signal if measurement method is High Frequency with 2 Counters. Leave this input unwired if measurement method is not High Frequency with 2 Counters. Measurement accuracy increases with increased measurement time and with increased signal frequency. If you measure a high-frequency signal for too long, however, the count register could roll over, which results in an incorrect measurement. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| High Frequency with 2 Counters (10157) | Use two counters to count pulses of the signal to measure during measurement time. |
| Large Range with 2 Counters (10205) | Use one counter to divide the frequency of the input signal by divisor to create a lower-frequency signal that the second counter can more easily measure. |
| Low Frequency with 1 Counter (10105) | Use one counter that uses a constant timebase to measure the input signal. |
| Dynamic Averaging (16065) | Uses one counter and automatically configures the counter settings based on the range of frequencies to be measured. During the acquisition, the counter dynamically adjusts the number of periods that are averaged to balance measurement accuracy and latency. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| Hz (10373) | Hertz. |
| Ticks (10304) | Timebase ticks. |
| Falling (10171) | Measure between falling edges. |
| Rising (10280) | Measure between rising edges. |

DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-gps-timestamp-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02165: DAQmx Create Channel (CI-GPS Timestamp) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-gps-timestamp-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-gps-timestamp-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates a channel that uses a special purpose counter to take a timestamp and synchronizes that counter to a GPS receiver. With the exception of devices that support multi-counter tasks, you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter

### DAQmx Create Channel (CI-GPS Timestamp) VI

Creates a channel that uses a special purpose counter to [take a timestamp](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_gpsstampmeas) and synchronizes that counter to a GPS receiver. With the exception of devices that support [multi-counter tasks](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxdevconsid_multicounterdevices), you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple counters simultaneously, use a separate task for each counter. Connect the input signals to the [default input terminals](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxdevconsid_counteroverview) of the counter unless you select different input terminals.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ci-gps-timestamp-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| gps synchronization method — gps synchronization method specifies the method to use to synchronize the counter to a GPS receiver. IRIG-B (10070) Use the IRIG-B synchronization method. The GPS receiver sends one synchronization pulse per second, as well as information about the number of days, hours, minutes, and seconds that elapsed since the beginning of the current year. None (10230) Do not synchronize the counter to a GPS receiver. The timestamp measurement returns the number of seconds that elapsed since the device powered up unless you set the CI.Timestamp.InitialSeconds property. PPS (10080) Use the PPS synchronization method. The GPS receiver sends one synchronization pulse per second, but does not send any timing information. The timestamp measurement returns the number of seconds that elapsed since the device powered up unless you set the CI.Timestamp.InitialSeconds property. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. counter — counter specifies the name of the counter to use to create the virtual channel. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels, including counters, for devices installed in the system. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units to use to return the timestamp. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. Seconds (10364) Seconds. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| IRIG-B (10070) | Use the IRIG-B synchronization method. The GPS receiver sends one synchronization pulse per second, as well as information about the number of days, hours, minutes, and seconds that elapsed since the beginning of the current year. |
| None (10230) | Do not synchronize the counter to a GPS receiver. The timestamp measurement returns the number of seconds that elapsed since the device powered up unless you set the CI.Timestamp.InitialSeconds property. |
| PPS (10080) | Use the PPS synchronization method. The GPS receiver sends one synchronization pulse per second, but does not send any timing information. The timestamp measurement returns the number of seconds that elapsed since the device powered up unless you set the CI.Timestamp.InitialSeconds property. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| Seconds (10364) | Seconds. |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-period-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02166: DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Period) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-period-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-period-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates a channel to measure the period of a digital signal. With the exception of devices that support multi-counter tasks, you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple counters simultaneously,

### DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Period) VI

Creates a channel to [measure the period of a digital signal](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_measperiod). With the exception of devices that support [multi-counter tasks](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxdevconsid_multicounterdevices), you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple counters simultaneously, use a separate task for each counter. Connect the input signal to the [default input terminal](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxdevconsid_counteroverview) of the counter unless you select a different input terminal.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ci-period-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| divisor — divisor is the value by which to divide the input signal when measurement method is Large Range with 2 Counters. Leave this input unwired if measurement method is not Large Range with 2 Counters. The larger the divisor, the more accurate the measurement. However, too large a value could cause the count register to roll over, which results in an incorrect measurement. measurement method — measurement method specifies the method to use to calculate the period or frequency of the signal. High Frequency with 2 Counters (10157) Use two counters to count pulses of the signal to measure during measurement time. Large Range with 2 Counters (10205) Use one counter to divide the frequency of the input signal by divisor to create a lower-frequency signal that the second counter can more easily measure. Low Frequency with 1 Counter (10105) Use one counter that uses a constant timebase to measure the input signal. Dynamic Averaging (16065) Uses one counter and automatically configures the counter settings based on the range of frequencies to be measured. During the acquisition, the counter dynamically adjusts the number of periods that are averaged to balance measurement accuracy and latency. minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. counter — counter specifies the name of the counter to use to create the virtual channel. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels, including counters, for devices installed in the system. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units to use to return time or period measurements. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. Seconds (10364) Seconds. Ticks (10304) Timebase ticks. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. starting edge — starting edge specifies between which edges to measure the frequency or period of the signal. Falling (10171) Measure between falling edges. Rising (10280) Measure between rising edges. measurement time — measurement time is the length of time in seconds to measure the frequency or period of the signal if measurement method is High Frequency with 2 Counters. Leave this input unwired if measurement method is not High Frequency with 2 Counters. Measurement accuracy increases with increased measurement time and with increased signal frequency. If you measure a high-frequency signal for too long, however, the count register could roll over, which results in an incorrect measurement. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| High Frequency with 2 Counters (10157) | Use two counters to count pulses of the signal to measure during measurement time. |
| Large Range with 2 Counters (10205) | Use one counter to divide the frequency of the input signal by divisor to create a lower-frequency signal that the second counter can more easily measure. |
| Low Frequency with 1 Counter (10105) | Use one counter that uses a constant timebase to measure the input signal. |
| Dynamic Averaging (16065) | Uses one counter and automatically configures the counter settings based on the range of frequencies to be measured. During the acquisition, the counter dynamically adjusts the number of periods that are averaged to balance measurement accuracy and latency. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| Seconds (10364) | Seconds. |
| Ticks (10304) | Timebase ticks. |
| Falling (10171) | Measure between falling edges. |
| Rising (10280) | Measure between rising edges. |

DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI

## TOPIC 02167: DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Position-Angular Encoder) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-position-angular-encoder-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-position-angular-encoder-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates a channel that uses an angular encoder to measure angular position. With the exception of devices that support multi-counter tasks, you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple counters s

### DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Position-Angular Encoder) VI

Creates a channel that uses an [angular encoder](/csh?context=nidaqmx_measfunds_quadencoders) to [measure angular position](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_angdisplacervdt). With the exception of devices that support [multi-counter tasks](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxdevconsid_multicounterdevices), you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple counters simultaneously, use a separate task for each counter. Connect the input signals to the [default input terminals](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxdevconsid_counteroverview) of the counter unless you select different input terminals.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ci-position-angular-encoder-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| decoding type — decoding type specifies how to count and interpret the pulses the encoder generates on signal A and signal B. X1, X2, and X4 are valid for quadrature encoders only. Two Pulse Counting is valid only for two-pulse encoders. X2 and X4 decoding are more sensitive to smaller changes in position than X1 encoding, with X4 being the most sensitive. However, more sensitive decoding is more likely to produce erroneous measurements if vibration exists in the encoder or other noise exists in the signals. Two Pulse Counting (10313) Increment the count on rising edges of signal A. Decrement the count on rising edges of signal B. If you select this value, z index phase is ignored. X1 (10090) If signal A leads signal B, count the rising edges of signal A. If signal B leads signal A, count the falling edges of signal A. X2 (10091) Count the rising and falling edges of signal A. X4 (10092) Count the rising and falling edges of signal A and signal B. z index phase — z index phase specifies the states at which signal A and signal B must be while signal Z is high for NI-DAQmx to reset the measurement. If signal Z is never high while signal A and signal B are high, for example, you must choose a phase other than A High B High. When signal Z transitions to high and how long it stays high varies from encoder to encoder. Refer to the documentation for the encoder to determine the timing of signal Z with respect to signal A and signal B. A High B High (10040) Reset the measurement when signal A and signal B are high. A High B Low (10041) Reset the measurement when signal A is high and signal B is low. A Low B High (10042) Reset the measurement when signal A is low and signal B high. A Low B Low (10043) Reset the measurement when signal A and signal B are low. z index value — z index value specifies in units the value to which to reset the measurement when signal Z is high and signal A and signal B are at the states you specify with z index phase. z index enable — z index enable specifies whether to use Z indexing for the channel. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. counter — counter specifies the name of the counter to use to create the virtual channel. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels, including counters, for devices installed in the system. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units to use to return angular position measurements from the channel. Degrees (10146) Degrees. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. Radians (10273) Radians. Ticks (10304) Timebase ticks. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. pulses per revolution — pulses per revolution is the number of pulses the encoder generates per revolution. This value is the number of pulses on either signal A or signal B, not the total number of pulses on both signal A and signal B. initial angle — initial angle is the starting angle of the encoder. This value is in the units you specify with the units input. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Two Pulse Counting (10313) | Increment the count on rising edges of signal A. Decrement the count on rising edges of signal B. If you select this value, z index phase is ignored. |
| X1 (10090) | If signal A leads signal B, count the rising edges of signal A. If signal B leads signal A, count the falling edges of signal A. |
| X2 (10091) | Count the rising and falling edges of signal A. |
| X4 (10092) | Count the rising and falling edges of signal A and signal B. |
| A High B High (10040) | Reset the measurement when signal A and signal B are high. |
| A High B Low (10041) | Reset the measurement when signal A is high and signal B is low. |
| A Low B High (10042) | Reset the measurement when signal A is low and signal B high. |
| A Low B Low (10043) | Reset the measurement when signal A and signal B are low. |
| Degrees (10146) | Degrees. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| Radians (10273) | Radians. |
| Ticks (10304) | Timebase ticks. |

## TOPIC 02168: DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Position-Linear Encoder) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-position-linear-encoder-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-position-linear-encoder-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates a channel that uses a linear encoder to measure linear position. With the exception of devices that support multi-counter tasks, you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple counters simu

### DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Position-Linear Encoder) VI

Creates a channel that uses a [linear encoder](/csh?context=nidaqmx_measfunds_twopulse) to [measure linear position](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_lindisplacelvdt). With the exception of devices that support [multi-counter tasks](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxdevconsid_multicounterdevices), you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple counters simultaneously, use a separate task for each counter. Connect the input signals to the [default input terminals](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxdevconsid_counteroverview) of the counter unless you select different input terminals.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ci-position-linear-encoder-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| decoding type — decoding type specifies how to count and interpret the pulses the encoder generates on signal A and signal B. X1, X2, and X4 are valid for quadrature encoders only. Two Pulse Counting is valid only for two-pulse encoders. X2 and X4 decoding are more sensitive to smaller changes in position than X1 encoding, with X4 being the most sensitive. However, more sensitive decoding is more likely to produce erroneous measurements if vibration exists in the encoder or other noise exists in the signals. Two Pulse Counting (10313) Increment the count on rising edges of signal A. Decrement the count on rising edges of signal B. If you select this value, z index phase is ignored. X1 (10090) If signal A leads signal B, count the rising edges of signal A. If signal B leads signal A, count the falling edges of signal A. X2 (10091) Count the rising and falling edges of signal A. X4 (10092) Count the rising and falling edges of signal A and signal B. z index phase — z index phase specifies the states at which signal A and signal B must be while signal Z is high for NI-DAQmx to reset the measurement. If signal Z is never high while signal A and signal B are high, for example, you must choose a phase other than A High B High. When signal Z transitions to high and how long it stays high varies from encoder to encoder. Refer to the documentation for the encoder to determine the timing of signal Z with respect to signal A and signal B. A High B High (10040) Reset the measurement when signal A and signal B are high. A High B Low (10041) Reset the measurement when signal A is high and signal B is low. A Low B High (10042) Reset the measurement when signal A is low and signal B high. A Low B Low (10043) Reset the measurement when signal A and signal B are low. z index value — z index value specifies in units the value to which to reset the measurement when signal Z is high and signal A and signal B are at the states you specify with z index phase. z index enable — z index enable specifies whether to use Z indexing for the channel. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. counter — counter specifies the name of the counter to use to create the virtual channel. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels, including counters, for devices installed in the system. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units to use to return linear position measurements from the channel. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. Inches (10379) Inches. Meters (10219) Meters. Ticks (10304) Timebase ticks. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. distance per pulse — distance per pulse is the distance to measure for each pulse the encoder generates on signal A or signal B. This value is in the units you specify with the units input. initial position — initial position is the position of the encoder when you begin the measurement. This value is in the units you specify with the units input. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Two Pulse Counting (10313) | Increment the count on rising edges of signal A. Decrement the count on rising edges of signal B. If you select this value, z index phase is ignored. |
| X1 (10090) | If signal A leads signal B, count the rising edges of signal A. If signal B leads signal A, count the falling edges of signal A. |
| X2 (10091) | Count the rising and falling edges of signal A. |
| X4 (10092) | Count the rising and falling edges of signal A and signal B. |
| A High B High (10040) | Reset the measurement when signal A and signal B are high. |
| A High B Low (10041) | Reset the measurement when signal A is high and signal B is low. |
| A Low B High (10042) | Reset the measurement when signal A is low and signal B high. |
| A Low B Low (10043) | Reset the measurement when signal A and signal B are low. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| Inches (10379) | Inches. |
| Meters (10219) | Meters. |
| Ticks (10304) | Timebase ticks. |
