## TOPIC 02169: DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Pulse Freq) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-pulse-freq-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-pulse-freq-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates a channel to measure pulse specifications, returning the measurements as pairs of frequency and duty cycle. With the exception of devices that support multi-counter tasks, you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input c

### DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Pulse Freq) VI

Creates a channel to measure [pulse specifications](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_configtimemeas), returning the measurements as pairs of frequency and duty cycle. With the exception of devices that support [multi-counter tasks](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxdevconsid_multicounterdevices), you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple counters simultaneously, use a separate task for each counter. Connect the input signal to the [default input terminal](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxdevconsid_counteroverview) of the counter unless you select a different input terminal.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ci-pulse-freq-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. counter — counter specifies the name of the counter to use to create the virtual channel. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels, including counters, for devices installed in the system. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units to use to return pulse specifications in terms of frequency. Hz (10373) Hertz. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Hz (10373) | Hertz. |

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-pulse-ticks-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02170: DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Pulse Ticks) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-pulse-ticks-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-pulse-ticks-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates a channel to measure pulse specifications, returning the measurements as pairs of high ticks and low ticks. With the exception of devices that support multi-counter tasks, you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input c

### DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Pulse Ticks) VI

Creates a channel to measure [pulse specifications](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_configtimemeas), returning the measurements as pairs of high ticks and low ticks. With the exception of devices that support [multi-counter tasks](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxdevconsid_multicounterdevices), you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple counters simultaneously, use a separate task for each counter. Connect the input signal to the [default input terminal](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxdevconsid_counteroverview) of the counter unless you select a different input terminal.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ci-pulse-ticks-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. counter — counter specifies the name of the counter to use to create the virtual channel. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels, including counters, for devices installed in the system. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. source of ticks — source of ticks is the terminal to which you connect a signal to use as the source of ticks. A DAQmx terminal constant lists all terminals available on devices installed in the system. You also can specify a source terminal by wiring a string that contains a terminal name. If you specify OnboardClock, or do not specify any terminal, NI-DAQmx selects the fastest onboard timebase available on the device. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-pulse-time-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02171: DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Pulse Time) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-pulse-time-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-pulse-time-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates a channel to measure pulse specifications, returning the measurements as pairs of high time and low time. With the exception of devices that support multi-counter tasks, you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input cha

### DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Pulse Time) VI

Creates a channel to measure [pulse specifications](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_configtimemeas), returning the measurements as pairs of high time and low time. With the exception of devices that support [multi-counter tasks](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxdevconsid_multicounterdevices), you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple counters simultaneously, use a separate task for each counter. Connect the input signal to the [default input terminal](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxdevconsid_counteroverview) of the counter unless you select a different input terminal.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ci-pulse-time-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. counter — counter specifies the name of the counter to use to create the virtual channel. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels, including counters, for devices installed in the system. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units to use to return pulse specifications in terms of high time and low time. Seconds (10364) Seconds. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Seconds (10364) | Seconds. |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-pulse-width-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02172: DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Pulse Width) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-pulse-width-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-pulse-width-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates a channel to measure the width of a digital pulse. starting edge determines whether to measure a high pulse or low pulse. With the exception of devices that support multi-counter tasks, you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one c

### DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Pulse Width) VI

Creates a channel to [measure the width of a digital pulse](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_measperiod). **starting edge** determines whether to measure a high pulse or low pulse. With the exception of devices that support [multi-counter tasks](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxdevconsid_multicounterdevices), you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple counters simultaneously, use a separate task for each counter. Connect the input signal to the [default input terminal](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxdevconsid_counteroverview) of the counter unless you select a different input terminal.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ci-pulse-width-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. counter — counter specifies the name of the counter to use to create the virtual channel. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels, including counters, for devices installed in the system. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units to use to return time or period measurements. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. Seconds (10364) Seconds. Ticks (10304) Timebase ticks. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. starting edge — starting edge specifies on which edge to begin measuring pulse width. Falling (10171) Begin measuring on the falling edge. The channel measures the width of low pulses. Rising (10280) Begin measuring on the rising edge. The channel measures the width of high pulses. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| Seconds (10364) | Seconds. |
| Ticks (10304) | Timebase ticks. |
| Falling (10171) | Begin measuring on the falling edge. The channel measures the width of low pulses. |
| Rising (10280) | Begin measuring on the rising edge. The channel measures the width of high pulses. |

With the exception of devices that support multi-counter tasks, you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple c

### DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Semi Period) VI

Creates a channel to [measure the time between state transitions of a digital signal](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_measperiod). With the exception of devices that support [multi-counter tasks](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxdevconsid_multicounterdevices), you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple counters simultaneously, use a separate task for each counter. Connect the input signal to the [default input terminal](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxdevconsid_counteroverview) of the counter unless you select a different input terminal.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ci-semi-period-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. counter — counter specifies the name of the counter to use to create the virtual channel. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels, including counters, for devices installed in the system. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units to use to return time or period measurements. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. Seconds (10364) Seconds. Ticks (10304) Timebase ticks. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |

| Ticks (10304) | Timebase ticks. |

## TOPIC 02174: DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Two Edge Separation) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-two-edge-separation-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-two-edge-separation-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates a channel that measures the amount of time between the rising or falling edge of one digital signal and the rising or falling edge of another digital signal. With the exception of devices that support multi-counter tasks, you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI b

### DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Two Edge Separation) VI

Creates a channel that [measures the amount of time between the rising or falling edge of one digital signal and the rising or falling edge of another digital signal](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_measperiod). With the exception of devices that support [multi-counter tasks](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxdevconsid_multicounterdevices), you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple counters simultaneously, use a separate task for each counter. Connect the input signals to the [default input terminals](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxdevconsid_countersigcon) of the counter unless you select different input terminals.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ci-two-edge-separation-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. counter — counter specifies the name of the counter to use to create the virtual channel. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels, including counters, for devices installed in the system. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units to use to return time or period measurements. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. Seconds (10364) Seconds. Ticks (10304) Timebase ticks. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. first edge — first edge specifies on which edge of the first signal to start each measurement. Falling (10171) Start each measurement on the falling edge of the first signal. Rising (10280) Start each measurement on the rising edge of the first signal. second edge — second edge specifies on which edge of the second signal to stop each measurement. Falling (10171) Stop each measurement on the falling edge of the second signal. Rising (10280) Stop each measurement on the rising edge of the second signal. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| Seconds (10364) | Seconds. |
| Ticks (10304) | Timebase ticks. |
| Falling (10171) | Start each measurement on the falling edge of the first signal. |
| Rising (10280) | Start each measurement on the rising edge of the first signal. |
| Falling (10171) | Stop each measurement on the falling edge of the second signal. |
| Rising (10280) | Stop each measurement on the rising edge of the second signal. |

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-velocity-angular-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02175: DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Velocity-Angular) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-velocity-angular-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-velocity-angular-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates a channel to measure the angular velocity of a digital signal. With the exception of devices that support multi-counter tasks, you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple counters simult

### DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Velocity-Angular) VI

Creates a channel to [measure the angular velocity](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_velocity) of a digital signal. With the exception of devices that support [multi-counter tasks](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxdevconsid_multicounterdevices), you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple counters simultaneously, use a separate task for each counter. Connect the input signal to the [default input terminal](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxdevconsid_counteroverview) of the counter unless you select a different input terminal.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ci-velocity-angular-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| decoding type — decoding type specifies how to count and interpret the pulses the encoder generates on signal A and signal B. X1, X2, and X4 are valid for quadrature encoders only. Two Pulse Counting is valid only for two-pulse encoders. X2 and X4 decoding are more sensitive to smaller changes in position than X1 encoding, with X4 being the most sensitive. However, more sensitive decoding is more likely to produce erroneous measurements if vibration exists in the encoder or other noise exists in the signals. Two Pulse Counting (10313) Increment the count on rising edges of signal A. Decrement the count on rising edges of signal B. If you select this value, z index phase is ignored. X1 (10090) If signal A leads signal B, count the rising edges of signal A. If signal B leads signal A, count the falling edges of signal A. X2 (10091) Count the rising and falling edges of signal A. X4 (10092) Count the rising and falling edges of signal A and signal B. minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. counter — counter specifies the name of the counter to use to create the virtual channel. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels, including counters, for devices installed in the system. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies in which unit to return velocity measurements from the channel. RPM (16080) Revolutions per minute. Radians/s (16081) Radians per second. Degrees/s (16082) Degrees per second. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. pulses per revolution — pulses per revolution is the number of pulses the encoder generates per revolution. This value is the number of pulses on either signal A or signal B, not the total number of pulses on both signal A and signal B. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Two Pulse Counting (10313) | Increment the count on rising edges of signal A. Decrement the count on rising edges of signal B. If you select this value, z index phase is ignored. |
| X1 (10090) | If signal A leads signal B, count the rising edges of signal A. If signal B leads signal A, count the falling edges of signal A. |
| X2 (10091) | Count the rising and falling edges of signal A. |
| X4 (10092) | Count the rising and falling edges of signal A and signal B. |
| RPM (16080) | Revolutions per minute. |
| Radians/s (16081) | Radians per second. |
| Degrees/s (16082) | Degrees per second. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-velocity-linear-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02176: DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Velocity-Linear) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-velocity-linear-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-velocity-linear-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates a channel that uses a linear encoder to measure linear velocity. With the exception of devices that support multi-counter tasks, you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple counters simu

### DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Velocity-Linear) VI

Creates a channel that uses a linear encoder to [measure linear velocity](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_velocity). With the exception of devices that support [multi-counter tasks](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxdevconsid_multicounterdevices), you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple counters simultaneously, use a separate task for each counter. Connect the input signal to the [default input terminal](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxdevconsid_counteroverview) of the counter unless you select a different input terminal.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-ci-velocity-linear-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| decoding type — decoding type specifies how to count and interpret the pulses the encoder generates on signal A and signal B. X1, X2, and X4 are valid for quadrature encoders only. Two Pulse Counting is valid only for two-pulse encoders. X2 and X4 decoding are more sensitive to smaller changes in position than X1 encoding, with X4 being the most sensitive. However, more sensitive decoding is more likely to produce erroneous measurements if vibration exists in the encoder or other noise exists in the signals. Two Pulse Counting (10313) Increment the count on rising edges of signal A. Decrement the count on rising edges of signal B. If you select this value, z index phase is ignored. X1 (10090) If signal A leads signal B, count the rising edges of signal A. If signal B leads signal A, count the falling edges of signal A. X2 (10091) Count the rising and falling edges of signal A. X4 (10092) Count the rising and falling edges of signal A and signal B. minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. counter — counter specifies the name of the counter to use to create the virtual channel. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels, including counters, for devices installed in the system. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies in which unit to return velocity measurements from the channel. in/s (15960) Inches per second. m/s (15959) Meters per second. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. distance per pulse — distance per pulse is the distance to measure for each pulse the encoder generates on signal A or signal B. This value is in the units you specify with the units input. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Two Pulse Counting (10313) | Increment the count on rising edges of signal A. Decrement the count on rising edges of signal B. If you select this value, z index phase is ignored. |
| X1 (10090) | If signal A leads signal B, count the rising edges of signal A. If signal B leads signal A, count the falling edges of signal A. |
| X2 (10091) | Count the rising and falling edges of signal A. |
| X4 (10092) | Count the rising and falling edges of signal A and signal B. |
| in/s (15960) | Inches per second. |
| m/s (15959) | Meters per second. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-co-pulse-generation-frequency-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02177: DAQmx Create Channel (CO-Pulse Generation-Frequency) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-co-pulse-generation-frequency-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-co-pulse-generation-frequency-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) to generate digital pulses that frequency and duty cycle define. The pulses appear on the default output terminal of the counter unless you select a different output terminal. icon Inputs/Outputs cdbl.png duty cycle duty cycle is the width of the pulse divided by the pulse period.

### DAQmx Create Channel (CO-Pulse Generation-Frequency) VI

Creates channel(s) to [generate digital pulses](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_generating_pulses) that **frequency** and **duty cycle** define. The pulses appear on the [default output terminal](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxdevconsid_counteroverview) of the counter unless you select a different output terminal.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-co-pulse-generation-frequency-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| duty cycle — duty cycle is the width of the pulse divided by the pulse period. NI-DAQmx uses this ratio combined with frequency to determine pulse width and the interval between pulses. frequency — frequency specifies at what frequency to generate pulses. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. counter — counter specifies the names of the counters to use to create the virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels, including counters, for devices installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of counters to this input. If you have an array of counters, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units in which to define pulse frequency. Hz (10373) Hertz. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. idle state — idle state specifies the resting state of the output terminal. High (10192) Terminal is at a high state at rest. Low (10214) Terminal is at a low state at rest. initial delay — initial delay is the amount of time in seconds to wait before generating the first pulse. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Hz (10373) | Hertz. |
| High (10192) | Terminal is at a high state at rest. |
| Low (10214) | Terminal is at a low state at rest. |

## TOPIC 02178: DAQmx Create Channel (CO-Pulse Generation-Ticks) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-co-pulse-generation-ticks-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-co-pulse-generation-ticks-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) to generate digital pulses defined by the number of timebase ticks that the pulse is at a high state and the number of timebase ticks that the pulse is at a low state. The pulses appear on the default output terminal of the counter unless you select a different output terminal. ic

### DAQmx Create Channel (CO-Pulse Generation-Ticks) VI

Creates channel(s) to [generate digital pulses](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_generating_pulses) defined by the number of timebase ticks that the pulse is at a high state and the number of timebase ticks that the pulse is at a low state. The pulses appear on the [default output terminal](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxdevconsid_counteroverview) of the counter unless you select a different output terminal.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-co-pulse-generation-ticks-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| low ticks — low ticks is the number of ticks the pulse is low. high ticks — high ticks is the number of ticks the pulse is high. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. counter — counter specifies the names of the counters to use to create the virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels, including counters, for devices installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of counters to this input. If you have an array of counters, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. source of ticks — source of ticks is the terminal to which you connect an external timebase. A DAQmx terminal constant lists all terminals available on devices installed in the system. You also can specify a source terminal by wiring a string that contains a terminal name. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. idle state — idle state specifies the resting state of the output terminal. High (10192) Terminal is at a high state at rest. Low (10214) Terminal is at a low state at rest. initial delay — initial delay is the number of timebase ticks to wait before generating the first pulse. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| High (10192) | Terminal is at a high state at rest. |
| Low (10214) | Terminal is at a low state at rest. |

## TOPIC 02179: DAQmx Create Channel (CO-Pulse Generation-Time) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-co-pulse-generation-time-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-co-pulse-generation-time-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`

- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) to generate digital pulses defined by the amount of time the pulse is at a high state and the amount of time the pulse is at a low state. The pulses appear on the default output terminal of the counter unless you select a different output terminal. icon Inputs/Outputs cdbl.png low

### DAQmx Create Channel (CO-Pulse Generation-Time) VI

Creates channel(s) to [generate digital pulses](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_generating_pulses) defined by the amount of time the pulse is at a high state and the amount of time the pulse is at a low state. The pulses appear on the [default output terminal](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxdevconsid_counteroverview) of the counter unless you select a different output terminal.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-co-pulse-generation-time-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| low time — low time is the amount of time the pulse is low. high time — high time is the amount of time the pulse is high. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. counter — counter specifies the names of the counters to use to create the virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels, including counters, for devices installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of counters to this input. If you have an array of counters, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units in which to define pulse high and low time. Seconds (10364) Seconds. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. idle state — idle state specifies the resting state of the output terminal. High (10192) Terminal is at a high state at rest. Low (10214) Terminal is at a low state at rest. initial delay — initial delay is the amount of time in seconds to wait before generating the first pulse. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Seconds (10364) | Seconds. |
| High (10192) | Terminal is at a high state at rest. |
| Low (10214) | Terminal is at a low state at rest. |

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-di-digital-input-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02180: DAQmx Create Channel (DI-Digital Input) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-di-digital-input-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-di-digital-input-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) to measure digital signals. You can group digital lines into one digital channel or separate them into multiple digital channels. If you specify one or more entire ports in the lines input by using port physical channel names, you cannot separate the ports into multiple channels.

### DAQmx Create Channel (DI-Digital Input) VI

Creates channel(s) to [measure digital signals](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_diglevelmeasgen). You can group digital [lines](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_linesports) into one [digital channel](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_virtchantypes) or separate them into multiple digital channels. If you specify one or more entire [ports](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_linesports) in the **lines** input by using port physical channel names, you cannot separate the ports into multiple channels. To separate ports into multiple channels, use this VI multiple times with a different port each time.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-di-digital-input-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. lines — lines specifies the names of the digital lines or ports to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all lines and ports for devices installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of digital lines or ports to this input. If you have an array of lines or ports, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. line grouping — line grouping specifies how to group digital lines into one or more virtual channels. If you specify one or more entire ports with the lines input, you must set this input to one channel for all lines. one channel for all lines (1) Combine all digital lines into a single virtual channel. one channel for each line (0) Create a separate virtual channel for each digital line. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| one channel for all lines (1) | Combine all digital lines into a single virtual channel. |
| one channel for each line (0) | Create a separate virtual channel for each digital line. |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-do-digital-output-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02181: DAQmx Create Channel (DO-Digital Output) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-do-digital-output-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-do-digital-output-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) to generate digital signals. You can group digital lines into one digital channel or separate them into multiple digital channels. If you specify one or more entire ports in lines input by using port physical channel names, you cannot separate the ports into multiple channels. To

### DAQmx Create Channel (DO-Digital Output) VI

Creates channel(s) to [generate digital signals](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_diglevelmeasgen). You can group digital [lines](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_linesports) into one [digital channel](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_virtchantypes) or separate them into multiple digital channels. If you specify one or more entire [ports](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_linesports) in **lines** input by using port physical channel names, you cannot separate the ports into multiple channels. To separate ports into multiple channels, use this VI multiple times with a different port each time.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-do-digital-output-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. lines — lines specifies the names of the digital lines or ports to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all lines and ports for devices installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of digital lines or ports to this input. Specifying a port and no lines is the equivalent of specifying all the lines of that port in order. Therefore, if you specify Dev1/port0 and port 0 has eight lines, this is expanded to Dev1/port0/line0:7. If you have an array of lines or ports, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. If you specify multiple lines, the order in which you specify the lines affects the order of the output with DAQmx Write. The first line specified is the most significant bit, and the last line specified is the least significant bit. Therefore, if the data you specify to write is 110, and the line order you specify is Dev1/port0/line0:2, the output is: Dev1/port0/line0 1 Dev1/port0/line1 1 Dev1/port0/line2 0 If the data you specify to write is 110 and the line order you specify is Dev1/port0/line2:0, line 2 is considered the most significant bit. Therefore, the output is: Dev1/port0/line0 0 Dev1/port0/line1 1 Dev1/port0/line2 1 name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. line grouping — line grouping specifies how to group digital lines into one or more virtual channels. If you specify one or more entire ports with the lines input, you must set this input to one channel for all lines. one channel for all lines (1) Combine all digital lines into a single virtual channel. one channel for each line (0) Create a separate virtual channel for each digital line. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Dev1/port0/line0 | 1 |
| Dev1/port0/line1 | 1 |
| Dev1/port0/line2 | 0 |
| Dev1/port0/line0 | 0 |
| Dev1/port0/line1 | 1 |
| Dev1/port0/line2 | 1 |
| one channel for all lines (1) | Combine all digital lines into a single virtual channel. |
| one channel for each line (0) | Create a separate virtual channel for each digital line. |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-power-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02182: DAQmx Create Channel (Power) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-power-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-power-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) to source and measure power. icon Inputs/Outputs cdbl.png current setpoint current setpoint sets the constant output current, in amperes. If the load draws current greater than the specified value, output voltage is reduced and the device operates in constant current mode. Can be

### DAQmx Create Channel (Power) VI

Creates channel(s) to source and measure power.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-power-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| current setpoint — current setpoint sets the constant output current, in amperes. If the load draws current greater than the specified value, output voltage is reduced and the device operates in constant current mode. Can be set while a task is in a committed state or running. Can be read at any time during a task. voltage setpoint — voltage setpoint sets the constant output voltage, in volts. Can be set while a task is in a committed state or running. Can be read at any time during a task. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. output enable — output enable enables or disables power module output. Can be set while a task is in a committed state or running. Can be read at any time during a task. When a task is not running, enabling the output does not take effect until the task is committed. When task is in committed state or running, the effect is immediate. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-acceleration-accelerometer-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02183: DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Acceleration-Accelerometer) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-acceleration-accelerometer-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-acceleration-accelerometer-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) that use an accelerometer to measure acceleration. You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI. icon Inputs/Outputs cdbl.png current excitation value current excitation value specifies in amperes the amount of excitation to supply to the sensor.

### DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Acceleration-Accelerometer) VI

Creates channel(s) that use an [accelerometer](/csh?context=nidaqmx_measfunds_accelerometers) to [measure acceleration](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_accel). You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-acceleration-accelerometer-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| current excitation value — current excitation value specifies in amperes the amount of excitation to supply to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine this value. current excitation source — current excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. input terminal configuration — input terminal configuration specifies the input terminal configuration for the channel. default (-1) At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. Differential (10106) Differential mode. NRSE (10078) Non-referenced single-ended mode. Pseudodifferential (12529) Pseudodifferential mode. RSE (10083) Referenced single-ended mode. minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units to use to return acceleration measurements from the channel. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. g (10186) 1 g is approximately equal to 9.81 m/s/s. m/s^2 (12470) Meters per second per second. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| External (10167) | Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| Internal (10200) | Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| None (10230) | Supply no excitation to the channel. |
| default (-1) | At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. |
| Differential (10106) | Differential mode. |
| NRSE (10078) | Non-referenced single-ended mode. |
| Pseudodifferential (12529) | Pseudodifferential mode. |
| RSE (10083) | Referenced single-ended mode. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| g (10186) | 1 g is approximately equal to 9.81 m/s/s. |
| m/s^2 (12470) | Meters per second per second. |

DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI
