## TOPIC 02184: DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Bridge) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-bridge-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-bridge-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) that measure a Wheatstone bridge. You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI. Use this instance with bridge-based sensors that measure phenomena other than strain, force, pressure, or torque, or that scale data to physical units NI-DAQmx does n

### DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Bridge) VI

Creates channel(s) that measure a [Wheatstone bridge](/csh?context=nidaqmx_measfunds_bridgeforcepressuretorque). 
 You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI. 
 Use this instance with bridge-based sensors that measure phenomena other than strain, force, pressure, or torque, or that scale data to physical units NI-DAQmx does not support.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-bridge-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies in which unit to return measurements from the channel. From TEDS (12516) Units defined by TEDS information associated with the channel. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. voltage excitation source — voltage excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. voltage excitation value — voltage excitation value specifies in volts the amount of excitation supplied to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine appropriate excitation values. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| From TEDS (12516) | Units defined by TEDS information associated with the channel. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| External (10167) | Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| Internal (10200) | Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| None (10230) | Supply no excitation to the channel. |

## TOPIC 02185: DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Current-Basic) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-current-basic-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-current-basic-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) to measure current. You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI. icon Inputs/Outputs cdbl.png external shunt resistor value external shunt resistor value specifies in ohms the resistance of an external shunt resistor. ci32.png input terminal con

### DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Current-Basic) VI

Creates channel(s) to [measure current](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_measuring_current). You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-current-basic-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| external shunt resistor value — external shunt resistor value specifies in ohms the resistance of an external shunt resistor. input terminal configuration — input terminal configuration specifies the input terminal configuration for the channel. default (-1) At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. Differential (10106) Differential mode. NRSE (10078) Non-referenced single-ended mode. Pseudodifferential (12529) Pseudodifferential mode. RSE (10083) Referenced single-ended mode. minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units to use to return measurements. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. From TEDS (12516) Units defined by TEDS information associated with the channel. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. shunt resistor location — shunt resistor location specifies the location of the shunt resistor. For devices with built-in shunt resistors, specify the location as Internal. For devices that do not have built-in shunt resistors, you must attach an external one, set this input to External and use the external shunt resistor value input to specify the value of the resistor. default (-1) NI-DAQmx selects Internal for devices with an internal shunt resistor. NI-DAQmx selects External for devices without an internal shunt resistor. External (10167) Use a shunt resistor external to the device. You must use the external shunt resistor value input to specify the value of the shunt resistor. Internal (10200) Use the built-in shunt resistor of the device. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| default (-1) | At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. |
| Differential (10106) | Differential mode. |
| NRSE (10078) | Non-referenced single-ended mode. |
| Pseudodifferential (12529) | Pseudodifferential mode. |
| RSE (10083) | Referenced single-ended mode. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| From TEDS (12516) | Units defined by TEDS information associated with the channel. |
| default (-1) | NI-DAQmx selects Internal for devices with an internal shunt resistor. NI-DAQmx selects External for devices without an internal shunt resistor. |
| External (10167) | Use a shunt resistor external to the device. You must use the external shunt resistor value input to specify the value of the shunt resistor. |
| Internal (10200) | Use the built-in shunt resistor of the device. |

## TOPIC 02186: DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Force-Bridge) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`

NI-DAQmx scales electrical values to physical values according to that TEDS information. icon Inputs/Outputs cdbl.png minimum value minimum value

### DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Force-Bridge) VI

Creates channel(s) that use a [Wheatstone bridge](/csh?context=nidaqmx_measfunds_bridgeforcepressuretorque) to [measure force or load](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_force). 
 You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI. NI-DAQmx scales electrical values to physical values according to that TEDS information.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-force-bridge-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies in which unit to return force measurements from the channel. Newtons (15875) Newtons. Pounds (15876) Pounds. kgf (15877) Kilograms-force From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. voltage excitation source — voltage excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. voltage excitation value — voltage excitation value specifies in volts the amount of excitation supplied to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine appropriate excitation values. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Newtons (15875) | Newtons. |
| Pounds (15876) | Pounds. |
| kgf (15877) | Kilograms-force |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| External (10167) | Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| Internal (10200) | Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| None (10230) | Supply no excitation to the channel. |

## TOPIC 02187: DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Force-IEPE Sensor) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-force-iepe-sensor-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-force-iepe-sensor-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) that use an IEPE force sensor to measure force or load. You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI. icon Inputs/Outputs cdbl.png current excitation value current excitation value specifies in amperes the amount of excitation to supply to the se

### DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Force-IEPE Sensor) VI

Creates channel(s) that use an [IEPE force sensor](/csh?context=nidaqmx_measfunds_forcepiezo) to [measure force or load](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_force). You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-force-iepe-sensor-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| current excitation value — current excitation value specifies in amperes the amount of excitation to supply to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine this value. current excitation source — current excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. input terminal configuration — input terminal configuration specifies the input terminal configuration for the channel. default (-1) At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. Differential (10106) Differential mode. NRSE (10078) Non-referenced single-ended mode. Pseudodifferential (12529) Pseudodifferential mode. RSE (10083) Referenced single-ended mode. minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies in which unit to return force measurements from the channel. Newtons (15875) Newtons. Pounds (15876) Pounds. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| External (10167) | Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| Internal (10200) | Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| None (10230) | Supply no excitation to the channel. |
| default (-1) | At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. |
| Differential (10106) | Differential mode. |
| NRSE (10078) | Non-referenced single-ended mode. |
| Pseudodifferential (12529) | Pseudodifferential mode. |
| RSE (10083) | Referenced single-ended mode. |
| Newtons (15875) | Newtons. |
| Pounds (15876) | Pounds. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |

## TOPIC 02188: DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Position-LVDT) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-position-lvdt-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-position-lvdt-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) that use an LVDT to measure linear position. You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI. icon Inputs/Outputs cdbl.png voltage excitation frequency voltage excitation frequency specifies in hertz the excitation frequency that the sensor requires

### DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Position-LVDT) VI

Creates channel(s) that use an [LVDT](/csh?context=nidaqmx_measfunds_lvdts) to [measure linear position](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_lindisplacelvdt). You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-position-lvdt-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| voltage excitation frequency — voltage excitation frequency specifies in hertz the excitation frequency that the sensor requires. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine this value. voltage excitation value — voltage excitation value specifies in volts the amount of excitation supplied to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine appropriate excitation values. voltage excitation source — voltage excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. voltage excitation wire mode — voltage excitation wire mode is the number of leads on the sensor. Some sensors require you to tie leads together to create a four- or five- wire sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation for more information. 4-Wire (4) Four wires. 5-Wire (5) Five wires. 6-Wire (6) Six wires. minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units to use to return linear position measurements from the channel. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. Inches (10379) Inches. Meters (10219) Meters. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| External (10167) | Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| Internal (10200) | Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| None (10230) | Supply no excitation to the channel. |
| 4-Wire (4) | Four wires. |
| 5-Wire (5) | Five wires. |
| 6-Wire (6) | Six wires. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| Inches (10379) | Inches. |
| Meters (10219) | Meters. |

## TOPIC 02189: DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Position-RVDT) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`

You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI. icon Inputs/Outputs cdbl.png voltage excitation frequency voltage excitation frequency specifies in hertz the excitation frequency that the sensor require

### DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Position-RVDT) VI

Creates channel(s) that use an [RVDT](/csh?context=nidaqmx_measfunds_rvdts) to measure [angular position](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_angdisplacervdt). You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-position-rvdt-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| voltage excitation frequency — voltage excitation frequency specifies in hertz the excitation frequency that the sensor requires. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine this value. voltage excitation value — voltage excitation value specifies in volts the amount of excitation supplied to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine appropriate excitation values. voltage excitation source — voltage excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. voltage excitation wire mode — voltage excitation wire mode is the number of leads on the sensor. Some sensors require you to tie leads together to create a four- or five- wire sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation for more information. 4-Wire (4) Four wires. 5-Wire (5) Five wires. 6-Wire (6) Six wires. minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units to use to return angular position measurements from the channel. Degrees (10146) Degrees. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. Radians (10273) Radians. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |

## TOPIC 02190: DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Pressure-Bridge) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-pressure-bridge-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-pressure-bridge-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) that use a Wheatstone bridge to measure pressure. You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI. NI-DAQmx scales electrical values to physical values according to that TEDS information. icon Inputs/Outputs cdbl.png minimum value minimum value spec

### DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Pressure-Bridge) VI

Creates channel(s) that use a [Wheatstone bridge](/csh?context=nidaqmx_measfunds_bridgeforcepressuretorque) to [measure pressure](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_pressure). 
 You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI. NI-DAQmx scales electrical values to physical values according to that TEDS information.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-pressure-bridge-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies in which unit to return pressure measurements from the channel. psi (15879) Pounds per square inch. bar (15880) Bar. Pascals (10081) Pascals. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. voltage excitation source — voltage excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. voltage excitation value — voltage excitation value specifies in volts the amount of excitation supplied to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine appropriate excitation values. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| psi (15879) | Pounds per square inch. |
| bar (15880) | Bar. |
| Pascals (10081) | Pascals. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| External (10167) | Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| Internal (10200) | Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| None (10230) | Supply no excitation to the channel. |

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`

- source_description: Creates channel(s) to measure resistance. You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI. icon Inputs/Outputs cdbl.png current excitation value current excitation value specifies in amperes the amount of excitation to supply to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documen

### DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Resistance) VI

Creates channel(s) to [measure resistance](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_measuring_resistance). You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-resistance-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| current excitation value — current excitation value specifies in amperes the amount of excitation to supply to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine this value. resistance configuration — resistance configuration specifies the number of wires to use for resistive measurements. 2-Wire (2) 2-Wire mode. 3-Wire (3) 3-Wire mode. 4-Wire (4) 4-Wire mode. minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units to use to return measurements. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. From TEDS (12516) Units defined by TEDS information associated with the channel. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. current excitation source — current excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| 2-Wire (2) | 2-Wire mode. |
| 3-Wire (3) | 3-Wire mode. |
| 4-Wire (4) | 4-Wire mode. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| From TEDS (12516) | Units defined by TEDS information associated with the channel. |
| External (10167) | Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| Internal (10200) | Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| None (10230) | Supply no excitation to the channel. |

## TOPIC 02192: DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Sound Pressure-Microphone) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-sound-pressure-microphone-vi.html`

- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) that use a microphone to measure sound pressure. You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI. icon Inputs/Outputs cdbl.png current excitation value current excitation value specifies in amperes the amount of excitation to supply to the sensor. R

### DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Sound Pressure-Microphone) VI

Creates channel(s) that use a microphone to [measure sound pressure](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_soundpress). You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-sound-pressure-microphone-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| current excitation value — current excitation value specifies in amperes the amount of excitation to supply to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine this value. current excitation source — current excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. input terminal configuration — input terminal configuration specifies the input terminal configuration for the channel. default (-1) At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. Differential (10106) Differential mode. NRSE (10078) Non-referenced single-ended mode. Pseudodifferential (12529) Pseudodifferential mode. RSE (10083) Referenced single-ended mode. maximum sound pressure level — maximum sound pressure level is the maximum instantaneous sound pressure level you expect to measure. This value is in decibels, referenced to 20 micropascals. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. You must use physical channels that you configured with TEDS information. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units to use to return sound pressure measurements. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. Pascals (10081) Pascals. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| External (10167) | Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| Internal (10200) | Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| None (10230) | Supply no excitation to the channel. |
| default (-1) | At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. |
| Differential (10106) | Differential mode. |
| NRSE (10078) | Non-referenced single-ended mode. |
| Pseudodifferential (12529) | Pseudodifferential mode. |
| RSE (10083) | Referenced single-ended mode. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| Pascals (10081) | Pascals. |

## TOPIC 02193: DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Strain-Strain Gage) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-strain-strain-gage-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-strain-strain-gage-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) to measure strain. You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI. icon Inputs/Outputs cdbl.png minimum value minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. cdbl.png maximum value maximum value specifies in units the maxi

### DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Strain-Strain Gage) VI

Creates channel(s) to [measure strain](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_meas_strain). You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-strain-strain-gage-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units to use to return strain measurements. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. Strain (10299) Strain. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. bridge information — bridge information specifies information about the bridge configuration and measurement. lead wire resistance — lead wire resistance is the amount of resistance in ohms in the lead wires. Ideally, this value is the same for all leads. initial bridge voltage — initial bridge voltage specifies in volts the output voltage of the bridge in the unloaded condition. NI-DAQmx subtracts this value from any measurements before applying scaling equations. Perform a voltage measurement on the bridge with no strain applied to determine this value. voltage excitation source — voltage excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. voltage excitation value — voltage excitation value specifies in volts the amount of excitation supplied to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine appropriate excitation values. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| Strain (10299) | Strain. |
| lead wire resistance — lead wire resistance is the amount of resistance in ohms in the lead wires. Ideally, this value is the same for all leads. initial bridge voltage — initial bridge voltage specifies in volts the output voltage of the bridge in the unloaded condition. NI-DAQmx subtracts this value from any measurements before applying scaling equations. Perform a voltage measurement on the bridge with no strain applied to determine this value. voltage excitation source — voltage excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. voltage excitation value — voltage excitation value specifies in volts the amount of excitation supplied to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine appropriate excitation values. |  |
| External (10167) | Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| Internal (10200) | Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| None (10230) | Supply no excitation to the channel. |

## TOPIC 02194: DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Temperature-RTD) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-temperature-rtd-vi.html`

- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) that use an RTD to measure temperature. You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI. icon Inputs/Outputs cdbl.png current excitation value current excitation value specifies in amperes the amount of excitation to supply to the sensor. Refer to t

### DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Temperature-RTD) VI

Creates channel(s) that use an [RTD](/csh?context=nidaqmx_measfunds_rtd) to [measure temperature](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_simtemp). You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-temperature-rtd-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| current excitation value — current excitation value specifies in amperes the amount of excitation to supply to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine this value. resistance configuration — resistance configuration specifies the number of wires to use for resistive measurements. 2-Wire (2) 2-Wire mode. 3-Wire (3) 3-Wire mode. 4-Wire (4) 4-Wire mode. minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units to use to return temperature measurements. deg C (10143) Degrees Celsius. deg F (10144) Degrees Fahrenheit. deg R (10145) Degrees Rankine. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. K (10325) Kelvins. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. current excitation source — current excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| 2-Wire (2) | 2-Wire mode. |
| 3-Wire (3) | 3-Wire mode. |
| 4-Wire (4) | 4-Wire mode. |
| deg C (10143) | Degrees Celsius. |
| deg F (10144) | Degrees Fahrenheit. |
| deg R (10145) | Degrees Rankine. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| K (10325) | Kelvins. |
| External (10167) | Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| Internal (10200) | Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| None (10230) | Supply no excitation to the channel. |

## TOPIC 02195: DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Temperature-Thermistor-Iex) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`

- source_description: Creates channel(s) that use a thermistor to measure temperature. Use this instance when the thermistor requires current excitation. You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI. icon Inputs/Outputs cdbl.png current excitation value current excitation value specifie

### DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Temperature-Thermistor-Iex) VI

Creates channel(s) that use a [thermistor](/csh?context=nidaqmx_measfunds_thermistors) to [measure temperature](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_simtemp). Use this instance when the thermistor requires current excitation. You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-temperature-thermistor-iex-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| current excitation value — current excitation value specifies in amperes the amount of excitation to supply to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine this value. resistance configuration — resistance configuration specifies the number of wires to use for resistive measurements. 2-Wire (2) 2-Wire mode. 3-Wire (3) 3-Wire mode. 4-Wire (4) 4-Wire mode. minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units to use to return temperature measurements. deg C (10143) Degrees Celsius. deg F (10144) Degrees Fahrenheit. deg R (10145) Degrees Rankine. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. K (10325) Kelvins. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. current excitation source — current excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| 2-Wire (2) | 2-Wire mode. |
| 3-Wire (3) | 3-Wire mode. |
| 4-Wire (4) | 4-Wire mode. |
| deg C (10143) | Degrees Celsius. |
| deg F (10144) | Degrees Fahrenheit. |
| deg R (10145) | Degrees Rankine. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| K (10325) | Kelvins. |
| External (10167) | Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the current excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |

### DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Temperature-Thermistor-Vex) VI

Creates channel(s) that use a [thermistor](/csh?context=nidaqmx_measfunds_thermistors) to [measure temperature](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_simtemp). Use this instance when the thermistor requires voltage excitation. You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-temperature-thermistor-vex-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| voltage excitation value — voltage excitation value specifies in volts the amount of excitation supplied to the sensor. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units to use to return temperature measurements. deg C (10143) Degrees Celsius. deg F (10144) Degrees Fahrenheit. deg R (10145) Degrees Rankine. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. K (10325) Kelvins. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. voltage excitation source — voltage excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. r1 — r1 specifies in ohms the value of the reference resistor. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| 2-Wire (2) | 2-Wire mode. |
| 3-Wire (3) | 3-Wire mode. |
| 4-Wire (4) | 4-Wire mode. |
| deg C (10143) | Degrees Celsius. |
| deg F (10144) | Degrees Fahrenheit. |
| deg R (10145) | Degrees Rankine. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| K (10325) | Kelvins. |
| External (10167) | Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| Internal (10200) | Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| None (10230) | Supply no excitation to the channel. |
