DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-temperature-thermocouple-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02197: DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Temperature-Thermocouple) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-temperature-thermocouple-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-temperature-thermocouple-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) that use a thermocouple to measure temperature. You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI. icon Inputs/Outputs cgenclassrntag.png cjc channel cjc channel specifies the channel that acquires the temperature of the thermocouple cold-junction if

### DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Temperature-Thermocouple) VI

Creates channel(s) that use a [thermocouple](/csh?context=nidaqmx_measfunds_thermocouples) to [measure temperature](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_simtemp). You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-temperature-thermocouple-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| cjc channel — cjc channel specifies the channel that acquires the temperature of the thermocouple cold-junction if you set cjc source to Channel. You can use a global channel or another virtual channel already in the task. If the channel is a temperature channel, NI-DAQmx acquires the temperature in the correct units. Other channel types, such as a resistance channel with a custom sensor, must use a custom scale to scale values to degrees Celsius. cjc source — cjc source specifies the source of cold-junction compensation. Channel (10113) You must use the cjc channel input to specify a channel. Constant Value (10116) You must use the cjc value input to specify the cold-junction temperature. Internal (10200) Use a cold-junction compensation channel built into the terminal block. If no such channel is available, this VI returns an error. minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units to use to return temperature measurements. deg C (10143) Degrees Celsius. deg F (10144) Degrees Fahrenheit. deg R (10145) Degrees Rankine. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. K (10325) Kelvins. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. cjc value — cjc value specifies in units the temperature of the cold junction if you set cjc source to Constant Value. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Channel (10113) | You must use the cjc channel input to specify a channel. |
| Constant Value (10116) | You must use the cjc value input to specify the cold-junction temperature. |
| Internal (10200) | Use a cold-junction compensation channel built into the terminal block. If no such channel is available, this VI returns an error. |
| deg C (10143) | Degrees Celsius. |
| deg F (10144) | Degrees Fahrenheit. |
| deg R (10145) | Degrees Rankine. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| K (10325) | Kelvins. |

DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI

## TOPIC 02198: DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Torque-Bridge) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-torque-bridge-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-torque-bridge-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) that use a Wheatstone bridge to measure torque. You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI. NI-DAQmx scales electrical values to physical values according to that TEDS information. icon Inputs/Outputs cdbl.png minimum value minimum value specif

### DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Torque-Bridge) VI

Creates channel(s) that use a [Wheatstone bridge](/csh?context=nidaqmx_measfunds_bridgeforcepressuretorque) to [measure torque](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_torque). 
 You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI. NI-DAQmx scales electrical values to physical values according to that TEDS information.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-torque-bridge-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies in which unit to return torque measurements from the channel. Nm (15881) Newton meters. oz-in (15882) Ounce-inches. lb-in (15883) Pound-inches. lb-ft (15884) Pound-feet. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. voltage excitation source — voltage excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. voltage excitation value — voltage excitation value specifies in volts the amount of excitation supplied to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine appropriate excitation values. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Nm (15881) | Newton meters. |
| oz-in (15882) | Ounce-inches. |
| lb-in (15883) | Pound-inches. |
| lb-ft (15884) | Pound-feet. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| External (10167) | Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| Internal (10200) | Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| None (10230) | Supply no excitation to the channel. |

## TOPIC 02199: DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Voltage-Basic) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-voltage-basic-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-voltage-basic-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates channel(s) to measure voltage. You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI. If the measurement requires the use of internal excitation or you need excitation to scale the voltage, use the instance of this VI. icon Inputs/Outputs ci32.png input terminal con

### DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Voltage-Basic) VI

Creates channel(s) to [measure voltage](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqhelp_meas_volt). You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI. If the measurement requires the use of internal excitation or you need excitation to scale the voltage, use the instance of this VI.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-voltage-basic-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| input terminal configuration — input terminal configuration specifies the input terminal configuration for the channel. default (-1) At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. Differential (10106) Differential mode. NRSE (10078) Non-referenced single-ended mode. Pseudodifferential (12529) Pseudodifferential mode. RSE (10083) Referenced single-ended mode. minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units to use to return measurements. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. From TEDS (12516) Units defined by TEDS information associated with the channel. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| default (-1) | At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. |
| Differential (10106) | Differential mode. |
| NRSE (10078) | Non-referenced single-ended mode. |
| Pseudodifferential (12529) | Pseudodifferential mode. |
| RSE (10083) | Referenced single-ended mode. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| From TEDS (12516) | Units defined by TEDS information associated with the channel. |

## TOPIC 02200: DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Voltage-Custom with Excitation) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`

- source_description: Creates channel(s) to measure voltage. Use this instance for custom sensors that require excitation. You can use the excitation to scale the measurement. You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI. icon Inputs/Outputs cdbl.png voltage excitation value voltage exc

### DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Voltage-Custom with Excitation) VI

Creates channel(s) to measure voltage. Use this instance for custom sensors that require excitation. You can use the excitation to scale the measurement. You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-voltage-custom-with-excitation-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| voltage excitation value — voltage excitation value specifies in volts the amount of excitation supplied to the sensor. Refer to the sensor documentation to determine appropriate excitation values. input terminal configuration — input terminal configuration specifies the input terminal configuration for the channel. default (-1) At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. Differential (10106) Differential mode. NRSE (10078) Non-referenced single-ended mode. Pseudodifferential (12529) Pseudodifferential mode. RSE (10083) Referenced single-ended mode. minimum value — minimum value specifies in units the minimum value you expect to measure. maximum value — maximum value specifies in units the maximum value you expect to measure. task in — task in specifies the task to which to add the virtual channels this VI creates. If you do not specify a task, NI-DAQmx creates a task for you and adds the virtual channels this VI creates to that task. physical channels — physical channels specifies the names of the physical channels to use to create virtual channels. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels on devices and modules installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of physical channels to this input. If you have an array of physical channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. name to assign — name to assign specifies a name to assign to the virtual channel this VI creates. If you do not wire a value to this input, NI-DAQmx uses the physical channel name as the virtual channel name. If you use this input to provide your own names for the virtual channels, you must use the names when you refer to these channels in other NI-DAQmx VIs and Property Nodes, such as the source input of the DAQmx Trigger VI. If you create multiple virtual channels with one DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI, you can specify a comma-separated list of names to assign to the virtual channels. If you provide fewer names than the number of virtual channels you create, NI-DAQmx automatically assigns names to the virtual channels. units — units specifies the units to use to return measurements. From Custom Scale (10065) Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. From TEDS (12516) Units defined by TEDS information associated with the channel. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. custom scale name — custom scale name specifies the name of a custom scale for the channel. If you want the channel to use a custom scale, wire the name of the custom scale to this input and set units to From Custom Scale. voltage excitation source — voltage excitation source specifies the source of excitation. External (10167) Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. Internal (10200) Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. None (10230) Supply no excitation to the channel. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI runs. The task contains any newly created virtual channels. If you did not wire a value to task in, NI-DAQmx automatically creates the task this output refers to. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| default (-1) | At run time, NI-DAQmx chooses the default terminal configuration for the channel. |
| Differential (10106) | Differential mode. |
| NRSE (10078) | Non-referenced single-ended mode. |
| Pseudodifferential (12529) | Pseudodifferential mode. |
| RSE (10083) | Referenced single-ended mode. |
| From Custom Scale (10065) | Units a custom scale specifies. If you select this value, you must wire a custom scale name to the custom scale name input. |
| From TEDS (12516) | Units defined by TEDS information associated with the channel. |
| External (10167) | Use an excitation source other than the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| Internal (10200) | Use the built-in excitation source of the device. If you select this value, you must use the voltage excitation value input to specify the amount of excitation. |
| None (10230) | Supply no excitation to the channel. |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-virtual-channel-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02201: DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-virtual-channel-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-virtual-channel-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates a virtual channel or set of virtual channels and adds them to a task. The instances of this polymorphic VI correspond to the I/O type of the channel, such as analog input, digital output, or counter output; the measurement or generation to perform, such as temperature measurement, voltage ge

### DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI

Creates a [virtual channel](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_chans) or set of virtual channels and adds them to a [task](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_tasksnidaqmx). The instances of this [polymorphic VI](/csh?context=lvcore_lvhelp_using_polymorphic_vis) correspond to the I/O type of the channel, such as analog input, digital output, or counter output; the measurement or generation to perform, such as temperature measurement, voltage generation, or event counting; and in some cases, the sensor to use, such as a thermocouple or RTD for temperature measurements.

If you use this VI within a loop without specifying a **task in**, NI-DAQmx creates a new task in each iteration of the loop. Use the [DAQmx Clear Task](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_mxcleartask) VI within the loop after you are finished with the task to avoid allocating unnecessary memory. Refer to [Task Creation and Destruction](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_taskcreationanddestruction) for more information about when NI-DAQmx creates tasks and when LabVIEW automatically destroys tasks.

The [DAQmx Channel](/csh?context=nidaqmx_nidaq_daqmx_channel_p) properties include additional channel configuration options.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-virtual-channel-vi.png']

- [DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Voltage-Basic) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-voltage-basic-vi.html) Creates channel(s) to measure voltage . If the measurement requires the use of internal excitation or you need excitation to scale the voltage, use the instance of this VI.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Voltage-RMS) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-voltage-rms-vi.html) Creates channel(s) to measure voltage RMS, the average (mean) power of the acquired voltage.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Temperature-Thermocouple) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-temperature-thermocouple-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that use a thermocouple to measure temperature .
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Temperature-RTD) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-temperature-rtd-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that use an RTD to measure temperature .
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Temperature-Thermistor-Iex) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-temperature-thermistor-iex-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that use a thermistor to measure temperature . Use this instance when the thermistor requires current excitation.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Temperature-Thermistor-Vex) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-temperature-thermistor-vex-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that use a thermistor to measure temperature . Use this instance when the thermistor requires voltage excitation.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Current-Basic) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-current-basic-vi.html) Creates channel(s) to measure current .
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Current-RMS) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-current-rms-vi.html) Creates a channel to measure current RMS, the average (mean) power of the acquired current.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Resistance) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-resistance-vi.html) Creates channel(s) to measure resistance .
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Strain-Strain Gage) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-strain-strain-gage-vi.html) Creates channel(s) to measure strain .
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Strain-Rosette Strain Gage) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-strain-rosette-strain-gage-vi.html) Creates channels to measure two-dimensional strain using a rosette strain gage.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Force-Bridge-Two-Point-Linear) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-force-bridge-two-point-linear-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that use a Wheatstone bridge to measure force or load . Use this instance with sensors whose specifications do not provide a polynomial for scaling or a table of electrical and physical values. When you use this scaling type , NI-DAQmx uses two points of electrical and physical values to calculate the slope and y-intercept of a linear equation and uses that equation to scale electrical values to physical values.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Force-Bridge-Table) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-force-bridge-table-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that use a Wheatstone bridge to measure force or load . Use this instance with sensors whose specifications provide a table of electrical values and the corresponding physical values. When you use this scaling type , NI-DAQmx performs linear scaling between each pair of electrical and physical values. The input limits specified with minimum value and maximum value must fall within the smallest and largest physical values. For any data outside those endpoints, NI-DAQmx coerces that data to the endpoints.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Force-Bridge-Polynomial) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-force-bridge-polynomial-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that use a Wheatstone bridge to measure force or load . Use this instance with sensors whose specifications provide a polynomial to convert electrical values to physical values. When you use this scaling type , NI-DAQmx requires coefficients for a polynomial that converts electrical values to physical values (forward), as well as coefficients for a polynomial that converts physical values to electrical values (reverse). If you only know one set of coefficients, use the DAQmx Compute Reverse Polynomial Coefficients VI to generate the other set.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Pressure-Bridge-Two-Point-Linear) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-pressure-bridge-two-point-linear-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that use a Wheatstone bridge to measure pressure . Use this instance with sensors whose specifications do not provide a polynomial for scaling or a table of electrical and physical values. When you use this scaling type , NI-DAQmx uses two points of electrical and physical values to calculate the slope and y-intercept of a linear equation and uses that equation to scale electrical values to physical values.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Pressure-Bridge-Table) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-pressure-bridge-table-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that use a Wheatstone bridge to measure pressure . Use this instance with sensors whose specifications provide a table of electrical values and the corresponding physical values. When you use this scaling type , NI-DAQmx performs linear scaling between each pair of electrical and physical values. The input limits specified with minimum value and maximum value must fall within the smallest and largest physical values. For any data outside those endpoints, NI-DAQmx coerces that data to the endpoints.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Pressure-Bridge-Polynomial) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-pressure-bridge-polynomial-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that use a Wheatstone bridge to measure pressure . Use this instance with sensors whose specifications provide a polynomial to convert electrical values to physical values. When you use this scaling type , NI-DAQmx requires coefficients for a polynomial that converts electrical values to physical values (forward), as well as coefficients for a polynomial that converts physical values to electrical values (reverse). If you only know one set of coefficients, use the DAQmx Compute Reverse Polynomial Coefficients VI to generate the other set.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Torque-Bridge-Two-Point-Linear) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-torque-bridge-two-point-linear-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that use a Wheatstone bridge to measure torque . Use this instance with sensors whose specifications do not provide a polynomial for scaling or a table of electrical and physical values. When you use this scaling type , NI-DAQmx uses two points of electrical and physical values to calculate the slope and y-intercept of a linear equation and uses that equation to scale electrical values to physical values.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Torque-Bridge-Table) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-torque-bridge-table-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that use a Wheatstone bridge to measure torque . Use this instance with sensors whose specifications provide a table of electrical values and the corresponding physical values. When you use this scaling type , NI-DAQmx performs linear scaling between each pair of electrical and physical values. The input limits specified with minimum value and maximum value must fall within the smallest and largest physical values. For any data outside those endpoints, NI-DAQmx coerces that data to the endpoints.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Torque-Bridge-Polynomial) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-torque-bridge-polynomial-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that use a Wheatstone bridge to measure torque . Use this instance with sensors whose specifications provide a polynomial to convert electrical values to physical values. When you use this scaling type , NI-DAQmx requires coefficients for a polynomial that converts electrical values to physical values (forward), as well as coefficients for a polynomial that converts physical values to electrical values (reverse). If you only know one set of coefficients, use the DAQmx Compute Reverse Polynomial Coefficients VI to generate the other set.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Frequency-Voltage) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-frequency-voltage-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that use a frequency-to-voltage converter to measure frequency .
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Position-LVDT) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-position-lvdt-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that use an LVDT to measure linear position .
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Position-RVDT) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-position-rvdt-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that use an RVDT to measure angular position .
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Position-EddyCurrentProxProbe) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-position-eddycurrentproxprobe-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that use an eddy current proximity probe to measure position .
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Acceleration-Accelerometer) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-acceleration-accelerometer-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that use an accelerometer to measure acceleration .
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Acceleration-Charge) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-acceleration-charge-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that use a charge-based sensor to measure acceleration .
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Acceleration-4 Wire DC Voltage) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-acceleration-4-wire-dc-voltage-vi.html) Creates channel(s) to measure acceleration . Use this instance for custom sensors that require excitation. You can use the excitation to scale the measurement.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Velocity-IEPE Sensor) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-velocity-iepe-sensor-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that use an IEPE velocity sensor to measure velocity .
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Force-IEPE Sensor) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-force-iepe-sensor-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that use an IEPE force sensor to measure force or load .
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Sound Pressure-Microphone) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-sound-pressure-microphone-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that use a microphone to measure sound pressure .
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Voltage-Custom with Excitation) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-voltage-custom-with-excitation-vi.html) Creates channel(s) to measure voltage. Use this instance for custom sensors that require excitation. You can use the excitation to scale the measurement.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Temperature-Built-in Sensor) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-temperature-built-in-sensor-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that use the built-in sensor of a terminal block or device to measure temperature. On SCXI modules, for example, the built-in sensor could be the CJC sensor.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Calculated Power) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-calculated-power-vi.html) Creates channel(s) to measure calculated power .
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AO-Voltage-Basic) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ao-voltage-basic-vi.html) Creates channel(s) to generate voltage .
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AO-Current-Basic) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ao-current-basic-vi.html) Creates channel(s) to generate current .
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AO-FuncGen) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ao-funcgen-vi.html) Creates a channel for continually generating a waveform on the selected physical channel.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (DI-Digital Input) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-di-digital-input-vi.html) Creates channel(s) to measure digital signals . You can group digital lines into one digital channel or separate them into multiple digital channels. If you specify one or more entire ports in the lines input by using port physical channel names, you cannot separate the ports into multiple channels. To separate ports into multiple channels, use this VI multiple times with a different port each time.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (DO-Digital Output) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-do-digital-output-vi.html) Creates channel(s) to generate digital signals . You can group digital lines into one digital channel or separate them into multiple digital channels. If you specify one or more entire ports in lines input by using port physical channel names, you cannot separate the ports into multiple channels. To separate ports into multiple channels, use this VI multiple times with a different port each time.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Frequency) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-frequency-vi.html) Creates a channel to measure the frequency of a digital signal . With the exception of devices that support multi-counter tasks , you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple counters simultaneously, use a separate task for each counter. Connect the input signal to the default input terminal of the counter unless you select a different input terminal.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Period) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-period-vi.html) Creates a channel to measure the period of a digital signal . With the exception of devices that support multi-counter tasks , you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple counters simultaneously, use a separate task for each counter. Connect the input signal to the default input terminal of the counter unless you select a different input terminal.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Count Edges) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-count-edges-vi.html) Creates a channel to count the number of rising or falling edges of a digital signal . With the exception of devices that support multi-counter tasks , you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple counters simultaneously, use a separate task for each counter. Connect the input signal to the default input terminal of the counter unless you select a different input terminal.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Duty Cycle) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-duty-cycle-vi.html) Creates channel(s) to duty cycle of a digital pulse. Connect the input signal to the default input terminal of the counter unless you select a different input terminal. With the exception of devices that support multi-counter tasks , you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple counters simultaneously, use a separate task for each counter.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Pulse Width) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-pulse-width-vi.html) Creates a channel to measure the width of a digital pulse . starting edge determines whether to measure a high pulse or low pulse. With the exception of devices that support multi-counter tasks , you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple counters simultaneously, use a separate task for each counter. Connect the input signal to the default input terminal of the counter unless you select a different input terminal.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Semi Period) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-semi-period-vi.html) Creates a channel to measure the time between state transitions of a digital signal . With the exception of devices that support multi-counter tasks , you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple counters simultaneously, use a separate task for each counter. Connect the input signal to the default input terminal of the counter unless you select a different input terminal.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Pulse Freq) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-pulse-freq-vi.html) Creates a channel to measure pulse specifications , returning the measurements as pairs of frequency and duty cycle. With the exception of devices that support multi-counter tasks , you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple counters simultaneously, use a separate task for each counter. Connect the input signal to the default input terminal of the counter unless you select a different input terminal.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Pulse Time) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-pulse-time-vi.html) Creates a channel to measure pulse specifications , returning the measurements as pairs of high time and low time. With the exception of devices that support multi-counter tasks , you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple counters simultaneously, use a separate task for each counter. Connect the input signal to the default input terminal of the counter unless you select a different input terminal.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Pulse Ticks) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-pulse-ticks-vi.html) Creates a channel to measure pulse specifications , returning the measurements as pairs of high ticks and low ticks. With the exception of devices that support multi-counter tasks , you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple counters simultaneously, use a separate task for each counter. Connect the input signal to the default input terminal of the counter unless you select a different input terminal.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Position-Angular Encoder) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-position-angular-encoder-vi.html) Creates a channel that uses an angular encoder to measure angular position . With the exception of devices that support multi-counter tasks , you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple counters simultaneously, use a separate task for each counter. Connect the input signals to the default input terminals of the counter unless you select different input terminals.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Position-Linear Encoder) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-position-linear-encoder-vi.html) Creates a channel that uses a linear encoder to measure linear position . With the exception of devices that support multi-counter tasks , you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple counters simultaneously, use a separate task for each counter. Connect the input signals to the default input terminals of the counter unless you select different input terminals.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Velocity-Angular) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-velocity-angular-vi.html) Creates a channel to measure the angular velocity of a digital signal. With the exception of devices that support multi-counter tasks , you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple counters simultaneously, use a separate task for each counter. Connect the input signal to the default input terminal of the counter unless you select a different input terminal.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Velocity-Linear) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-velocity-linear-vi.html) Creates a channel that uses a linear encoder to measure linear velocity . With the exception of devices that support multi-counter tasks , you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple counters simultaneously, use a separate task for each counter. Connect the input signal to the default input terminal of the counter unless you select a different input terminal.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (CI-Two Edge Separation) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-two-edge-separation-vi.html) Creates a channel that measures the amount of time between the rising or falling edge of one digital signal and the rising or falling edge of another digital signal . With the exception of devices that support multi-counter tasks , you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple counters simultaneously, use a separate task for each counter. Connect the input signals to the default input terminals of the counter unless you select different input terminals.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (CI-GPS Timestamp) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ci-gps-timestamp-vi.html) Creates a channel that uses a special purpose counter to take a timestamp and synchronizes that counter to a GPS receiver. With the exception of devices that support multi-counter tasks , you can create only one counter input channel at a time with this VI because a task can contain only one counter input channel. To read from multiple counters simultaneously, use a separate task for each counter. Connect the input signals to the default input terminals of the counter unless you select different input terminals.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (CO-Pulse Generation-Frequency) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-co-pulse-generation-frequency-vi.html) Creates channel(s) to generate digital pulses that frequency and duty cycle define. The pulses appear on the default output terminal of the counter unless you select a different output terminal.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (CO-Pulse Generation-Time) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-co-pulse-generation-time-vi.html) Creates channel(s) to generate digital pulses defined by the amount of time the pulse is at a high state and the amount of time the pulse is at a low state. The pulses appear on the default output terminal of the counter unless you select a different output terminal.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (CO-Pulse Generation-Ticks) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-co-pulse-generation-ticks-vi.html) Creates channel(s) to generate digital pulses defined by the number of timebase ticks that the pulse is at a high state and the number of timebase ticks that the pulse is at a low state. The pulses appear on the default output terminal of the counter unless you select a different output terminal.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Voltage-Basic) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-voltage-basic-vi.html) Creates channel(s) to measure voltage . You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI. If the measurement requires the use of internal excitation or you need excitation to scale the voltage, use the instance of this VI.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Temperature-Thermocouple) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-temperature-thermocouple-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that use a thermocouple to measure temperature . You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Temperature-RTD) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-temperature-rtd-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that use an RTD to measure temperature . You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Temperature-Thermistor-Iex) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-temperature-thermistor-iex-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that use a thermistor to measure temperature . Use this instance when the thermistor requires current excitation. You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Temperature-Thermistor-Vex) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-temperature-thermistor-vex-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that use a thermistor to measure temperature . Use this instance when the thermistor requires voltage excitation. You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Current-Basic) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-current-basic-vi.html) Creates channel(s) to measure current . You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Resistance) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-resistance-vi.html) Creates channel(s) to measure resistance . You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Strain-Strain Gage) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-strain-strain-gage-vi.html) Creates channel(s) to measure strain . You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Force-Bridge) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-force-bridge-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that use a Wheatstone bridge to measure force or load . You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI. NI-DAQmx scales electrical values to physical values according to that TEDS information.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Force-IEPE Sensor) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-force-iepe-sensor-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that use an IEPE force sensor to measure force or load . You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Pressure-Bridge) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-pressure-bridge-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that use a Wheatstone bridge to measure pressure . You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI. NI-DAQmx scales electrical values to physical values according to that TEDS information.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Torque-Bridge) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-torque-bridge-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that use a Wheatstone bridge to measure torque . You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI. NI-DAQmx scales electrical values to physical values according to that TEDS information.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Position-LVDT) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-position-lvdt-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that use an LVDT to measure linear position . You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Position-RVDT) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-position-rvdt-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that use an RVDT to measure angular position . You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Acceleration-Accelerometer) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-acceleration-accelerometer-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that use an accelerometer to measure acceleration . You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Sound Pressure-Microphone) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-sound-pressure-microphone-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that use a microphone to measure sound pressure . You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Voltage-Custom with Excitation) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-voltage-custom-with-excitation-vi.html) Creates channel(s) to measure voltage. Use this instance for custom sensors that require excitation. You can use the excitation to scale the measurement. You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Bridge) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-bridge-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that measure voltage ratios from a Wheatstone bridge . Use this instance with bridge-based sensors that measure phenomena other than strain, force, pressure, or torque, or that scale data to physical units NI-DAQmx does not support.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (AI-Charge) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-ai-charge-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that use a sensor with charge output.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (TEDS-AI-Bridge) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-teds-ai-bridge-vi.html) Creates channel(s) that measure a Wheatstone bridge . You must configure the physical channel(s) with TEDS information to use this VI. Use this instance with bridge-based sensors that measure phenomena other than strain, force, pressure, or torque, or that scale data to physical units NI-DAQmx does not support.
- [DAQmx Create Channel (Power) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/channels-llb/daqmx-create-channel-power-vi.html) Creates channel(s) to source and measure power.

Parent topic:

DAQmx - Data Acquisition

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/create/scale-llb/daqmx-create-scale-linear-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02202: DAQmx Create Scale (Linear) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/scale-llb/daqmx-create-scale-linear-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/scale-llb/daqmx-create-scale-linear-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates a custom scale that uses the equation y=mx+b, where x is a pre-scaled value, and y is a scaled value. The equation is identical for input and output. If the equation is in the form x=my+b, you must first solve for y in terms of x. icon Inputs/Outputs cstr.png name name identifies the custom

### DAQmx Create Scale (Linear) VI

Creates a [custom scale](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_customscales) that uses the equation *y*=*mx*+*b*, where *x* is a pre-scaled value, and *y* is a scaled value. The equation is identical for input and output. If the equation is in the form *x*=*my*+*b*, you must first solve for *y* in terms of *x*.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-scale-linear-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| name — name identifies the custom scale for later use, such as with the DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI slope — slope is the slope, m, in the equation. y-intercept — y-intercept is the y-intercept, b, in the equation. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. pre-scaled units — pre-scaled units is the units of the values to scale. Amps (10342) Amperes. bar (15880) Bar. deg C (10143) Degrees Celsius. deg F (10144) Degrees Fahrenheit. deg R (10145) Degrees Rankine. Degrees (10146) Degrees. From TEDS (12516) Units defined by TEDS information associated with the channel. g (10186) G. 1 g is approximately equal to 9.81 m/s/s. Hz (10373) Hertz. Inches (10379) Inches. Kelvins (10325) Kelvins. kgf (15877) Kilograms-force lb-ft (15884) Pound-feet. lb-in (15883) Pound-inches. m/s^2 (12470) Meters per second per second. Meters (10219) Meters. mVolts/Volt (15897) Millivolts per volt. Nm (15881) Newton meters. Newtons (15875) Newtons. Ohms (10384) Ohms. oz-in (15882) Ounce-inches. Pascals (10081) Pascals. Pounds (15876) Pounds. psi (15879) Pounds per square inch. Radians (10273) Radians. Seconds (10364) Seconds. Strain (10299) Strain. Volts/Volt (15896) Volts per volt. Volts (10348) Volts. Watts (16203) Watts. scaled units — scaled units is the units to use for the scaled value. You can use an arbitrary string. LabVIEW uses the units to label a graph or chart. scale out — scale out is a reference to the custom scale this VI creates. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |

## TOPIC 02203: DAQmx Create Scale (Map Ranges) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/scale-llb/daqmx-create-scale-map-ranges-vi.html`

- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates a custom scale that scales values proportionally from a range of pre-scaled values to a range of scaled values. icon Inputs/Outputs cdbl.png pre-scaled maximum pre-scaled maximum is the largest value in the range of pre-scaled values. NI-DAQmx maps this value to scaled maximum. cdbl.png pre-

### DAQmx Create Scale (Map Ranges) VI

Creates a [custom scale](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_customscales) that scales values proportionally from a range of pre-scaled values to a range of scaled values.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-scale-map-ranges-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| pre-scaled maximum — pre-scaled maximum is the largest value in the range of pre-scaled values. NI-DAQmx maps this value to scaled maximum. pre-scaled minimum — pre-scaled minimum is the smallest value in the range of pre-scaled values. NI-DAQmx maps this value to scaled minimum. name — name identifies the custom scale for later use, such as with the DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI scaled minimum — scaled minimum is the smallest value in the range of scaled values. NI-DAQmx maps this value to pre-scaled minimum. Read operations clip samples that are smaller than this value. Write operations generate errors for samples that are smaller than this value. scaled maximum — scaled maximum is the largest value in the range of scaled values. NI-DAQmx maps this value to pre-scaled maximum. Read operations clip samples that are larger than this value. Write operations generate errors for samples that are larger than this value. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. pre-scaled units — pre-scaled units is the units of the values to scale. ## TOPIC 02204: DAQmx Create Scale (Polynomial) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/scale-llb/daqmx-create-scale-polynomial-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/scale-llb/daqmx-create-scale-polynomial-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates a custom scale that uses an nth order polynomial equation. NI-DAQmx requires both a polynomial to convert pre-scaled values to scaled values (forward) and a polynomial to convert scaled values to pre-scaled values (reverse). If you only know one set of coefficients, use the DAQmx Compute Rev

### DAQmx Create Scale (Polynomial) VI

Creates a [custom scale](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_customscales) that uses an *n*th order polynomial equation. NI-DAQmx requires both a polynomial to convert pre-scaled values to scaled values (forward) and a polynomial to convert scaled values to pre-scaled values (reverse). If you only know one set of coefficients, use the [DAQmx Compute Reverse Polynomial Coefficients](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_mxcomputereversecoeff) VI to generate the other set.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-scale-polynomial-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| name — name identifies the custom scale for later use, such as with the DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI forward coeff — forward coeff is an array of coefficients for the polynomial that converts pre-scaled values to scaled values. Each element of the array corresponds to a term of the equation. For example, if index three of the array is 9, the fourth term of the equation is 9x3. reverse coeff — reverse coeff is an array of coefficients for the polynomial that converts scaled values to pre-scaled values. Each element of the array corresponds to a term of the equation. For example, if index three of the array is 9, the fourth term of the equation is 9y3. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. pre-scaled units — pre-scaled units is the units of the values to scale. Amps (10342) Amperes. bar (15880) Bar. deg C (10143) Degrees Celsius. deg F (10144) Degrees Fahrenheit. deg R (10145) Degrees Rankine. Degrees (10146) Degrees. From TEDS (12516) Units defined by TEDS information associated with the channel. g (10186) G. 1 g is approximately equal to 9.81 m/s/s. Hz (10373) Hertz. Inches (10379) Inches. Kelvins (10325) Kelvins. kgf (15877) Kilograms-force lb-ft (15884) Pound-feet. lb-in (15883) Pound-inches. m/s^2 (12470) Meters per second per second. Meters (10219) Meters. mVolts/Volt (15897) Millivolts per volt. Nm (15881) Newton meters. Newtons (15875) Newtons. Ohms (10384) Ohms. oz-in (15882) Ounce-inches. Pascals (10081) Pascals. Pounds (15876) Pounds. psi (15879) Pounds per square inch. Radians (10273) Radians. Seconds (10364) Seconds. Strain (10299) Strain. Volts/Volt (15896) Volts per volt. Volts (10348) Volts. Watts (16203) Watts. scaled units — scaled units is the units to use for the scaled value. You can use an arbitrary string. LabVIEW uses the units to label a graph or chart. scale out — scale out is a reference to the custom scale this VI creates. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |

## TOPIC 02205: DAQmx Create Scale (Table) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/scale-llb/daqmx-create-scale-table-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/scale-llb/daqmx-create-scale-table-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates a custom scale that maps an array of pre-scaled values to an array of corresponding scaled values. NI-DAQmx applies linear interpolation to values that fall between the values in the table. Read operations clip scaled samples that are outside the maximum and minimum scaled values found in th

### DAQmx Create Scale (Table) VI

Creates a [custom scale](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_customscales) that maps an array of pre-scaled values to an array of corresponding scaled values. NI-DAQmx applies linear interpolation to values that fall between the values in the table. Read operations clip scaled samples that are outside the maximum and minimum scaled values found in the table. Write operations generate errors for samples that are outside the minimum and maximum scaled values found in the table.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-scale-table-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| name — name identifies the custom scale for later use, such as with the DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI scaled values — scaled values is the array of scaled values that map to the values in pre-scaled values. pre-scaled values — pre-scaled values is the array of pre-scaled values that map to the values in scaled values. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. pre-scaled units — pre-scaled units is the units of the values to scale. Amps (10342) Amperes. bar (15880) Bar. deg C (10143) Degrees Celsius. deg F (10144) Degrees Fahrenheit. deg R (10145) Degrees Rankine. Degrees (10146) Degrees. From TEDS (12516) Units defined by TEDS information associated with the channel. g (10186) G. 1 g is approximately equal to 9.81 m/s/s. Hz (10373) Hertz. Inches (10379) Inches. Kelvins (10325) Kelvins. kgf (15877) Kilograms-force lb-ft (15884) Pound-feet. lb-in (15883) Pound-inches. m/s^2 (12470) Meters per second per second. Meters (10219) Meters. mVolts/Volt (15897) Millivolts per volt. Nm (15881) Newton meters. Newtons (15875) Newtons. Ohms (10384) Ohms. oz-in (15882) Ounce-inches. Pascals (10081) Pascals. Pounds (15876) Pounds. psi (15879) Pounds per square inch. Radians (10273) Radians. Seconds (10364) Seconds. Strain (10299) Strain. Volts/Volt (15896) Volts per volt. Volts (10348) Volts. Watts (16203) Watts. scaled units — scaled units is the units to use for the scaled value. You can use an arbitrary string. LabVIEW uses the units to label a graph or chart. scale out — scale out is a reference to the custom scale this VI creates. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
