DAQmx Create Scale VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/create/scale-llb/daqmx-create-scale-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02206: DAQmx Create Scale VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/scale-llb/daqmx-create-scale-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/scale-llb/daqmx-create-scale-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates and configures a custom scale. Apply custom scales to input and output channels to provide additional scaling. The instances of this polymorphic VI correspond to the type of scale this VI creates. icon

### DAQmx Create Scale VI

Creates and configures a [custom scale](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_customscales). Apply custom scales to input and output channels to provide additional scaling. The instances of this [polymorphic VI](/csh?context=lvcore_lvhelp_using_polymorphic_vis) correspond to the type of scale this VI creates.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-scale-vi.png']

- [DAQmx Create Scale (Linear) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/scale-llb/daqmx-create-scale-linear-vi.html) Creates a custom scale that uses the equation y = mx + b , where x is a pre-scaled value, and y is a scaled value. The equation is identical for input and output. If the equation is in the form x = my + b , you must first solve for y in terms of x .
- [DAQmx Create Scale (Map Ranges) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/scale-llb/daqmx-create-scale-map-ranges-vi.html) Creates a custom scale that scales values proportionally from a range of pre-scaled values to a range of scaled values.
- [DAQmx Create Scale (Polynomial) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/scale-llb/daqmx-create-scale-polynomial-vi.html) Creates a custom scale that uses an n th order polynomial equation. NI-DAQmx requires both a polynomial to convert pre-scaled values to scaled values (forward) and a polynomial to convert scaled values to pre-scaled values (reverse). If you only know one set of coefficients, use the DAQmx Compute Reverse Polynomial Coefficients VI to generate the other set.
- [DAQmx Create Scale (Table) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/scale-llb/daqmx-create-scale-table-vi.html) Creates a custom scale that maps an array of pre-scaled values to an array of corresponding scaled values. NI-DAQmx applies linear interpolation to values that fall between the values in the table. Read operations clip scaled samples that are outside the maximum and minimum scaled values found in the table. Write operations generate errors for samples that are outside the minimum and maximum scaled values found in the table.

Parent topic:

DAQmx Scale Setup

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/create/task-llb/daqmx-create-task-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02207: DAQmx Create Task VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/task-llb/daqmx-create-task-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/task-llb/daqmx-create-task-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates a task and adds virtual channels to that task if you specify them in the global virtual channels input. If you specify a task to copy, this VI duplicates the configuration of the specified task in the newly created task before it adds any additional global virtual channels. If you use this V

### DAQmx Create Task VI

Creates a [task](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_tasksnidaqmx) and adds [virtual channels](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_chans) to that task if you specify them in the **global virtual channels** input. If you specify a **task to copy**, this VI duplicates the configuration of the specified task in the newly created task before it adds any additional global virtual channels.

If you use this VI within a loop, NI-DAQmx creates a new task in each iteration of the loop. Use the [DAQmx Clear Task](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_mxcleartask) VI within the loop after you are finished with the task to avoid allocating unnecessary memory. Refer to [Task Creation and Destruction](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_taskcreationanddestruction) for more information on when NI-DAQmx creates tasks and when LabVIEW automatically destroys tasks.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-task-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| auto cleanup — auto cleanup specifies if LabVIEW automatically destroys the task when the application completes execution. If auto cleanup is FALSE, LabVIEW does not automatically destroy the task until you exit LabVIEW. Use the DAQmx Clear Task VI to manually destroy the task. new task name — new task name is the name to assign to the task. If you use this VI in a loop and specify a name for the task, you must use the DAQmx Clear Task VI within the loop after you are finished with the task. Otherwise, NI-DAQmx attempts to create multiple tasks with the same name, which results in an error. Refer to Task Creation and Destruction for more information. task to copy — task to copy is the name of a task to make a copy of. global virtual channels — global virtual channels specifies a global virtual channel or list of global virtual channels to add to the task. A DAQmx global channel constant lists all global virtual channels on the system. If you have an array of global virtual channels, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the new task. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Advanced Task Options

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/create/task-llb/daqmx-create-watchdog-timer-task-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02208: DAQmx Create Watchdog Timer Task VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/task-llb/daqmx-create-watchdog-timer-task-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/task-llb/daqmx-create-watchdog-timer-task-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates and configures a task that controls the watchdog timer of a device. The timer activates when you start the task. Use the digital physical channel expiration states input to set expiration states for digital channels. If your device supports expiration states for other channel types, use the

### DAQmx Create Watchdog Timer Task VI

Creates and configures a task that controls the watchdog timer of a device. The timer activates when you start the task. Use the digital physical channel expiration states input to set expiration states for digital channels. If your device supports expiration states for other channel types, use the [DAQmx Configure Watchdog Expiration States](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_mxconfigurewatchdogexpstates) to configure those channels' expiration states. This VI does not program the watchdog timer on a real-time controller. Use the Real-Time Watchdog VIs to program the watchdog timer on a real-time controller.

The [DAQmx Watchdog](/csh?context=nidaqmx_nidaq_daqmx_watchdog_p) properties contain additional watchdog timer configuration options.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-watchdog-timer-task-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| auto cleanup — auto cleanup specifies if LabVIEW automatically destroys the task when the application completes execution. If auto cleanup is FALSE, LabVIEW does not automatically destroy the task until you exit LabVIEW. Use the DAQmx Clear Task VI to manually destroy the task. new task name — new task name is the name to assign to the task. If you use this VI in a loop and specify a name for the task, you must use the DAQmx Clear Task VI within the loop after you are finished with the task. Otherwise, NI-DAQmx attempts to create multiple tasks with the same name, which results in an error. Refer to Task Creation and Destruction for more information. device in — device in is the name as configured in MAX of the device to which this operation applies. A DAQmx device name constant lists all devices installed in the system. timeout — timeout is the amount of time in seconds until the watchdog timer expires. A value of -1 means the internal timer never expires. Set this input to -1 if you use an Expiration Trigger to expire the watchdog task. If this time elapses, the device sets the physical channels to the states you specify with the digital physical channel expiration states input. Use the DAQmx Control Watchdog Task VI with the action input set to reset timer to prevent the watchdog timer from expiring. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. digital physical channel expiration states — digital physical channel expiration states contains the states to which to set digital physical channels when the watchdog timer expires. Each element of the array contains a digital physical channel name and the corresponding state for that digital physical channel. physical channel — physical channel is the digital line or port to modify. You cannot modify dedicated digital input lines. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all lines and ports for devices installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of digital lines or ports to this input. If you have an array of lines or ports, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. expiration state — expiration state specifies the state to which to set the digital physical channel when the watchdog timer expires. High (10192) High logic. Low (10214) Low logic. No Change (10160) Expiration does not affect the port. Do not change the state of any lines in the port, and do not lock the port. For example, if a line is high when the timer expires, that line stays high, and you can write new values to the line. You can select this value only for entire ports. Tristate (10310) High-impedance state. You can select this state only on devices with bidirectional ports, and you can select it only for entire ports. You cannot select this state for dedicated digital output lines. task out — task out is a reference to the new task. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| physical channel — physical channel is the digital line or port to modify. You cannot modify dedicated digital input lines. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all lines and ports for devices installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of digital lines or ports to this input. If you have an array of lines or ports, use the DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI to convert the array to a list. expiration state — expiration state specifies the state to which to set the digital physical channel when the watchdog timer expires. High (10192) High logic. Low (10214) Low logic. No Change (10160) Expiration does not affect the port. Do not change the state of any lines in the port, and do not lock the port. For example, if a line is high when the timer expires, that line stays high, and you can write new values to the line. You can select this value only for entire ports. Tristate (10310) High-impedance state. You can select this state only on devices with bidirectional ports, and you can select it only for entire ports. You cannot select this state for dedicated digital output lines. |  |
| High (10192) | High logic. |
| Low (10214) | Low logic. |
| No Change (10160) | Expiration does not affect the port. Do not change the state of any lines in the port, and do not lock the port. For example, if a line is high when the timer expires, that line stays high, and you can write new values to the line. You can select this value only for entire ports. |
| Tristate (10310) | High-impedance state. You can select this state only on devices with bidirectional ports, and you can select it only for entire ports. You cannot select this state for dedicated digital output lines. |

DAQmx Watchdog

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/create/timing-llb/daqmx-create-timing-source-control-loop-from-task-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02209: DAQmx Create Timing Source (Control Loop From Task) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/timing-llb/daqmx-create-timing-source-control-loop-from-task-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/timing-llb/daqmx-create-timing-source-control-loop-from-task-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates a timing source that uses a combination of the sample clock and the specified sleep time to determine when to send ticks to a Timed Loop. You must create an analog input task that uses sample clock timing and wire that task to the task/channels in input of this VI. Do not use the DAQmx Start

### DAQmx Create Timing Source (Control Loop From Task) VI

Creates a timing source that uses a combination of the sample clock and the specified sleep time to determine when to send ticks to a Timed Loop. You must create an analog input task that uses sample clock timing and wire that task to the **task/channels in** input of this VI. Do not use the [DAQmx Start Task VI](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_mxstart) to start the task. The Timed Loop starts the task automatically. National Instruments recommends that you use this instance instead of the instance for best performance with Timed Loops.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-timing-source-control-loop-from-task-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| timing source name — timing source name is the name to assign to the timing source this VI creates. task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. sleep time (us) — sleep time (us) specifies in microseconds the amount of time the Timed Loop sleeps after each sample clock pulse. The default sleep time is 0. This input is ignored if you are not using a real-time OS. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function completes execution. timing source out — timing source out is the name of the timing source this VI creates. Wire this output to the source input of the Input Node of a Timed Loop. If you specified a name for the timing source in the timing source name input, this output contains the same name. If you did not specify a name for the timing source, NI-DAQmx creates a name and sets this output to that name. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

DAQmx Create Timing Source VI

## TOPIC 02210: DAQmx Create Timing Source (Digital Change Detection) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/timing-llb/daqmx-create-timing-source-digital-change-detection-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/timing-llb/daqmx-create-timing-source-digital-change-detection-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates a timing source that sends ticks to a Timed Loop on rising and/or falling edges of one or more digital lines. You can use this instance of this VI only with devices that support digital change detection. Use the instance of the DAQmx Create Timing Source VI with other devices. That instance

### DAQmx Create Timing Source (Digital Change Detection) VI

Creates a timing source that sends ticks to a Timed Loop on rising and/or falling edges of one or more digital lines. You can use this instance of this VI only with devices that support digital change detection. Use the instance of the DAQmx Create Timing Source VI with other devices. That instance uses a counter and cannot detect edges on multiple lines.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-timing-source-digital-change-detection-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| timing source name — timing source name is the name to assign to the timing source this VI creates. rising edge physical channels — rising edge physical channels specifies the names of the digital lines or ports on which to detect rising edges. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all lines and ports for devices installed in your system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of digital lines or ports to this input. falling edge physical channels — falling edge physical channels specifies the names of the digital lines or ports on which to detect falling edges. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all lines and ports for devices installed in your system. You also can wire a string that contains a list or range of digital lines or ports to this input. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. timing source out — timing source out is the name of the timing source this VI creates. Wire this output to the source input of the Input Node of a Timed Loop. If you specified a name for the timing source in the timing source name input, this output contains the same name. If you did not specify a name for the timing source, NI-DAQmx creates a name and sets this output to that name. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

DAQmx Create Timing Source VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/create/timing-llb/daqmx-create-timing-source-digital-edge-using-counter-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02211: DAQmx Create Timing Source (Digital Edge using Counter) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/timing-llb/daqmx-create-timing-source-digital-edge-using-counter-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/timing-llb/daqmx-create-timing-source-digital-edge-using-counter-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates a timing source that sends ticks to a Timed Loop on rising or falling edges of a digital signal. NI-DAQmx uses the counter you specify to detect the edges of the digital signal. Use this VI to base a timing source on an external digital signal or on an internal digital signal not included in

### DAQmx Create Timing Source (Digital Edge using Counter) VI

Creates a timing source that sends ticks to a Timed Loop on rising or falling edges of a digital signal. NI-DAQmx uses the **counter** you specify to detect the edges of the digital signal. Use this VI to base a timing source on an external digital signal or on an internal digital signal not included in the instance of the DAQmx Create Timing Source VI.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-timing-source-digital-edge-using-counter-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| timing source name — timing source name is the name to assign to the timing source this VI creates. counter — counter specifies the name of the counter to use for the timing source. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels, including counters, for devices installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a name of a counter to this input. source — source specifies the terminal to which you connect the digital signal you want to use for the timing source. A DAQmx terminal constant lists all terminals available on devices installed in the system. You also can specify a source terminal by wiring a string that contains a terminal name. edge — edge specifies on which edges of the digital signal you want to send ticks to the Timed Loop. Falling (10171) Send ticks on falling edges. Rising (10280) Send ticks on rising edges. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. edge count — edge count specifies the number of edges of the digital signal that must occur for the timing source to send a tick to the Timed Loop. For example, if you set this value to 3, the timing source sends a tick to the Timed Loop on every third edge of the digital signal. timing source out — timing source out is the name of the timing source this VI creates. Wire this output to the source input of the Input Node of a Timed Loop. If you specified a name for the timing source in the timing source name input, this output contains the same name. If you did not specify a name for the timing source, NI-DAQmx creates a name and sets this output to that name. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Falling (10171) | Send ticks on falling edges. |
| Rising (10280) | Send ticks on rising edges. |

NI-DAQmx uses the counter you specify to generate the signal for the timing source. icon Inputs/Outputs cstr.png timing source name timing source name is the name to assign to the timing source this VI creates. cdaqmxs

### DAQmx Create Timing Source (Frequency) VI

Creates a timing source that sends ticks to a Timed Loop at a constant frequency. NI-DAQmx uses the **counter** you specify to generate the signal for the timing source.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-timing-source-frequency-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| timing source name — timing source name is the name to assign to the timing source this VI creates. counter — counter specifies the name of the counter to use for the timing source. The DAQmx physical channel constant lists all physical channels, including counters, for devices installed in the system. You also can wire a string that contains a name of a counter to this input. frequency — frequency specifies in hertz the frequency at which you want to send ticks to the Timed Loop. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. timing source out — timing source out is the name of the timing source this VI creates. Wire this output to the source input of the Input Node of a Timed Loop. If you specified a name for the timing source in the timing source name input, this output contains the same name. If you did not specify a name for the timing source, NI-DAQmx creates a name and sets this output to that name. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |

## TOPIC 02213: DAQmx Create Timing Source (Signal From Task) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/timing-llb/daqmx-create-timing-source-signal-from-task-vi.html`

- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates a timing source that uses the signal you specify to determine when to send ticks to a Timed Loop. You must create a task that can generate the signal and wire that task to the task/channels in input of this VI. Do not use the DAQmx Start Task VI to start the task. The Timed Loop starts the t

### DAQmx Create Timing Source (Signal From Task) VI

Creates a timing source that uses the **signal** you specify to determine when to send ticks to a Timed Loop. You must create a task that can generate the signal and wire that task to the **task/channels in** input of this VI. Do not use the [DAQmx Start Task](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_mxstart) VI to start the task. The Timed Loop starts the task automatically.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-timing-source-signal-from-task-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| timing source name — timing source name is the name to assign to the timing source this VI creates. task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. signal — signal is the signal to use for the timing source. Change Detection Event (12511) Send a tick to the Timed Loop each time the Change Detection Event occurs. Counter Output Event (12494) Send a tick to the Timed Loop each time the Counter Output Event occurs. Sample Clock (12487) Send a tick to the Timed Loop on each active edge of the Sample Clock. Sample Complete Event (12530) Send a tick to the Timed Loop each time the Sample Complete Event occurs. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. timing source out — timing source out is the name of the timing source this VI creates. Wire this output to the source input of the Input Node of a Timed Loop. If you specified a name for the timing source in the timing source name input, this output contains the same name. If you did not specify a name for the timing source, NI-DAQmx creates a name and sets this output to that name. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |  |
| --- | --- |
| Change Detection Event (12511) | Send a tick to the Timed Loop each time the Change Detection Event occurs. |
| Counter Output Event (12494) | Send a tick to the Timed Loop each time the Counter Output Event occurs. |
| Sample Clock (12487) | Send a tick to the Timed Loop on each active edge of the Sample Clock. |
| Sample Complete Event (12530) | Send a tick to the Timed Loop each time the Sample Complete Event occurs. |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Create Timing Source VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/create/timing-llb/daqmx-create-timing-source-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02214: DAQmx Create Timing Source VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/create/timing-llb/daqmx-create-timing-source-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/create/timing-llb/daqmx-create-timing-source-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Creates a timing source for a Timed Loop. The timing source is based on signals a device generates. The instances of this polymorphic VI correspond to the type of signal to use for the timing source. Wire the timing source out output to the source input of the Input Node of a Timed Loop to use this

### DAQmx Create Timing Source VI

Creates a timing source for a Timed Loop. The timing source is based on signals a device generates. The instances of this polymorphic VI correspond to the type of signal to use for the timing source. Wire the **timing source out** output to the **source** input of the Input Node of a Timed Loop to use this timing source with that Timed Loop.

Timing sources send ticks to a timed loop. Those ticks determine when the Timed Loop executes. By default, the Timed Loop executes each time it receives a tick from a timing source, but other Timed Loop settings can affect when it executes. For example, if you set **period** in the Loop Configuration dialog to 2, the Timed Loop executes on every other tick the timing source sends.

Refer to [NI-DAQmx 7.4 and Later Single-Point Real-Time Applications](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_readwritelate) for an overview of real-time architectures and applications.

The [DAQmx Timing Source](/csh?context=nidaqmx_nidaq_daqmx_timing_source_p) properties include additional timing source configuration options.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-timing-source-vi.png']

- [DAQmx Create Timing Source (Control Loop From Task) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/timing-llb/daqmx-create-timing-source-control-loop-from-task-vi.html) Creates a timing source that uses a combination of the sample clock and the specified sleep time to determine when to send ticks to a Timed Loop. You must create an analog input task that uses sample clock timing and wire that task to the task/channels in input of this VI. Do not use the DAQmx Start Task VI to start the task. The Timed Loop starts the task automatically. National Instruments recommends that you use this instance instead of the instance for best performance with Timed Loops.
- [DAQmx Create Timing Source (Frequency) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/timing-llb/daqmx-create-timing-source-frequency-vi.html) Creates a timing source that sends ticks to a Timed Loop at a constant frequency. NI-DAQmx uses the counter you specify to generate the signal for the timing source.
- [DAQmx Create Timing Source (Digital Edge using Counter) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/timing-llb/daqmx-create-timing-source-digital-edge-using-counter-vi.html) Creates a timing source that sends ticks to a Timed Loop on rising or falling edges of a digital signal. NI-DAQmx uses the counter you specify to detect the edges of the digital signal. Use this VI to base a timing source on an external digital signal or on an internal digital signal not included in the instance of the DAQmx Create Timing Source VI.
- [DAQmx Create Timing Source (Digital Change Detection) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/timing-llb/daqmx-create-timing-source-digital-change-detection-vi.html) Creates a timing source that sends ticks to a Timed Loop on rising and/or falling edges of one or more digital lines. You can use this instance of this VI only with devices that support digital change detection. Use the instance of the DAQmx Create Timing Source VI with other devices. That instance uses a counter and cannot detect edges on multiple lines.
- [DAQmx Create Timing Source (Signal From Task) VI](../../../../vi-lib/daqmx/create/timing-llb/daqmx-create-timing-source-signal-from-task-vi.html) Creates a timing source that uses the signal you specify to determine when to send ticks to a Timed Loop. You must create a task that can generate the signal and wire that task to the task/channels in input of this VI. Do not use the DAQmx Start Task VI to start the task. The Timed Loop starts the task automatically.

Parent topic:

Timed Loop

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/miscellaneous-llb/daqmx-compute-reverse-polynomial-coefficients-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02215: DAQmx Compute Reverse Polynomial Coefficients VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/miscellaneous-llb/daqmx-compute-reverse-polynomial-coefficients-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/miscellaneous-llb/daqmx-compute-reverse-polynomial-coefficients-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Computes a set of coefficients for a polynomial that approximates the inverse of the polynomial with the coefficients you specify with the coefficients input. This VI generates a table of x versus y values over the range of x. This VI then finds a polynomial fit, using the least squares method to co

### DAQmx Compute Reverse Polynomial Coefficients VI

Computes a set of coefficients for a polynomial that approximates the inverse of the polynomial with the coefficients you specify with the **coefficients** input. This VI generates a table of *x* versus *y* values over the range of *x*. This VI then finds a polynomial fit, using the least squares method to compute a polynomial that computes *x* when given a value for *y*.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-compute-reverse-polynomial-coefficients-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| reverse polynomial order — reverse polynomial order is the order of the reverse polynomial to compute. For example, an input of 3 indicates a 3rd order polynomial. A value of -1 indicates a reverse polynomial of the same order as the forward polynomial. coefficients — coefficients is the array of coefficients for the polynomial that computes y given a value of x. Each element of the array corresponds to a term of the equation. For example, if index three of the array is 9, the fourth term of the equation is 9x^3. maximum valid value of x — maximum valid value of x is the maximum value of x for which you use the polynomial. This is the largest value of x for which the VI generates a y value in the table. minimum valid value of x — minimum valid value of x is the minimum value of x for which you use the polynomial. This is the smallest value of x for which the VI generates a y value in the table. number of points to compute within x range — number of points to compute within x range is the number of points in the table of x versus y values. The VI spaces the values evenly between minimum valid value of x and maximum valid value of x. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. reverse coefficients — reverse coefficients is the array of coefficients for the reverse polynomial. Each element of the array corresponds to a term of the equation. For example, if index three of the array is 9, the fourth term of the equation is 9y^3. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Scale Setup

## TOPIC 02216: DAQmx Create Event VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/miscellaneous-llb/daqmx-create-event-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/miscellaneous-llb/daqmx-create-event-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Contains a Register for Events function, an Event structure, and a DAQmx Task Name constant. You must register the DAQmx event you want to use and configure the Event structure to handle that event. You must also use the DAQmx Task Name constant to select or create a task. You can use a DAQmx Task N

### DAQmx Create Event VI

Contains a [Register for Events](/csh?context=lvcore_glang_register_events) function, an [Event structure](/csh?context=lvcore_glang_event_structure), and a [DAQmx Task Name constant](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_mxtaskconst).
 You must [register](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvhowto_dynamic_register_event) the DAQmx event you want to use and [configure](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvhowto_cfgevents) the Event structure to handle that event. You must also use the DAQmx Task Name constant to select or [create](/csh?context=nidaqmx_daqmxio_creatingtasks) a task. You can use a DAQmx Task Name control in place of the constant.

Note

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-create-event-vi.png']

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/miscellaneous-llb/daqmx-flatten-channel-string-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02217: DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/miscellaneous-llb/daqmx-flatten-channel-string-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/miscellaneous-llb/daqmx-flatten-channel-string-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Converts an array of physical or virtual channel names to a comma-delimited list of names. You can use this VI to convert an array of channel names to a single string prior to using the DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI or the DAQmx Create Task VI. icon Inputs/Outputs c1dstr.png names in names in is t

### DAQmx Flatten Channel String VI

Converts an array of physical or virtual channel names to a comma-delimited list of names. You can use this VI to convert an array of channel names to a single string prior to using the [DAQmx Create Virtual Channel](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_mxcreatechannel) VI or the [DAQmx Create Task](/csh?context=nidaqmx_lvdaqmx_mxcreatetask) VI.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-flatten-channel-string-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| names in — names in is the array of physical or virtual channel names. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. names out — names out is the resulting comma-delimited list of physical or virtual channel names. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Utilities

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/miscellaneous-llb/daqmx-unflatten-channel-string-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02218: DAQmx Unflatten Channel String VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/miscellaneous-llb/daqmx-unflatten-channel-string-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/miscellaneous-llb/daqmx-unflatten-channel-string-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Converts a comma-delimited list or range of physical or virtual channels into an array of physical or virtual channel names. icon Inputs/Outputs cstr.png names in names in is the list or range of physical or virtual channels. cerrcodeclst.png error in error in describes error conditions that occur b

### DAQmx Unflatten Channel String VI

Converts a comma-delimited list or range of physical or virtual channels into an array of physical or virtual channel names.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-unflatten-channel-string-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| names in — names in is the list or range of physical or virtual channels. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. names out — names out is the array of physical or virtual channel names. Each element of the array contains a single channel. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Utilities

## TOPIC 02219: DAQmx Read (Analog 1D DBL 1Chan NSamp) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/read-llb/daqmx-read-analog-1d-dbl-1chan-nsamp-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/read-llb/daqmx-read-analog-1d-dbl-1chan-nsamp-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Reads one or more floating-point samples from a task that contains a single analog input channel. icon Inputs/Outputs cgenclassrntag.png task/channels in task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels,

### DAQmx Read (Analog 1D DBL 1Chan NSamp) VI

Reads one or more floating-point samples from a task that contains a single [analog input channel](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_virtchantypes).

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-read-analog-1d-dbl-1chan-nsamp-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. number of samples per channel — number of samples per channel specifies the number of samples to read. If you leave this input unwired or set it to -1, NI-DAQmx determines how many samples to read based on if the task acquires samples continuously or acquires a finite number of samples. If the task acquires samples continuously and you set this input to -1, this VI reads all the samples currently available in the buffer. If the task acquires a finite number of samples and you set this input to -1, the VI waits for the task to acquire all requested samples, then reads those samples. If you set the Read All Available Samples property to TRUE, the VI reads the samples currently available in the buffer and does not wait for the task to acquire all requested samples. timeout — timeout specifies the amount of time in seconds to wait for samples to become available. If the time elapses, the VI returns an error and any samples read before the timeout elapsed. The default timeout is 10 seconds. If you set timeout to -1, the VI waits indefinitely. If you set timeout to 0, the VI tries once to read the requested samples and returns an error if it is unable to. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. data — data returns a 1D array of samples. Each element in the array corresponds to a sample from the channel. NI-DAQmx scales the data to the units of the measurement, including any custom scaling you apply to the channels. Use the DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI or the DAQ Assistant to specify these units. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

Parent topic:

DAQmx Read VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/read-llb/daqmx-read-analog-1d-dbl-nchan-1samp-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02220: DAQmx Read (Analog 1D DBL NChan 1Samp) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/read-llb/daqmx-read-analog-1d-dbl-nchan-1samp-vi.html`

- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Reads a single floating-point sample from each channel in a task that contains one or more analog input channels. icon Inputs/Outputs cgenclassrntag.png task/channels in task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of vi

### DAQmx Read (Analog 1D DBL NChan 1Samp) VI

Reads a single floating-point sample from each channel in a task that contains one or more [analog input channels](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_virtchantypes).

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-read-analog-1d-dbl-nchan-1samp-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. timeout — timeout specifies the amount of time in seconds to wait for samples to become available. If the time elapses, the VI returns an error and any samples read before the timeout elapsed. The default timeout is 10 seconds. If you set timeout to -1, the VI waits indefinitely. If you set timeout to 0, the VI tries once to read the requested samples and returns an error if it is unable to. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. data — data returns a 1D array of samples. Each element of the array corresponds to a channel in the task. The order of the channels in the array corresponds to the order in which you add the channels to the task or to the order of the channels you specify with the Channels to Read property. NI-DAQmx scales the data to the units of the measurement, including any custom scaling you apply to the channels. Use the DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI or the DAQ Assistant to specify these units. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

Each element of the array corresponds to a channel in the task. NI-DAQmx scales the data to the units of the measurement, including any custom scaling you apply to the channels. Use the DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI or the DAQ Assistant to specify these units. error out — error out contains error information. ## TOPIC 02223: DAQmx Read (Analog 1D Wfm NChan NSamp) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/read-llb/daqmx-read-analog-1d-wfm-nchan-nsamp-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/read-llb/daqmx-read-analog-1d-wfm-nchan-nsamp-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Reads one or more waveforms from a task that contains one or more analog input channels. icon Inputs/Outputs cgenclassrntag.png task/channels in task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx

### DAQmx Read (Analog 1D Wfm NChan NSamp) VI

Reads one or more waveforms from a task that contains one or more [analog input channels](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_virtchantypes).

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-read-analog-1d-wfm-nchan-nsamp-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. number of samples per channel — number of samples per channel specifies the number of samples to read. If you leave this input unwired or set it to -1, NI-DAQmx determines how many samples to read based on if the task acquires samples continuously or acquires a finite number of samples. If the task acquires samples continuously and you set this input to -1, this VI reads all the samples currently available in the buffer. If the task acquires a finite number of samples and you set this input to -1, the VI waits for the task to acquire all requested samples, then reads those samples. If you set the Read All Available Samples property to TRUE, the VI reads the samples currently available in the buffer and does not wait for the task to acquire all requested samples. timeout — timeout specifies the amount of time in seconds to wait for samples to become available. If the time elapses, the VI returns an error and any samples read before the timeout elapsed. The default timeout is 10 seconds. If you set timeout to -1, the VI waits indefinitely. If you set timeout to 0, the VI tries once to read the requested samples and returns an error if it is unable to. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. data — data returns a 1D array of waveforms. Each element of the array corresponds to a channel in the task. The order of the channels in the array corresponds to the order in which you add the channels to the task or to the order of the channels you specify with the Channels to Read property. NI-DAQmx scales the data to the units of the measurement, including any custom scaling you apply to the channels. Use the DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI or the DAQ Assistant to specify these units. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

DAQmx Read VI

- source_description: Reads one or more floating-point samples from a task that contains one or more analog input channels. icon Inputs/Outputs cgenclassrntag.png task/channels in task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channe

### DAQmx Read (Analog 2D DBL NChan NSamp) VI

Reads one or more floating-point samples from a task that contains one or more [analog input channels](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_virtchantypes).

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-read-analog-2d-dbl-nchan-nsamp-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. number of samples per channel — number of samples per channel specifies the number of samples to read. If you leave this input unwired or set it to -1, NI-DAQmx determines how many samples to read based on if the task acquires samples continuously or acquires a finite number of samples. If the task acquires samples continuously and you set this input to -1, this VI reads all the samples currently available in the buffer. If the task acquires a finite number of samples and you set this input to -1, the VI waits for the task to acquire all requested samples, then reads those samples. If you set the Read All Available Samples property to TRUE, the VI reads the samples currently available in the buffer and does not wait for the task to acquire all requested samples. timeout — timeout specifies the amount of time in seconds to wait for samples to become available. If the time elapses, the VI returns an error and any samples read before the timeout elapsed. The default timeout is 10 seconds. If you set timeout to -1, the VI waits indefinitely. If you set timeout to 0, the VI tries once to read the requested samples and returns an error if it is unable to. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. data — data returns a 2D array of samples. Each row corresponds to a channel in the task. Each column corresponds to a sample from each channel. The order of the channels in the array corresponds to the order in which you add the channels to the task or to the order of the channels you specify with the Channels to Read property. NI-DAQmx scales the data to the units of the measurement, including any custom scaling you apply to the channels. Use the DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI or the DAQ Assistant to specify these units. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |

If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. timeout — timeout specifies the amount of time in seconds to wait for samples to become available. If the time elapses, the VI returns an error and any samples read before the timeout elapsed. The default timeout is 10 seconds. If you set timeout to -1, the VI waits indefinitely. If you set timeout to 0, the VI tries once to read the requested samples and returns an error if it is unable to. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. data — data returns a waveform. NI-DAQmx scales the data to the units of the measurement, including any custom scaling you apply to the channels. Use the DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI or the DAQ Assistant to specify these units. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

### DAQmx Read (Analog Wfm 1Chan NSamp Duration) VI

Reads a waveform from a task that contains a single [analog input channel for a specified amount of time](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_virtchantypes).

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-read-analog-wfm-1chan-nsamp-duration-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. duration — duration specifies the amount of time in seconds to read samples. timeout — timeout specifies the amount of time in seconds to wait for samples to become available. If the time elapses, the VI returns an error and any samples read before the timeout elapsed. The default timeout is 10 seconds. If you set timeout to -1, the VI waits indefinitely. If you set timeout to 0, the VI tries once to read the requested samples and returns an error if it is unable to. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. data — data returns a waveform. NI-DAQmx scales the data to the units of the measurement, including any custom scaling you apply to the channels. Use the DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI or the DAQ Assistant to specify these units. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

## TOPIC 02232: DAQmx Read (Analog Wfm 1Chan NSamp) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/read-llb/daqmx-read-analog-wfm-1chan-nsamp-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/read-llb/daqmx-read-analog-wfm-1chan-nsamp-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Reads a waveform from a task that contains a single analog input channel. icon Inputs/Outputs cgenclassrntag.png task/channels in task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task

### DAQmx Read (Analog Wfm 1Chan NSamp) VI

Reads a waveform from a task that contains a single [analog input channel](/csh?context=nidaqmx_mxcncpts_virtchantypes).

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-read-analog-wfm-1chan-nsamp-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. number of samples per channel — number of samples per channel specifies the number of samples to read. If you leave this input unwired or set it to -1, NI-DAQmx determines how many samples to read based on if the task acquires samples continuously or acquires a finite number of samples. If the task acquires samples continuously and you set this input to -1, this VI reads all the samples currently available in the buffer. If the task acquires a finite number of samples and you set this input to -1, the VI waits for the task to acquire all requested samples, then reads those samples. If you set the Read All Available Samples property to TRUE, the VI reads the samples currently available in the buffer and does not wait for the task to acquire all requested samples. timeout — timeout specifies the amount of time in seconds to wait for samples to become available. If the time elapses, the VI returns an error and any samples read before the timeout elapsed. The default timeout is 10 seconds. If you set timeout to -1, the VI waits indefinitely. If you set timeout to 0, the VI tries once to read the requested samples and returns an error if it is unable to. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. data — data returns a waveform. NI-DAQmx scales the data to the units of the measurement, including any custom scaling you apply to the channels. Use the DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI or the DAQ Assistant to specify these units. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/read-llb/daqmx-read-counter-1d-dbl-1chan-nsamp-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02233: DAQmx Read (Counter 1D DBL 1Chan NSamp) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/read-llb/daqmx-read-counter-1d-dbl-1chan-nsamp-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/read-llb/daqmx-read-counter-1d-dbl-1chan-nsamp-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Reads one or more floating-point samples from a counter task. Use an instance that reads a floating-point value when NI-DAQmx scales counter samples to a floating-point value, such as for frequency and period measurement. icon Inputs/Outputs cgenclassrntag.png task/channels in task/channels in is th

### DAQmx Read (Counter 1D DBL 1Chan NSamp) VI

Reads one or more floating-point samples from a counter task. Use an instance that reads a floating-point value when NI-DAQmx scales counter samples to a floating-point value, such as for frequency and period measurement.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-read-counter-1d-dbl-1chan-nsamp-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. number of samples per channel — number of samples per channel specifies the number of samples to read. If you leave this input unwired or set it to -1, NI-DAQmx determines how many samples to read based on if the task acquires samples continuously or acquires a finite number of samples. If the task acquires samples continuously and you set this input to -1, this VI reads all the samples currently available in the buffer. If the task acquires a finite number of samples and you set this input to -1, the VI waits for the task to acquire all requested samples, then reads those samples. If you set the Read All Available Samples property to TRUE, the VI reads the samples currently available in the buffer and does not wait for the task to acquire all requested samples. timeout — timeout specifies the amount of time in seconds to wait for samples to become available. If the time elapses, the VI returns an error and any samples read before the timeout elapsed. The default timeout is 10 seconds. If you set timeout to -1, the VI waits indefinitely. If you set timeout to 0, the VI tries once to read the requested samples and returns an error if it is unable to. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. data — data returns a 1D array of samples. Each element in the array corresponds to a sample from the channel. NI-DAQmx scales the data to the units of the measurement, including any custom scaling you apply to the channels. Use the DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI or the DAQ Assistant to specify these units. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

DAQmx Read VI

<!--NI_TOPIC bundle=ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref path=vi-lib/daqmx/read-llb/daqmx-read-counter-1d-dbl-nchan-1samp-vi.html language=enus -->
## TOPIC 02234: DAQmx Read (Counter 1D DBL NChan 1Samp) VI

- bundle_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- source_path: `vi-lib/daqmx/read-llb/daqmx-read-counter-1d-dbl-nchan-1samp-vi.html`
- source_url: https://docs-be.ni.com/bundle/ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref/raw/resource/enus/vi-lib/daqmx/read-llb/daqmx-read-counter-1d-dbl-nchan-1samp-vi.html
- document_id: `ni-daqmx-labview-api-ref`
- page_type: `leaf`
- content_type: `reference`
- source_description: Reads one floating-point sample from one or more counter tasks. Use an instance that reads a floating-point value when NI-DAQmx scales counter samples to a floating-point value. icon Inputs/Outputs cgenclassrntag.png task/channels in task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual chan

### DAQmx Read (Counter 1D DBL NChan 1Samp) VI

Reads one floating-point sample from one or more counter tasks. Use an instance that reads a floating-point value when NI-DAQmx scales counter samples to a floating-point value.

[IMAGE alt='icon' src='daqmx-read-counter-1d-dbl-nchan-1samp-vi.png']

#### Inputs/Outputs

| task/channels in — task/channels in is the name of the task or a list of virtual channels to which the operation applies. If you provide a list of virtual channels, NI-DAQmx creates a task automatically. timeout — timeout specifies the amount of time in seconds to wait for samples to become available. If the time elapses, the VI returns an error and any samples read before the timeout elapsed. The default timeout is 10 seconds. If you set timeout to -1, the VI waits indefinitely. If you set timeout to 0, the VI tries once to read the requested samples and returns an error if it is unable to. error in — error in describes error conditions that occur before this VI or function runs. The default is no error. If an error occurred before this VI or function runs, the VI or function passes the error in value to error out. If an error occurs while this VI or function runs, the VI or function runs normally and sets its own error status in error out. Use the Simple Error Handler or General Error Handler VIs to display the description of the error code. Use error in and error out to check errors and to specify execution order by wiring error out from one node to error in of the next node. task out — task out is a reference to the task after this VI or function runs. If you wired a channel or list of channels to task/channels in, NI-DAQmx creates this task automatically. data — data returns a 1D array of samples. Each element of the array corresponds to a channel in the task. The order of the channels in the array corresponds to the order in which you add the channels to the task or to the order of the channels you specify with the Channels to Read property. NI-DAQmx scales the data to the units of the measurement, including any custom scaling you apply to the channels. Use the DAQmx Create Virtual Channel VI or the DAQ Assistant to specify these units. error out — error out contains error information. If error in indicates that an error occurred before this VI or function ran, error out contains the same error information. Otherwise, error out describes the error status that this VI or function produces. Right-click the error out indicator on the front panel and select Explain Error from the shortcut menu for more information about the error. |
| --- |

DAQmx Read VI
